theme_raw: Delta-X: AVIRIS-NG L3 Derived Aboveground Biomass, MRD, Louisiana, USA, 2021, V2
coverage: Atchafalaya and Terrebonne Basins, coastal Louisiana, USA
license: NASA EOSDIS open data; cite dataset
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF biomass maps
access: ORNL DAAC guide and data access page
notes: Complements the already cataloged Delta-X vegetation type and belowground biomass products by adding the upstream AVIRIS-NG-derived aboveground biomass L3 product.
desc_zh: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Aboveground Biomass Maps V2 是 Mississippi River Delta 的 AVIRIS-NG 派生草本地上生物量产品。ORNL DAAC guide 说明该数据覆盖 Louisiana 沿海 Atchafalaya 和 Terrebonne basins，空间分辨率约 4.7-4.8 m，时间覆盖 2021 年 4 月和 8 月，包含 4 个 COG GeoTIFF 文件；AGB 通过将实测样方与同期 AVIRIS-NG 像元反射率配对并训练随机森林回归模型估算。它适合湿地蓝碳、植被生产力、沉积/淤积过程和 Delta-X 生态地貌建模。
desc_en: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Aboveground Biomass Maps V2 provide AVIRIS-NG-derived herbaceous aboveground biomass over the Mississippi River Delta. The ORNL DAAC guide states that the dataset covers the Atchafalaya and Terrebonne basins in coastal Louisiana at about 4.7-4.8 m resolution for April and August 2021, with four COG GeoTIFF files; AGB was estimated by pairing field biomass samples with coincident AVIRIS-NG reflectance spectra and training a Random Forest regression model. It supports wetland blue-carbon studies, vegetation productivity mapping, sediment/accretion modeling and Delta-X ecogeomorphic analyses.
openness: open ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: AVIRIS-NG derived aboveground biomass product
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide states the dataset contains four COG files, 4.7-4.8 m resolution, April/August 2021 coverage and AGB derived from coincident AVIRIS-NG reflectance spectra and field samples.
scale: four COG files for Atchafalaya and Terrebonne basins
feature_count: one AGB band derived from AVIRIS-NG spectra

name: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Water Quality TSS and Turbidity V3
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / Delta-X
theme_canonical: AVIRIS-NG derived water quality, suspended solids and turbidity products
theme_raw: Delta-X: AVIRIS-NG L3-derived Water Quality, TSS, and Turbidity, MRD, V3
coverage: Atchafalaya River and Terrebonne Basins, Mississippi River Delta, coastal Louisiana, USA
license: NASA EOSDIS open data without restriction; cite dataset
formats: netCDF gridded estimates, CSV regression coefficients, masks
access: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC
notes: Latest V3 product; older V2 page points to this DOI as the latest version. Derived from AVIRIS-NG L2 BRDF-corrected imagery and in-situ water measurements.
desc_zh: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Water Quality TSS and Turbidity V3 是 Mississippi River Delta 水体的高光谱派生水质产品。Earthdata/ORNL 记录说明其由 AVIRIS-NG L2 BRDF-corrected imagery 推导，覆盖 Atchafalaya River 与 Terrebonne Basins，时间为 2021-03-27 至 2021-09-24，提供 TSS 浓度和浊度估计、cloud/water masks、netCDF gridded estimates 以及 CSV 回归系数，空间分辨率约 3.8-5.4 m。它适合悬浮泥沙、浊度、河口三角洲水动力和湿地韧性研究。
desc_en: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Water Quality TSS and Turbidity V3 is a hyperspectral-derived water-quality product for the Mississippi River Delta. The Earthdata/ORNL record states that estimates are derived from AVIRIS-NG L2 BRDF-corrected imagery over the Atchafalaya River and Terrebonne basins from 2021-03-27 to 2021-09-24, providing TSS concentration, turbidity, cloud/water masks, gridded netCDF estimates and CSV regression coefficients at about 3.8-5.4 m resolution. It supports suspended-sediment, turbidity, deltaic hydrodynamics and wetland-resilience studies.
openness: open NASA EOSDIS/ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: AVIRIS-NG derived water quality product
evidence: Earthdata/OpenCLI extracted record for DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2152 states V3, 50 files/granules, L3 processing, AVIRIS-NG L2 BRDF-corrected input, TSS/turbidity retrievals, masks, netCDF estimates and CSV regression coefficients.
scale: 50 granules/files
feature_count: TSS, turbidity, cloud and water mask variables derived from AVIRIS-NG

name: MUESLI Hyperspectral and LiDAR Airborne Data Set
provider: INRAE / INP Toulouse / Zenodo / IEEE GRSS EOD
theme_canonical: airborne hyperspectral and LiDAR multisensor urban-rural benchmark
theme_raw: MUESLI Hyperspectral & LiDar Data Set
coverage: Saint-André / Aurignac area, France
formats: hyperspectral GeoTIFF tiles, LiDAR ZIP, shapefile ground truth and README
access: Zenodo record
time_span: airborne acquisition campaign in June 2016; dataset published 2021-12-20
notes: EOD page states the image was acquired over Saint-André, France during the MUESLI campaign. Zenodo provides the 33.6 GB hyperspectral archive, 4.0 GB LiDAR archive and ground-truth shapefile components.
desc_zh: MUESLI Hyperspectral and LiDAR Airborne Data Set 是法国 MUESLI 机载采集项目发布的高光谱与 LiDAR 多传感器数据集。EOD 页面说明高光谱影像于 2016 年 6 月在法国 Saint-André 区域获取，包含 415 个谱段、范围约 414-2523 nm、2 m 像元，完整影像约 12800 x 7680，并提供 41 类地物真值；Zenodo 页面提供 33.6 GB 高光谱 tiles、4.0 GB LiDAR 数据和 shapefile 真值，授权 CC BY 4.0。它适合分类、去噪、多传感器融合、端元/丰度估计和大幅面机载成像光谱算法评估。
desc_en: The MUESLI Hyperspectral and LiDAR Airborne Data Set is a multisensor airborne benchmark from the French MUESLI acquisition campaign. The EOD page states that the hyperspectral image was acquired over Saint-André, France in June 2016, with 415 spectral bands from about 414 to 2523 nm, 2 m pixels, a full image of about 12800 x 7680 pixels and 41-class ground truth; the Zenodo record provides a 33.6 GB hyperspectral tile archive, a 4.0 GB LiDAR archive and shapefile ground truth under CC BY 4.0. It supports classification, denoising, multisensor fusion, endmember/abundance estimation and large airborne imaging-spectroscopy benchmarking.
openness: open Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: airborne hyperspectral and LiDAR benchmark dataset
evidence: Zenodo record lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5793191, 37.7 GB of files including Hyper_2m_Tiles.zip and LiDAR_MUESLI.zip, CC BY 4.0, while the EOD page states 415 bands, 414-2523 nm, 2 m pixels, 12800x7680 image size and 41-class ground truth.
scale: 37.7 GB total; 33.6 GB hyperspectral tiles plus 4.0 GB LiDAR
feature_count: 415 spectral bands

name: WHISPERS 2022 PRISMA Hyperspectral Pansharpening Challenge Dataset
provider: WHISPERS 2022 / Open Remote Sensing / IEEE GRSS EOD
theme_canonical: PRISMA hyperspectral pansharpening and super-resolution benchmark
theme_raw: PRISMA dataset for WHISPERS 2022 hyperspectral pansharpening challenge
coverage: global / scenes used in WHISPERS 2022 PRISMA pansharpening challenge
license: research benchmark; reuse terms should be checked from download package
formats: Google Drive data package and accompanying mini toolbox
access: Open Remote Sensing data link and EOD catalog page
doi_or_citation: Vivone et al. 2022 IEEE JSTARS, DOI 10.1109/JSTARS.2022.3220974
notes: EOD states four datasets were distributed for the WHISPERS 2022 hyperspectral pansharpening challenge, with full-resolution and reduced-resolution PRISMA setups.
desc_zh: 该 PRISMA 数据集服务于 2022 WHISPERS 高光谱 pansharpening challenge。EOD 页面说明数据包含 4 个 PRISMA 相关子集：两个用于全分辨率问题，panchromatic 约 5 m、高光谱 VNIR-SWIR 约 30 m；另外两个为降分辨率设置，panchromatic 约 30 m、高光谱约 180 m。Open Remote Sensing 页面提供论文 DOI、Google Drive 数据链接和 mini toolbox，适合高光谱-全色融合、超分辨率、pansharpening 和算法公平比较。
desc_en: This PRISMA dataset was used for the 2022 WHISPERS hyperspectral pansharpening challenge. The EOD catalog states that four datasets were distributed: two for full-resolution pansharpening with a 5 m panchromatic band and 30 m VNIR-SWIR hyperspectral imagery, and two reduced-resolution setups with 30 m panchromatic and 180 m hyperspectral imagery. The Open Remote Sensing page provides the paper DOI, Google Drive data link and mini toolbox, making it useful for hyperspectral-panchromatic fusion, super-resolution, pansharpening and fair algorithm comparison.
openness: public challenge data link; terms should be checked in package
source_type: hyperspectral pansharpening challenge dataset
evidence: EOD page states the PRISMA dataset contains four distributed datasets used for the 2022 WHISPERS hyperspectral pansharpening challenge, while Open Remote Sensing provides the data link and DOI 10.1109/JSTARS.2022.3220974.
scale: four PRISMA pansharpening benchmark datasets; EOD lists 1.7 GB
feature_count: PRISMA VNIR-SWIR hyperspectral imagery plus panchromatic band

name: Salinas Hyperspectral Anomaly Detection Dataset by Ma and Hou
provider: Donglei Ma / Zengfu Hou / zephyrhours.github.io
theme_canonical: Salinas hyperspectral anomaly-detection task dataset
theme_raw: Salinas datasets for hyperspectral anomaly detection
coverage: Salinas agricultural scene, California, USA
formats: Google Drive or Baidu Netdisk download package
access: Zengfu Hou datasets page with Google Drive and Baidu Netdisk links
time_span: task dataset used in 2019 anomaly-detection papers
notes: Boundary record: derived/task-oriented Salinas anomaly-detection dataset rather than a new acquisition. Kept separate from the generic EHU scene collection because it provides an anomaly-detection download package and citation instructions.
desc_zh: 该 Salinas anomaly detection 数据集由 Donglei Ma 和 Zengfu Hou 提供，Zengfu Hou 数据页说明其主要用于高光谱异常检测，并给出 Google Drive/Baidu Netdisk 下载入口及两篇 2019 年 Remote Sensing 异常检测论文引用。它不是新的航飞获取数据，而是围绕经典 Salinas AVIRIS 场景整理的异常检测任务版本；因此适合需要异常目标/掩膜或任务约定的算法比较，而不应与原始 Salinas 场景混淆。
desc_en: This Salinas anomaly-detection dataset is provided by Donglei Ma and Zengfu Hou. Hou's dataset page states that it is mainly used for hyperspectral anomaly detection, provides Google Drive and Baidu Netdisk download links, and asks users to cite two 2019 Remote Sensing anomaly-detection papers. It is not a new acquisition, but a task-oriented anomaly-detection version of the classic Salinas AVIRIS scene, so it is useful for algorithm comparisons that require anomaly labels or task conventions and should not be confused with the raw Salinas scene.
openness: public dataset page with cloud-drive links; license not stated
source_type: hyperspectral anomaly-detection task dataset
evidence: Zengfu Hou datasets page lists 'Salinas datasets', states it is provided by Donglei Ma and Zengfu Hou, mainly used for hyperspectral anomaly detection, gives Google Drive/Baidu Netdisk download links, and lists the relevant 2019 Remote Sensing citations.
scale: Salinas anomaly-detection task package
feature_count: based on Salinas AVIRIS hyperspectral scene

name: AVIRIS-NG L1B Calibrated Radiance Facility Instrument Collection V1
provider: NASA JPL / ORNL DAAC
theme_canonical: Airborne facility imaging spectroscopy calibrated radiance
theme_raw: AVIRIS-NG_L1B_radiance
coverage: AVIRIS-NG facility flights across multiple campaigns, 2014-2022 and continuing
license: NASA EOSDIS open data; Earthdata/ORNL DAAC terms apply
formats: ENVI binary radiance image files and headers, geometric lookup tables, observation geometry, locations, quicklooks, KML footprints
access: ORNL DAAC dataset guide and Earthdata access
time_span: 2014-2022 in guide; additional facility data added as available
notes: Complements the existing AVIRIS-NG L2 reflectance record by adding the calibrated radiance facility archive. ORNL DAAC describes six L1B file types per flight line and 380-2510 nm VSWIR measurements at 5 nm intervals.
desc_zh: AVIRIS-NG L1B 是 NASA AVIRIS-NG 机载成像光谱仪的 EOSDIS facility instrument 辐射定标归档。ORNL DAAC 指南说明该集合包含正射校正辐射影像、几何查找表、观测几何、像元位置、quicklook 和足迹文件，覆盖 380-2510 nm、约 5 nm 间隔。它是 AVIRIS-NG L2 反射率、甲烷制图、生态地质和多任务 benchmark 的上游观测源。
desc_en: AVIRIS-NG L1B is the NASA EOSDIS facility archive of calibrated radiance from the AVIRIS-NG airborne imaging spectrometer. ORNL DAAC describes orthocorrected radiance images, geometric lookup tables, observation geometry, pixel locations, quicklooks and footprint files, spanning 380-2510 nm at about 5 nm intervals. It is the upstream observation archive for AVIRIS-NG reflectance, methane mapping, ecology, geology and many downstream benchmarks.
openness: open NASA EOSDIS collection; Earthdata login may be needed
source_type: observed airborne facility radiance archive
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide states the AVIRIS-NG L1B collection contains calibrated radiance files, geometry and illumination products, 380-2510 nm measurements at 5-nm intervals, ENVI-format data, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2095. Phase248 CMR recheck: short_name AVIRIS-NG_L1B_radiance_2095, concept_id C2662359874-ORNL_CLOUD.
scale: altitude dependent; 425-band VSWIR instrument archive
feature_count: 425 contiguous bands in standard AVIRIS-NG products

name: AVIRIS-3 L1B Calibrated Radiance Facility Instrument Collection
provider: NASA JPL / ORNL DAAC
theme_canonical: Airborne facility imaging spectroscopy calibrated radiance
theme_raw: AV3_L1B_RDN
coverage: NASA AVIRIS-3 facility flights, campaign-dependent
license: NASA EOSDIS open data; Earthdata/ORNL DAAC terms apply
formats: netCDF calibrated radiance, observation parameters, band mask, GeoTIFF quicklooks, YAML processing metadata
access: ORNL DAAC dataset guide and Earthdata access
time_span: facility archive, starting 2023 and updated as data become available
notes: Upstream calibrated-radiance counterpart to the existing AVIRIS-3 L2A and L2B product records. ORNL DAAC states AVIRIS-3 measures 390-2500 nm at about 7.4 nm intervals and provides RDN, OBS, BANDMASK and quicklook products.
desc_zh: AVIRIS-3 L1B 是 NASA/JPL AVIRIS-3 机载高光谱仪的辐射定标 facility archive。数据包含未正射的校准辐射、观测参数、几何查找表、波段插值掩膜、GeoTIFF quicklook 和 YAML 处理元数据，光谱范围为 390-2500 nm、约 7.4 nm 间隔。它是 AVIRIS-3 L2A 反射率和 L2B 温室气体产品的上游源数据。
desc_en: AVIRIS-3 L1B is the calibrated-radiance facility archive for NASA/JPL's AVIRIS-3 airborne imaging spectrometer. It includes radiance, observation-parameter, geometric-lookup, band-mask, GeoTIFF quicklook and YAML processing metadata products over 390-2500 nm at about 7.4 nm intervals. It is the upstream source archive for AVIRIS-3 L2A reflectance and L2B greenhouse-gas products.
openness: open NASA EOSDIS collection; Earthdata login may be needed
source_type: observed airborne AVIRIS-3 radiance archive
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide states AVIRIS-3 L1B contains calibrated radiance and observational parameters in netCDF, quicklooks as GeoTIFFs, YAML metadata, 390-2500 nm measurements at 7.4-nm intervals, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2356. Phase248 CMR recheck: short_name AV3_L1B_RDN_2356, concept_id C3236537162-ORNL_CLOUD.
scale: 0.5-20 m altitude dependent; 284-band radiance products
feature_count: 284 calibrated radiance bands

name: AVIRIS-3 L2B Greenhouse Gas Enhancements Facility Instrument Collection
provider: NASA JPL / ORNL DAAC
theme_canonical: Airborne hyperspectral greenhouse gas enhancement product
theme_raw: AV3_L2B_GHG
coverage: NASA AVIRIS-3 facility flights, campaign-dependent
license: NASA EOSDIS open data; Earthdata/ORNL DAAC terms apply
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF methane and carbon dioxide enhancement products, quicklooks, YAML processing metadata
access: ORNL DAAC dataset guide and Earthdata access
time_span: facility archive, starting 2023 and updated as data become available
notes: AVIRIS-3 L2B GHG products derive methane and carbon dioxide enhancements from AVIRIS-3 radiance using matched-filter retrievals. This complements the existing AVIRIS-5 L2B GHG and AVIRIS-3 L2A records.
desc_zh: AVIRIS-3 L2B Greenhouse Gas Enhancements 是 NASA AVIRIS-3 机载高光谱仪的温室气体增强量产品集合。数据包含甲烷和二氧化碳增强量、逐像元不确定性和敏感度，以云优化 GeoTIFF、quicklook 和 YAML 元数据发布。该集合适合甲烷/CO2 点源探测、温室气体遥感算法验证以及 EMIT 类高光谱气体产品对比。
desc_en: AVIRIS-3 L2B Greenhouse Gas Enhancements is the facility product collection for methane and carbon dioxide enhancement retrievals from NASA's AVIRIS-3 airborne imaging spectrometer. It provides enhancement layers, per-pixel uncertainty and sensitivity as cloud-optimized GeoTIFFs with quicklooks and YAML metadata. The collection supports methane/CO2 point-source detection, greenhouse-gas retrieval validation and comparison with EMIT-like hyperspectral gas products.
openness: open NASA EOSDIS collection; Earthdata login may be needed
source_type: airborne greenhouse gas hyperspectral product
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide states AVIRIS-3 L2B GHG includes methane and carbon dioxide enhancements with uncertainty and sensitivity, COG/YAML products, 390-2500 nm AVIRIS-3 source measurements, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2358.
scale: 0.5-20 m altitude dependent; gas enhancement products derived from 390-2500 nm imaging spectroscopy
feature_count: CH4 and CO2 enhancement, sensitivity and uncertainty layers

name: AVIRIS-5 L1B Calibrated Radiance Facility Instrument Collection
provider: NASA JPL / ORNL DAAC

coverage: NASA AVIRIS-5 facility flights, initially GEMx and related campaigns
license: US Government Works / NASA open data; Earthdata/ORNL DAAC terms apply
formats: netCDF calibrated radiance and observation parameters, geolocation lookup tables, TIFF quicklooks, YAML processing metadata
access: Earthdata / ORNL DAAC guide and data access
time_span: 2025-04-29/2026-06-01 in NASA Open Data metadata; facility archive updated as available
notes: Canonical ORNL DAAC guide now verified for AVIRIS-5 L1B; CASEI links this DOI/product to GEMx AVIRIS-5 deployments. This record replaces the earlier NASA Open Data landing URL with the dataset guide while preserving DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2483.
desc_zh: AVIRIS-5 L1B 是 NASA/JPL AVIRIS-5 新一代机载高光谱仪的辐射定标 facility archive。ORNL DAAC 规范 guide 和 CASEI 均指向 DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2483，产品包含校准辐射和观测几何等数据，面向 GEMx 等 AVIRIS-5 飞行任务。它是 AVIRIS-5 L2A 反射率和 L2B 温室气体产品的上游源数据。
desc_en: AVIRIS-5 L1B is the calibrated-radiance facility archive for NASA/JPL's AVIRIS-5 airborne imaging spectrometer. The canonical ORNL DAAC guide and CASEI both point to DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2483, covering calibrated radiance and observation-geometry products for AVIRIS-5 deployments such as GEMx. It is the upstream source archive for AVIRIS-5 L2A reflectance and L2B greenhouse-gas products.
openness: open NASA ORNL DAAC dataset; Earthdata login may be needed for file access
source_type: observed airborne AVIRIS-5 radiance archive
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide cites AVIRIS-5 L1B Calibrated Radiance, Facility Instrument Collection with DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2483; CASEI lists the same DOI under AVIRIS-5/GEMx and describes AVIRIS-5 as 380-2500 nm, 425-channel airborne imaging spectroscopy. Phase248 CMR recheck: short_name AV5_L1B_RDN_2483, concept_id C4076410273-ORNL_CLOUD.
scale: 12 m to sub-meter altitude dependent; 425-channel VSWIR radiance products
feature_count: 425 VSWIR channels

name: Cross-Scene Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Wetland Image Data
provider: Zenodo / Ningbo University / Hubei University
theme_canonical: Cross-scene satellite hyperspectral wetland classification benchmark
theme_raw: ZY1-02D and GF-5 AHSI wetland scenes
coverage: Yancheng and Huanghekou / Yellow River Estuary wetlands, China
formats: RAR archive with hyperspectral wetland image data
access: Zenodo open dataset download
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.8105220; ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing 2023
time_span: Yancheng GF-5/ZY1-02D scenes and Huanghekou ZY1-02D scenes including 2020-06-28 and 2021-09-29
notes: Distinct from the older Yancheng Hyperion wetland change dataset already in the catalog. This Zenodo record uses Chinese AHSI satellite data from ZY1-02D and GF-5 for cross-scene wetland mapping.
desc_zh: Cross-Scene Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Wetland image data 是面向跨场景湿地制图和域适应研究的开放高光谱数据集。Zenodo 页面说明数据覆盖中国盐城和黄河口湿地，盐城包含 ZY1-02D 与 GF-5 AHSI 高光谱影像，黄河口包含两期 ZY1-02D AHSI 影像。数据以 1.3 GB 的 wetland.rar 发布，采用 CC BY 4.0 许可，适合跨传感器、跨场景湿地分类实验。
desc_en: Cross-Scene Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Wetland image data is an open benchmark for cross-scene wetland mapping and domain-adaptation research. The Zenodo record covers Yancheng and Huanghekou / Yellow River Estuary wetlands in China, including ZY1-02D and GF-5 AHSI hyperspectral scenes for Yancheng and two ZY1-02D AHSI dates for Huanghekou. It is distributed as a 1.3 GB wetland.rar archive under CC BY 4.0 and supports cross-sensor and cross-scene wetland classification experiments.
openness: open Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: satellite hyperspectral wetland benchmark
evidence: Zenodo record states Dataset Open, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.8105220, CC BY 4.0, a 1.3 GB wetland.rar file, and describes ZY1-02D and GF-5 AHSI wetland HSI scenes for Yancheng and Huanghekou.
scale: satellite AHSI scenes over two Chinese wetland regions
feature_count: multiple ZY1-02D and GF-5 AHSI scenes

name: PANGAEA Heron Reef CASI Airborne Hyperspectral Image Data
provider: PANGAEA / University of Queensland / CSIRO
theme_canonical: Airborne coral reef hyperspectral imagery
theme_raw: CASI Heron Reef airborne hyperspectral image
coverage: Heron Reef, Great Barrier Reef, Queensland, Australia
formats: TIFF image data and PANGAEA tabular metadata
access: PANGAEA dataset page and raw-data file links
notes: PANGAEA describes this as airborne hyperspectral image data derived from CASI over Heron Reef, processed to at-surface data at 1 m spatial resolution.
desc_zh: PANGAEA Heron Reef CASI 数据集提供澳大利亚大堡礁 Heron Reef 的航空高光谱影像。页面说明数据来自 2002 年 7 月 1 日和 3 日 Compact Airborne Spectrographic Imager (CASI) 飞行，包含 19 个波段，经过辐射、大气和几何校正形成 1 m 分辨率的 at-surface TIFF 影像。它适合珊瑚礁底质、浅海水深和海岸带高光谱算法验证。
desc_en: The PANGAEA Heron Reef CASI dataset provides airborne hyperspectral imagery over Heron Reef in the Great Barrier Reef, Australia. The record describes CASI acquisitions from July 1 and 3, 2002, processed and corrected to at-surface data with 19 bands at 1 m spatial resolution in TIFF format. It supports coral reef benthic mapping, shallow-water bathymetry and coastal hyperspectral algorithm validation.
openness: open PANGAEA dataset under CC BY 3.0
source_type: airborne hyperspectral coral reef image dataset
evidence: PANGAEA record states airborne hyperspectral 19-band CASI image data over Heron Reef, 1 m spatial resolution, TIFF format, CC BY 3.0 license, and DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.788686.
scale: 1 m spatial resolution; 19 CASI bands
feature_count: 19 bands

name: PANGAEA Inland Water Remote Sensing Validation HySpex Gyrocopter Dataset
provider: PANGAEA / Inland Water Remote Sensing Validation Campaign 2017
theme_canonical: Airborne inland-water hyperspectral validation imagery
theme_raw: HySpex VNIR 1600 gyrocopter overflights
coverage: Geiseltalsee and related inland-water validation sites, Germany
formats: band-sequential hyperspectral image files, ENVI headers, DSM, digital orthophoto, PANGAEA metadata
access: PANGAEA dataset page and binary object downloads
notes: Includes HySpex VNIR 1600 hyperspectral imagery, DSM and orthophoto from gyrocopter overflights for inland-water remote-sensing validation.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集来自 2017 年 Inland Water Remote Sensing Validation Campaign 的旋翼机航飞，包含 HySpex VNIR 1600 高光谱影像、数字表面模型和数字正射影像。页面说明高光谱数据包含 80 个波段，覆盖 400-1000 nm，以传感器处辐亮度形式给出，并经 PARGE 地理参考和校正，约 2.5 m GSD。它适合内陆水体遥感、大气/辐射校验和机载-卫星水色算法验证。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset comes from gyrocopter overflights during the 2017 Inland Water Remote Sensing Validation Campaign and includes HySpex VNIR 1600 hyperspectral imagery, a digital surface model and digital orthophoto. The record describes 80 spectral bands from 400-1000 nm, at-sensor radiance, PARGE georeferencing and rectification, and about 2.5 m GSD for the hyperspectral data. It supports inland-water remote sensing, radiometric validation and airborne-to-satellite aquatic algorithm testing.
openness: open PANGAEA dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: airborne hyperspectral inland-water validation dataset
evidence: PANGAEA record states HySpex VNIR 1600 imagery from gyrocopter overflights, 80 bands from 400-1000 nm, at-sensor radiance, PARGE georeferencing, 2.5 m GSD, CC BY 4.0, and DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.955224.

The record states that the map was produced from July 1 and 3, 2002 CASI acquisitions using radiative-transfer model inversion, corresponding to the Detailed Depth ALUT treatment in Hedley et al. 2009. desc_en: The EnMAP EnICCS Cloud and Cloud Shadow Benchmarking Dataset is an open benchmark for cloud and cloud-shadow detection in EnMAP hyperspectral imagery. The Zenodo record states that it contains five pairs of hand-drawn cloud and shadow masks for EnMAP scenes acquired in 2023-2024 around Siaya County, western Kenya, distributed as GeoTIFFs with datatake and tile IDs for retrieving the corresponding EnMAP imagery. It supports EnMAP cloud masking, shadow masking and hyperspectral preprocessing evaluation.

desc_en: The Plastic Litter Project 2021 dataset comes from an ESA Discovery campaign for remote sensing of marine plastic litter and contains Sentinel-2 multispectral data, UAS RGB, UAS hyperspectral data, orthophoto maps and ancillary observations. The Zenodo record states that the campaign used artificial floating targets in the Gulf of Gera, Lesvos Island, Greece, from June to October 2021, with date-organized archives totaling about 159.5 GB. It supports floating plastic detection, UAS-to-satellite scaling and aquatic hyperspectral remote-sensing studies.

The Zenodo record states that `hyperspectral.zip` contains hyperspectral remote-sensing data used to extract tree-level reflectance values, accompanied by area-of-interest data, forest masks, plot locations and corrected tree in-situ defoliation data under CC BY 4.0. desc_en: The S34I Aramo release is an airborne multi-source fusion product for the Aramo pilot area in Spain, derived from hyperspectral, LiDAR, and RGB aerial acquisitions. The Zenodo record provides about 5.6 GB of files, with a core GeoTIFF combining hyperspectral band-ratio layers, LiDAR intensity/DTM/DSM rasters, and RGB orthomosaic bands at roughly 1.2 m resolution. It is useful for mineral exploration, critical raw materials mapping, and multi-sensor fusion benchmarking, while being a derived hyperspectral fusion raster rather than the full original hyperspectral cube.

The camera recorded 512 by 512 pixel images with 204 bands from 400 to 1000 nm, imaging snow and sea ice at roughly 30-minute intervals during parts of April and July 2020. The dataset is useful for spatially resolved cryosphere hyperspectral imaging, near-surface remote-sensing validation, and sea-ice reflectance studies.

desc_en: hyDRaCAT is a hyperspectral reflectance and BRDF reference library for Arctic tundra vegetation communities at representative Siberian Yamal and Alaskan North Slope sites. Measurements were collected with GER1500 field spectroradiometers and a ground-based spectro-goniometer over roughly 350-1050 nm, then released through PANGAEA. It is useful for tundra endmember characterization, BRDF normalization, and vegetation-parameter retrieval supporting imaging-spectroscopy missions such as EnMAP.

source_type: reference spectral library
evidence: PANGAEA states hyDRaCAT provides surface reflectance and BRDF for Siberian and Alaskan tundra, measured with GER1500 field spectroradiometers over 350-1050 nm and distributed as spectral library files under CC BY 3.0.

formats: zipped UHI BSQ files, ENVI-style headers, tab-delimited metadata
access: PANGAEA dataset download table
notes: Close-range underwater hyperspectral imaging from an ROV, not airborne or satellite Earth observation. Included as an open remote-sensing hyperspectral imaging dataset for seabed mapping and mineral exploration.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集发布了 RV SONNE SO242/2 航次中 ROV 搭载水下高光谱成像仪采集的深海海底数据。数据位于东南太平洋 Peru Basin 的锰结核区，约 4200 m 水深，记录 378-805 nm 的 112 个波段，空间分辨率约 1 mm。它适合海底矿物、锰结核识别和水下高光谱遥感方法验证，但属于近距离水下成像而非航空/卫星影像。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset releases deep-seafloor data acquired by an ROV-mounted Underwater Hyperspectral Imager during RV SONNE cruise SO242/2. The data cover a manganese-nodule field in the Peru Basin at about 4200 m water depth, recording 112 bands from 378 to 805 nm with approximately 1 mm spatial resolution. It is valuable for seabed mineral mapping, manganese nodule detection, and underwater hyperspectral remote-sensing method development, while being close-range underwater imagery rather than airborne or satellite data.
openness: public PANGAEA dataset, CC BY 3.0
source_type: underwater hyperspectral image data
evidence: PANGAEA describes first deep-seafloor hyperspectral image data acquired by an ROV-mounted UHI, with 112 bands between 378 and 805 nm, zipped BSQ/header files, and CC BY 3.0 license.
scale: ROV transects
feature_count: 80 data points

name: HyPlant Campus Klein-Altendorf Airborne Imaging Spectrometer Example Dataset
provider: Forschungszentrum Juelich / e!DAL Plant Genomics and Phenomics Repository
theme_canonical: Airborne SIF and vegetation spectroscopy
theme_raw: HyPlant DUAL and FLUO airborne hyperspectral data over Campus Klein-Altendorf, Germany
coverage: Campus Klein-Altendorf agricultural research station, Germany
formats: ENVI BSQ/HDR, at-sensor radiance, top-of-canopy radiance, top-of-canopy reflectance, reflectance indices, SIF products
access: e!DAL / IPK DOI repository with directory download
notes: Full HyPlant processing-chain example dataset. The repository exposes DUAL and FLUO directories with at-sensor radiance, reflectance and fluorescence products; it is not a global archive but is a high-value reproducible airborne hyperspectral reference scene.
desc_zh: 该数据集是 HyPlant 高性能机载成像光谱仪处理链的公开示例数据，覆盖德国 Campus Klein-Altendorf 农业研究站。DUAL 模块提供 400-2500 nm 植被高光谱数据，FLUO 模块提供 670-780 nm 高光谱荧光观测，仓储中包含机载辐亮度、冠层顶部辐亮度、冠层顶部反射率、指数和 SIF 产品等目录。它适合学习 HyPlant 数据结构、SIF 反演、植被生化参数提取和机载高光谱处理链验证。
desc_en: This is the public example dataset for the HyPlant high-performance airborne imaging spectrometer processing chain, acquired over the Campus Klein-Altendorf agricultural research station in Germany. The DUAL module provides 400-2500 nm vegetation imaging spectroscopy, while the FLUO module provides high-spectral-resolution 670-780 nm fluorescence observations; the repository includes at-sensor radiance, top-of-canopy radiance, top-of-canopy reflectance, index, and SIF product directories. It is useful for learning HyPlant data structure, SIF retrieval, vegetation biochemical retrieval, and airborne hyperspectral processing-chain validation.
openness: public DOI repository record
source_type: airborne hyperspectral example scene
evidence: e!DAL describes a HyPlant example dataset from Campus Klein-Altendorf with DUAL hyperspectral data in 400-2500 nm, FLUO 670-780 nm fluorescence data, DOI 10.5447/IPK/2019/21, and 10 directories / 26 files totaling 64.5 GB.
scale: single airborne agricultural research station scene

name: HyData HyPlant FLUO At-Sensor Radiance and FLOX SIF Dataset 2018-2023
provider: Juelich DATA / Forschungszentrum Juelich
theme_canonical: Airborne SIF retrieval benchmark
theme_raw: HyPlant FLUO at-sensor radiance packages, FLOX measurements, and SIF products from selected campaigns
coverage: selected HyPlant campaigns, mainly vegetation/SIF sites, 2018-2023
formats: HyPlant FLUO radiance packages, FLOX measurements, SFMNN/SFM SIF products
access: Juelich DATA / JuSER DOI record
notes: Focused on HyPlant FLUO at-sensor radiance and SIF-method development rather than the full DUAL VSWIR cube archive.
desc_zh: HyData 是 Juelich DATA 发布的 HyPlant FLUO 机载高光谱数据包，面向太阳诱导叶绿素荧光（SIF）反演方法研发。记录包含 2018-2023 年若干活动的 HyPlant FLUO 传感器顶层辐亮度数据、对应 FLOX 地面测量，以及匹配的 SFMNN 和 SFM SIF 产品。它适合机器学习/物理混合 SIF 反演、传感器校准影响分析和 FLEX/DESIS/OCO-3 跨尺度荧光研究。
desc_en: HyData is a Juelich DATA release of HyPlant FLUO airborne hyperspectral packages for sun-induced chlorophyll fluorescence retrieval-method development. It contains HyPlant FLUO at-sensor radiance measurements from selected campaigns between 2018 and 2023, matching FLOX field measurements, and corresponding SFMNN and SFM SIF products. It is useful for machine-learning and physics-guided SIF retrieval, sensor-calibration analysis, and FLEX/DESIS/OCO-3 fluorescence cross-scale research.
openness: public Juelich DATA DOI record
source_type: airborne hyperspectral SIF training and validation dataset
evidence: JuSER/Juelich DATA lists HyData as a 2024 dataset with DOI 10.26165/juelich-data/qkjkpw; the FLEX data page states it consists of HyPlant FLUO at-sensor radiance measurements from 2018-2023 campaigns, FLOX measurements, and matching SFMNN/SFM SIF products.
scale: multi-campaign airborne SIF dataset

name: Selected HyPlant DESIS and OCO-3 SIF Cross-Comparison Acquisitions
provider: Juelich DATA / Forschungszentrum Juelich / DLR / ESA partners
theme_canonical: Airborne-spaceborne SIF cross-comparison
theme_raw: spatially and temporally matched HyPlant, DESIS and OCO-3 acquisitions for SIF product comparison
coverage: selected HyPlant/DESIS and OCO-3/DESIS matching acquisitions
formats: HyPlant, DESIS and OCO-3 acquisition references/products
access: Juelich DATA / JuSER DOI record
notes: Cross-comparison package rather than a single mission archive. Included because it links airborne HyPlant and spaceborne hyperspectral DESIS/OCO-3 SIF products for reproducible validation.
desc_zh: 该 Juelich DATA 数据集整理了空间和时间上接近匹配的 HyPlant/DESIS 与 OCO-3/DESIS 观测，用于太阳诱导叶绿素荧光产品交叉比较。它把机载 HyPlant 高光谱荧光观测与 DESIS、OCO-3 等空间载荷数据联系起来，是 SIF 产品验证和跨尺度一致性分析的可引用数据包。它不是单一影像库，而是面向算法验证的精选匹配观测集合。
desc_en: This Juelich DATA record collects spatially and temporally close HyPlant/DESIS and OCO-3/DESIS acquisitions for cross-comparing sun-induced fluorescence products. It links airborne HyPlant hyperspectral fluorescence observations with DESIS and OCO-3 spaceborne products, making it a citable package for SIF product validation and cross-scale consistency analysis. It is a curated comparison dataset rather than a single-scene imagery archive.
openness: public Juelich DATA DOI record
source_type: airborne-spaceborne hyperspectral SIF comparison dataset
evidence: JuSER lists the dataset with DOI 10.26165/juelich-data/ynylfq, and the FLEX data page describes it as acquisitions and references of spatially and temporally closely matching HyPlant/DESIS and OCO-3/DESIS acquisitions.
scale: selected cross-sensor acquisitions

name: Global Airborne Observatory Sabah Forest Canopy Trait GeoTIFF Dataset
provider: Global Airborne Observatory / Arizona State University / Zenodo
theme_canonical: Airborne imaging spectroscopy derived forest traits
theme_raw: GAO imaging spectroscopy and LiDAR-derived canopy trait maps for Danum and Sepilok, Sabah
coverage: Danum and Sepilok sites, Sabah, Borneo, Malaysia
formats: GeoTIFF rasters
access: Zenodo record with direct downloadable GeoTIFF files
notes: Derived canopy-property rasters, not raw imaging spectroscopy cubes. Included as an open GAO product explicitly derived from airborne imaging spectroscopy and linked to a peer-reviewed Communications Earth & Environment paper.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布了 Global Airborne Observatory 在马来西亚 Sabah（Borneo）Danum 与 Sepilok 样区的森林冠层属性栅格。数据由 2016 年 GAO 任务获取的成像光谱和 LiDAR 数据派生，包含叶化学性状、冠层高度、叶面积密度、地上碳密度等 GeoTIFF 图层。它适合热带森林功能性状、碳储量和机载成像光谱派生产品研究，但不是原始高光谱立方体。
desc_en: This Zenodo release provides Global Airborne Observatory forest-canopy property rasters for the Danum and Sepilok sites in Sabah, Borneo, Malaysia. The products are derived from the 2016 GAO imaging spectroscopy and LiDAR mission and include GeoTIFF layers for leaf chemical traits, canopy height, leaf area density, aboveground carbon density, and related variables. It is valuable for tropical forest functional-trait, carbon, and airborne imaging-spectroscopy product studies, while not being the raw hyperspectral cube archive.
openness: public Zenodo dataset, CC BY 4.0
source_type: derived airborne imaging spectroscopy forest trait rasters
evidence: Zenodo states the dataset is plot-level mapping data derived from the Global Airborne Observatory mission in Sabah in 2016, all layers are GeoTIFF, leaf chemical traits are at 4 m resolution, and the record is a CC BY 4.0 open dataset.
scale: two tropical forest study landscapes
feature_count: 10 GeoTIFF files

name: Caribbean Marine Maps GAO Airborne Imaging Spectroscopy Reef Products
provider: The Nature Conservancy / Arizona State University Global Airborne Observatory
theme_canonical: Airborne imaging spectroscopy coral reef products
theme_raw: GAO hyperspectral imagery-derived coral reef bathymetry, habitat and cover maps for Saint Croix and the Dominican Republic
coverage: Saint Croix, U.S. Virgin Islands and Dominican Republic reef sites
license: public conservation data page; specific spatial-data terms should be checked at download
formats: web map and downloadable spatial data
access: Caribbean Marine Maps page with spatial-data download and reef explorer
doi_or_citation: Schill et al. 2021 Frontiers in Marine Science 8:698004; Asner et al. 2020 Remote Sensing 12:310
time_span: GAO airborne campaigns supporting 2020-2021 reef publications
notes: Derived reef products from airborne imaging spectroscopy rather than raw 427-band GAO data. Access is through a project data page and Google-hosted spatial download.
desc_zh: Caribbean Marine Maps 页面公开了 ASU Global Airborne Observatory 为 The Nature Conservancy 在 Saint Croix 和多米尼加共和国采集的机载成像光谱珊瑚礁产品。页面说明 GAO 以约 1 m 分辨率采集 427 个光谱采样，并从高光谱航空数据派生水深、活珊瑚覆盖、栖息地复杂度、藻类、海草和沙地覆盖图。该数据适合珊瑚礁修复选址和海岸生态遥感应用，但公开的是派生空间产品而非原始高光谱影像。
desc_en: The Caribbean Marine Maps page provides ASU Global Airborne Observatory reef products collected for The Nature Conservancy in Saint Croix and the Dominican Republic. The page states that GAO collected approximately 1 m, 427-sample airborne imaging spectroscopy data and derived maps of bathymetry, live coral cover, habitat complexity, algal cover, seagrass cover, and sand cover. The release is useful for coral restoration site selection and coastal ecosystem remote sensing, while the public access is to derived spatial products rather than raw hyperspectral imagery.
openness: public project page with downloadable spatial data
source_type: derived airborne imaging spectroscopy reef maps
evidence: The Caribbean Marine Maps airborne imagery page states that GAO collected hyperspectral imagery for TNC in Saint Croix and the Dominican Republic and that ASU derived high-resolution maps for bathymetry, live coral cover, habitat complexity, algal cover, seagrass cover and sand cover, with spatial data made available.
scale: Caribbean reef study sites

name: SISTER AVIRIS Classic L2B Vegetative Biochemical Traits 30 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER
theme_canonical: Vegetation biochemical traits from airborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: AVIRIS-Classic derived chlorophyll, nitrogen concentration and leaf mass per area GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected AVIRIS-Classic flight lines in the United States
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: Experimental SISTER CRID 002 downstream product derived from AVIRIS-Classic corrected reflectance and fractional cover. Complements existing AVIRIS-Classic L1/L2 and AVIRIS-NG SISTER product rows.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC SISTER v002 数据集提供由 AVIRIS-Classic 成像光谱数据派生的 30 m 植被生化性状产品。产品包括叶绿素含量、氮浓度和叶质量面积的云优化 GeoTIFF，并附带 quicklook、元数据、处理配置和日志文件。它适合 SBG 预研算法、植被功能性状反演和航空高光谱下游产品评估。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC SISTER v002 dataset provides 30 m vegetation biochemical trait products derived from AVIRIS-Classic imaging spectroscopy. The products include cloud-optimized GeoTIFFs for chlorophyll content, nitrogen concentration, and leaf mass per area, with quicklooks, metadata, run configurations, and processing logs. It supports SBG pre-launch algorithm development, vegetation functional-trait retrieval, and evaluation of airborne hyperspectral downstream products.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived airborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC states this V002 dataset contains AVIRIS-Classic-derived Level 2B vegetative biochemical traits at 30 m, 220 files for 22 flight lines, cloud optimized GeoTIFF outputs for chlorophyll, nitrogen and leaf mass per area, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2230.
scale: 22 selected flight lines
feature_count: 220 files

name: SISTER DESIS L2B Fractional Cover 30 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER / DLR DESIS
theme_canonical: Fractional cover from spaceborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: DESIS derived soil, vegetation, water and snow fractional cover GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected DESIS scenes across global test sites
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: Experimental SISTER CRID 002 product derived from DESIS corrected reflectance. Adds a DESIS L2B product level not represented by the existing DESIS L2A rows. v65 DOI checked against ORNL DAAC guide Citation section.
desc_zh: 该 SISTER v002 数据集提供由 DESIS 空间载荷成像光谱数据派生的 30 m 分数覆盖产品。GeoTIFF 中包含土壤、植被、水体和雪/冰四类覆盖比例，并配套 quicklook、元数据和处理可追溯文件。它适合 SBG 类产品原型、地表覆盖混合像元分析和 DESIS 下游算法验证。
desc_en: This SISTER v002 dataset provides 30 m fractional-cover products derived from DESIS spaceborne imaging spectroscopy. The GeoTIFFs include soil, vegetation, water, and snow/ice area fractions, along with quicklooks, metadata, and processing traceability files. It is useful for SBG-like product prototyping, mixed-pixel surface-cover analysis, and DESIS downstream algorithm validation.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived spaceborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide Citation section gives DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2199 for this SISTER DESIS L2B fractional cover dataset; this corrects a previous DOI transposition with the DESIS L2A resampled reflectance product.
scale: 3 selected DESIS scenes
feature_count: 15 files

name: SISTER DESIS L2B Vegetative Biochemical Traits 30 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER / DLR DESIS
theme_canonical: Vegetation biochemical traits from spaceborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: DESIS derived chlorophyll, nitrogen concentration and leaf mass per area GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected DESIS scenes across global test sites
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: DESIS spectral range limits some trait models; ORNL documentation notes nitrogen and leaf mass per area require infrared wavelengths beyond DESIS range.
desc_zh: 该 SISTER v002 数据集提供由 DESIS 影像光谱数据派生的 30 m 植被生化性状产品。数据以云优化 GeoTIFF 发布，并包含叶绿素、氮浓度和叶质量面积相关变量、quicklook 和处理可追溯文件。由于 DESIS 光谱范围较短，氮和叶质量面积模型存在传感器波段限制，适合作为 DESIS 与宽谱段 VSWIR 传感器算法差异的对比样本。
desc_en: This SISTER v002 dataset provides 30 m vegetation biochemical trait products derived from DESIS imaging spectroscopy. The release includes cloud-optimized GeoTIFFs for chlorophyll, nitrogen, and leaf mass per area related variables, plus quicklooks and processing traceability files. Because DESIS has a shorter spectral range than full VSWIR instruments, the nitrogen and leaf-mass-area products carry a useful sensor-limitation context for cross-sensor algorithm comparison.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived spaceborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC states this V002 DESIS dataset contains 18 files, including three cloud optimized GeoTIFF scenes for vegetative biochemical traits, 30 m spatial resolution, DESIS spectral resolution of 2.5 nm, temporal coverage 2020-08-18 to 2022-06-06, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2200.
scale: 3 selected DESIS scenes
feature_count: 18 files

name: SISTER PRISMA L2B Aquatic Pigments 30 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER / ASI PRISMA
theme_canonical: Aquatic pigments from spaceborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: PRISMA derived chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected PRISMA aquatic scenes across global test sites
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: Experimental aquatic pigment product derived from PRISMA corrected reflectance; complements the existing PRISMA archive, PRISMA L2A SISTER rows, and PRISMA simulated benchmark rows.
desc_zh: 该 SISTER v002 数据集提供由 PRISMA 卫星成像光谱数据派生的 30 m 水体色素产品。GeoTIFF 产品包含叶绿素 a 和藻蓝蛋白浓度，并附带 quicklook、元数据、运行配置和日志。它适合内陆/近岸水体高光谱水色算法、蓝藻监测和 SBG 水生生态产品原型研究。
desc_en: This SISTER v002 dataset provides 30 m aquatic pigment products derived from PRISMA satellite imaging spectroscopy. The GeoTIFF products contain chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin concentration estimates, with quicklooks, metadata, run configurations, and logs. It supports inland and coastal water-color hyperspectral algorithms, cyanobacteria monitoring, and SBG aquatic-ecosystem product prototyping.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived spaceborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC states this V002 PRISMA aquatic pigments dataset has 135 files, three cloud optimized GeoTIFF files for each of 15 flightlines, products for chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin in mg m-3, spatial resolution 30 m, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2223.
scale: 15 selected PRISMA scenes
feature_count: 135 files

name: SISTER EMIT L2B Aquatic Pigments 60 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER / EMIT
theme_canonical: Aquatic pigments from spaceborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: EMIT derived chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected EMIT aquatic scenes across global test sites
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: Experimental aquatic pigment product derived from EMIT reflectance scenes, distinct from the main EMIT mineral and reflectance products already cataloged.
desc_zh: 该 SISTER v002 数据集提供由 EMIT 空间站成像光谱数据派生的 60 m 水体色素产品。产品以云优化 GeoTIFF 发布，包含叶绿素 a 和藻蓝蛋白浓度估计，并配有 quicklook 和处理可追溯文件。它补充了 EMIT 矿物、反射率和温室气体方向之外的水生生态应用数据。
desc_en: This SISTER v002 dataset provides 60 m aquatic pigment products derived from EMIT imaging spectroscopy. The cloud-optimized GeoTIFFs include chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin concentration estimates, with quicklooks and processing traceability files. It complements EMIT mineral, reflectance, and greenhouse-gas products by covering aquatic-ecosystem applications.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived spaceborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC states this V002 EMIT aquatic pigments dataset has 99 files, three cloud optimized GeoTIFF files for each of 11 flightlines, chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin variables in mg m-3, spatial resolution 60 m, temporal coverage 2022-08-10 to 2023-03-25, and DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2217.
scale: 11 selected EMIT scenes
feature_count: 99 files

name: SISTER EMIT L2B Snow Grain Size 60 m V002
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / SISTER / EMIT
theme_canonical: Snow grain size from spaceborne imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: EMIT derived snow grain size and quality mask GeoTIFF products
coverage: selected EMIT snow scenes across global test sites
license: NASA Earthdata / ORNL DAAC open data terms
formats: cloud optimized GeoTIFF, PNG quicklooks, JSON metadata/runconfig, logs
access: ORNL DAAC SISTER user guide and Get Data link
notes: Experimental EMIT cryosphere downstream product. Included to represent SISTER snow/ice products for spaceborne imaging spectroscopy. v64 DOI checked against ORNL DAAC guide Citation section.
desc_zh: 该 SISTER v002 数据集提供由 EMIT 成像光谱数据派生的 60 m 雪粒径产品。云优化 GeoTIFF 包含雪粒径和质量保证掩膜两个波段，并配有 quicklook、元数据和处理日志。它适合雪冰反照率、积雪物理参数和 SBG 冰雪应用算法原型研究。
desc_en: This SISTER v002 dataset provides 60 m snow-grain-size products derived from EMIT imaging spectroscopy. The cloud-optimized GeoTIFFs include snow grain size and a quality-assurance mask, with quicklooks, metadata, and processing logs. It supports snow/ice albedo studies, cryosphere physical-parameter retrieval, and SBG snow-product algorithm prototyping.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset
source_type: derived spaceborne hyperspectral L2B product
evidence: ORNL DAAC guide Citation section gives DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2221 and states this dataset contains EMIT-derived L2B snow grain size products at 60 m, including cloud optimized GeoTIFF snow grain size outputs for two flight lines plus quicklooks and traceability files.
scale: 2 selected EMIT scenes
feature_count: 10 files

name: MDAS Augsburg Multimodal Remote Sensing Benchmark Dataset
provider: TUM mediaTUM / DLR / GFZ / Zhu-XLab
theme_canonical: Multimodal benchmark with airborne hyperspectral imagery
theme_raw: HySpex hyperspectral imagery, Sentinel-1 SAR, Sentinel-2 multispectral, DSM and GIS data for Augsburg
coverage: Augsburg, Germany; city-scale benchmark; data collected on 2018-05-07
formats: downloadable research data archive; associated code on Zenodo/GitHub
access: mediaTUM DOI landing page with data-server download, FTP and rsync options
notes: Found via a 2-hop chain from MDAS ESSD article to mediaTUM data DOI and Zenodo code DOI. The Zenodo record is code/pretrained models; the data DOI is the authoritative dataset landing page.
desc_zh: MDAS 是德国 Augsburg 城市范围的多模态遥感 benchmark，包含同一天获取的 SAR、多光谱、高光谱、DSM 和 GIS 数据。高光谱部分由 DLR Remote Sensing Technology Institute 使用 HySpex 在 23 条飞行带中采集，数据用于分辨率增强、光谱解混和地物分类基线实验。mediaTUM 页面提供约 35 GB 数据下载入口，另有 Zenodo/GitHub 代码和预训练模型记录。
desc_en: MDAS is a city-scale multimodal remote-sensing benchmark for Augsburg, Germany, combining SAR, multispectral imagery, hyperspectral imagery, DSM, and GIS data acquired on the same date. The hyperspectral component was acquired by DLR's Remote Sensing Technology Institute with HySpex over 23 flight strips, supporting baseline experiments for resolution enhancement, spectral unmixing, and land-cover classification. The mediaTUM record provides about 35 GB of downloadable data, with companion code and pretrained models on Zenodo/GitHub.
openness: public mediaTUM research data record under CC BY-SA 4.0
source_type: multimodal airborne hyperspectral benchmark
evidence: The ESSD paper states MDAS includes SAR, multispectral image, hyperspectral image, DSM and GIS data for Augsburg collected on 2018-05-07 and gives the data DOI 10.14459/2022mp1657312; mediaTUM states HySpex data were acquired by DLR in 23 flight strips and offers a 35 GB download.
scale: city-scale multimodal benchmark
feature_count: 23 HySpex flight strips plus other modalities

name: Zhu Hyperspectral Unmixing Datasets and Ground Truths Collection
provider: Feiyun Zhu / Hyperspectral unmixing research mirrors
theme_canonical: Classic unmixing benchmark scenes
theme_raw: Samson, Jasper Ridge, Urban and Cuprite hyperspectral unmixing datasets with ground-truth abundance/endmember files
coverage: Samson, Jasper Ridge, Urban HYDICE and Cuprite benchmark scenes
license: free for research and education only; cite associated papers
formats: ENVI IMG, MATLAB MAT, ZIP ground-truth files
access: dataset mirror page with escience.cn download links
doi_or_citation: Zhu, F. 2017. Hyperspectral Unmixing: Ground Truth Labeling, Datasets, Benchmark Performances and Survey. arXiv:1708.05125
notes: Found by following citations from multiple unmixing papers to the original escience Dataset_GT page, then using an accessible mirror that preserves dataset descriptions and download links. Licensing is research/education only, so redistribution should be avoided.
desc_zh: 该页面整理了高光谱解混领域常用的 Samson、Jasper Ridge、Urban 和 Cuprite 真实场景及其端元/丰度 ground truth。页面说明数据以 ENVI 和 MATLAB 格式提供，Samson 为 95 x 95 子区、156 波段，Jasper Ridge 为 100 x 100 子区、198/224 波段，Urban 和 Cuprite 也提供常用预处理版本。它是许多解混论文 2 跳引用链中反复出现的经典数据来源，但使用边界是科研和教育用途。
desc_en: This page collects the classic hyperspectral unmixing scenes Samson, Jasper Ridge, Urban, and Cuprite together with endmember and abundance ground-truth files. The page describes ENVI and MATLAB downloads, including the 95 by 95, 156-band Samson subset, 100 by 100 Jasper Ridge subsets with 198/224 bands, and common Urban and Cuprite preprocessing variants. It is a frequently cited source reached through multi-hop unmixing-paper references, but the stated use boundary is research and education only.
openness: public mirror page; research and education use only
source_type: classic hyperspectral unmixing benchmark collection
evidence: The mirror page cites the original escience Dataset_GT source, states the datasets and ground truths are free for research and education only, lists Samson/Jasper Ridge/Urban/Cuprite, and provides ENVI/MATLAB/ground-truth download links with dimensions, wavelengths and endmember descriptions.
scale: four classic benchmark scenes
feature_count: 4 scenes plus ground truth variants

name: ReDSUNN Multitemporal Hyperspectral Unmixing Datasets
provider: Zenodo / Ricardo Borsoi / Tales Imbiriba / Pau Closas
theme_canonical: Multitemporal hyperspectral unmixing benchmark
theme_raw: synthetic and real multitemporal hyperspectral images for dynamical unmixing
coverage: synthetic examples plus a real multitemporal hyperspectral image sequence used in ReDSUNN/TIP 2023 experiments
formats: MATLAB MAT files
access: Zenodo record with direct downloadable files
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.7796598; Dynamical Hyperspectral Unmixing with Variational Recurrent Neural Networks, IEEE TIP 2023
notes: Found by following the unmixing-method literature/code chain to the ReDSUNN Zenodo archive. Includes synthetic and real multitemporal data; not all files are observed Earth imagery.
desc_zh: ReDSUNN 多时相高光谱解混数据集发布在 Zenodo，用于评估动态高光谱解混算法。记录包含合成例子、端元文件和一个真实多时相高光谱数据文件，均为 MATLAB MAT 格式，总大小约 321 MB，并关联 IEEE TIP 2023 的变分循环神经网络解混论文和 GitHub 代码。它适合多时相端元变化、丰度演化和动态解混算法验证。
desc_en: The ReDSUNN multitemporal hyperspectral unmixing dataset is a Zenodo release for evaluating dynamical hyperspectral unmixing algorithms. It includes synthetic examples, endmember files, and a real multitemporal hyperspectral data file in MATLAB MAT format, totaling about 321 MB, and is linked to the IEEE TIP 2023 variational recurrent neural network unmixing paper and GitHub code. It is useful for testing endmember variability, abundance evolution, and dynamic unmixing methods.
openness: public Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: multitemporal hyperspectral unmixing benchmark
evidence: Zenodo states the dataset contains synthetic and real multitemporal hyperspectral images for dynamical hyperspectral unmixing, lists MAT files totaling 321.4 MB, links the ReDSUNN GitHub repository, and gives DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7796598 under CC BY 4.0.
scale: synthetic examples plus real multitemporal sequence
feature_count: 6 listed MAT files

name: EHU IC Synthetic Hyperspectral Collection and Synthesis Toolbox

theme_raw: synthetic hyperspectral images generated from USGS spectral-library endmembers with multiple SNR levels
coverage: synthetic 128 x 128 scenes with 431 spectral bands

access: EHU GIC wiki page with toolbox and synthetic collection download links

time_span: page last edited 2011-04-05; collection remains accessible
notes: Found as a third-hop lead from the Zhu dataset page's synthesis-tools section. This is synthetic benchmark data, not observed remote sensing imagery.
desc_zh: EHU/GIC 的 Hyperspectral Imagery Synthesis tools 页面提供 MATLAB 合成工具和 IC Synthetic Hyperspectral Collection。合成影像由 USGS 谱库中的五个端元生成，每个场景为 128 x 128 像素、431 个波段，并在 ZIP 中提供无噪声版本和 SNR 20/40/60/80 dB 的噪声版本。它适合高光谱解混、噪声鲁棒性和算法教学验证，但属于合成数据而非真实遥感影像。
desc_en: The EHU/GIC Hyperspectral Imagery Synthesis tools page provides a MATLAB synthesis toolbox and the IC Synthetic Hyperspectral Collection. The synthetic images are generated from five selected USGS spectral-library endmembers, with 128 by 128 pixels and 431 bands, and each ZIP includes a noise-free image plus SNR 20/40/60/80 dB variants. The collection is useful for hyperspectral unmixing, noise-robustness testing, and teaching, while being synthetic rather than observed remote-sensing imagery.
openness: public EHU/GIC wiki downloads; toolbox GPL
source_type: synthetic hyperspectral benchmark collection
evidence: The EHU/GIC page states the toolbox is distributed under GNU GPL, describes the IC Synthetic Hyperspectral Collection as 128x128 images with 431 spectral bands generated from five USGS endmembers, and provides download links for Legendre, Spheric Gaussian, Exponential Gaussian, Rational Gaussian and Matern Gaussian field collections.
scale: five synthetic collection families
feature_count: 5 ZIP collections; 5 MAT files per ZIP

name: SWIR Hyperspectral Porous Stones Moisture Maps Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Royal Institute for Cultural Heritage / University of Antwerp
theme_canonical: Close-range SWIR hyperspectral material moisture benchmark
theme_raw: Imec Snapscan SWIR hyperspectral cubes and moisture maps of porous stones and brick
coverage: laboratory samples of five natural stones and one archaeological red brick at controlled saturation levels
formats: MAT files in ZIP archive, CSV mean spectra
access: Zenodo record with direct downloadable archive
notes: Found through a 2/3-hop chain from University of Antwerp remote-sensing publications to WHISPERS and Zenodo. This is close-range laboratory hyperspectral imaging for material/moisture mapping, not airborne or satellite Earth observation.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供六类多孔建筑材料的 SWIR 高光谱影像、对应水分图和平均光谱 CSV，包括五种天然石材和一类考古红砖。样品在干燥、25%、50%、75%、90% 和 100% 饱和状态下采集，使用 Imec Snapscan SWIR 相机，约 1100-1670 nm、100 个波段。它适合材料水分反演、文化遗产无损检测和高光谱解混/回归算法验证，但属于近距离实验室成像而非航空/卫星遥感。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides SWIR hyperspectral images, moisture maps, and mean-spectra CSV files for six porous building materials: five natural stones and one archaeological red brick. Samples were imaged under dry, 25%, 50%, 75%, 90%, and 100% saturation conditions with an Imec Snapscan SWIR camera over roughly 1100-1670 nm and 100 spectral bands. It is useful for material moisture retrieval, cultural-heritage nondestructive testing, and hyperspectral unmixing/regression validation, while being close-range laboratory imaging rather than airborne or satellite remote sensing.
openness: public Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range SWIR hyperspectral material benchmark
evidence: Zenodo states the v3 dataset provides hyperspectral images, moisture maps and mean spectra for six porous building materials, acquired with an Imec Snapscan SWIR camera over 1100-1670 nm with 100 bands, distributed as an 11.5 GB ZIP plus CSV under CC BY 4.0.
scale: 72 sample objects across six saturation states
feature_count: hyperspectral and moisture-map MAT files plus CSV

name: Checkerboard Hyperspectral Unmixing Dataset with Ground Truth
provider: Zenodo / University of Antwerp Vision Lab
theme_canonical: Controlled unmixing benchmark with spectral variability
theme_raw: Imec Snapscan VNIR checkerboard hyperspectral images with known abundance ground truth
coverage: laboratory checkerboard target under seven illumination conditions
formats: ZIP archive with MATLAB MAT files and scripts
access: Zenodo record with direct downloadable archive
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.15632215; A benchmark linear unmixing dataset with spectral variability and ground truth, WHISPERS 2025
time_span: published 2025-06-10; version v1 with newer Zenodo version noted
notes: Found through a 2-hop chain from DBLP Paul Scheunders data-artifact entries to the Zenodo DOI and related arXiv/WHISPERS unmixing papers. Close-range laboratory benchmark, not airborne or satellite imagery.
desc_zh: Checkerboard 数据集是面向高光谱解混和光谱可变性研究的受控实验 benchmark。它包含 7 幅由 Imec Snapscan VNIR 相机采集的棋盘图案高光谱影像，每幅为 400 x 400 像素、150 个波段，并在不同入射角和光照强度下采集以引入端元光谱变化。棋盘图案由黑、黄、红、蓝四种颜色按 25% 间隔混合，因此可生成已知丰度的混合像元 ground truth。
desc_en: The Checkerboard dataset is a controlled benchmark for hyperspectral unmixing and spectral variability research. It contains seven hyperspectral images of a printed checkerboard acquired with an Imec Snapscan VNIR camera, each with 400 by 400 pixels and 150 spectral bands, under varying illumination angles and intensities. The checkerboard is built from black, yellow, red, and blue colors mixed at 25% intervals, providing known abundance ground truth for mixed pixels.
openness: public Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range hyperspectral unmixing benchmark
evidence: Zenodo states the dataset contains seven hyperspectral images of a printed checkerboard pattern, 400x400 pixels and 150 spectral bands, acquired with an Imec Snapscan VNIR camera under varying illumination conditions, and provides a 1.3 GB `checkerboard_dataset.zip` under CC BY 4.0.
scale: 7 controlled laboratory scenes
feature_count: 7 hyperspectral images plus white-panel scans and scripts

name: PCB-Vision RGB-Hyperspectral Printed Circuit Board Benchmark
provider: RODARE / HZDR Helmholtz Institute Freiberg for Resource Technology
theme_canonical: Close-range e-waste hyperspectral segmentation
theme_raw: Specim FX10 VNIR hyperspectral cubes and RGB images of printed circuit boards
coverage: 53 printed circuit board scenes acquired on a conveyor-belt imaging setup
formats: HSI data files with HDR headers, PNG/JPG masks and RGB images, ZIP archive
access: RODARE DOI record with direct downloadable ZIP
notes: Found as a second-hop lead from Electrolyzers-HSI related-work table and HIF/HZDR hyperspectral recycling publications. Close-range industrial benchmark, not airborne/spaceborne remote sensing.
desc_zh: PCB-Vision 是面向电子废弃物回收的 RGB-高光谱多场景 benchmark，包含 53 块印刷电路板的高空间分辨率 RGB 图像和对应 VNIR 高光谱立方体。高光谱部分由 Specim FX10 在 400-1000 nm 范围采集，共 224 个波段，并提供 PCB 掩膜、集成电路、电容和连接器等分割 ground truth。它适合高光谱语义分割、RGB-HSI 融合和近距离工业分选算法验证。
desc_en: PCB-Vision is a multi-scene RGB-hyperspectral benchmark for electronic-waste recycling, containing 53 printed circuit boards with high-resolution RGB images and paired VNIR hyperspectral cubes. The hyperspectral data were acquired with a Specim FX10 over 400-1000 nm with 224 bands, and the release includes PCB masks plus segmentation ground truth for integrated circuits, capacitors, and connectors. It supports hyperspectral semantic segmentation, RGB-HSI fusion, and close-range industrial sorting algorithm development.
openness: public RODARE dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range RGB-hyperspectral benchmark
evidence: RODARE states PCB-Vision is an open dataset of 53 PCB scenes, RGB images collected on a conveyor belt and hyperspectral images acquired with a Specim FX10, with 224 VNIR bands from 400-1000 nm, masks/ground truth, and DOI 10.14278/rodare.2704.
scale: 53 close-range PCB scenes
feature_count: 53 hyperspectral cubes plus RGB and masks

name: Multi-Sensor Spectral Database of WEEE Polymers
provider: RODARE / HZDR / RAMSES-4-CE
theme_canonical: Polymer identification with hyperspectral and point spectroscopy
theme_raw: VNIR, SWIR, MWIR hyperspectral imaging plus Raman, FTIR and PSR spectra for 23 WEEE polymers
coverage: laboratory polymer samples from waste electrical and electronic equipment
formats: ZIP archive with spectral library and sensor data
access: RODARE DOI record with direct downloadable ZIP
notes: Found via third-hop HZDR/RODARE related identifiers from Polymers and Electrolyzers-HSI records. Multi-sensor material database rather than remote-sensing imagery.
desc_zh: 该 RODARE 数据库面向废弃电气电子设备中的聚合物识别，包含 23 类聚合物的多传感器光谱数据。成像部分覆盖 VNIR、SWIR 和 MWIR 高光谱反射率，同时还包含 Raman、FTIR 和便携式光谱仪点测数据。它适合聚合物分选、跨传感器光谱识别和高光谱材料分类研究，但属于近距离工业材料数据库。
desc_en: This RODARE database supports identification of polymers from waste electrical and electronic equipment, covering 23 polymer types. The imaging component includes VNIR, SWIR, and MWIR hyperspectral reflectance data, complemented by Raman, FTIR, and portable spectroradiometer point measurements. It is useful for polymer sorting, cross-sensor spectral identification, and hyperspectral material-classification studies, while being a close-range industrial material database.
openness: public RODARE dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: multi-sensor hyperspectral material database
evidence: RODARE states the database includes spectra from imaging and point sensors on 23 polymers, with HSI sensors mapping reflectance in VNIR 350-1000 nm, SWIR 1000-2500 nm and MWIR 2500-5000 nm, a 110.8 MB `RAMSES_Polymer_Library.zip`, DOI 10.14278/rodare.2448, and CC BY 4.0 files.
scale: 23 polymer classes
feature_count: 23 polymers with multiple sensor modalities

name: Polymers Hyperspectral Imaging E-Waste Benchmark
provider: RODARE / HZDR / HIF Exploration
theme_canonical: Plastic-type identification with close-range hyperspectral imaging
theme_raw: AisaFENIX VNIR-SWIR hyperspectral cubes of reference and shredded polymer samples
coverage: laboratory reference and shredded polymer samples common in e-waste
formats: DAT/HDR hyperspectral cubes, PNG masks, ZIP archive
access: RODARE DOI record with direct downloadable ZIP
notes: Found through HZDR/RODARE publication chain from Scheunders/Gloaguen hyperspectral recycling works. Close-range industrial materials benchmark, not Earth-observation imagery.
desc_zh: Polymers Hyperspectral Imaging 是面向塑料类型识别的多场景高光谱 benchmark，包含参考聚合物样品和电子废弃物中常见的粉碎聚合物样品。数据由 AisaFENIX 光谱仪采集，覆盖 400-2500 nm、450 个波段，文件包含高光谱 .dat/.hdr 立方体和多类分割掩膜。它适合塑料分选、深度学习高光谱分类和工业回收场景算法验证。
desc_en: Polymers Hyperspectral Imaging is a multi-scene benchmark for plastic-type identification, covering reference polymer samples and shredded polymer pieces common in e-waste. The data were acquired with an AisaFENIX spectrometer over 400-2500 nm with 450 bands, and the files include hyperspectral .dat/.hdr cubes and multi-class segmentation masks. It supports plastic sorting, deep-learning hyperspectral classification, and industrial recycling algorithm validation.
openness: public RODARE dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range hyperspectral polymer benchmark
evidence: RODARE states the polymers dataset is a multiscene hyperspectral benchmark with reference and shredded polymer samples, 450 bands in 400-2500 nm acquired by AisaFENIX, HSI .dat/.hdr data, masks for six classes, a 542.1 MB ZIP, DOI 10.14278/rodare.3390, and CC BY 4.0 license.
scale: reference and shredded polymer sample scenes
feature_count: train/test hyperspectral scenes and masks

name: Electrolyzers-HSI RGB-Hyperspectral Recycling Benchmark
provider: RODARE / HZDR / HIF Exploration
theme_canonical: Close-range hyperspectral material classification for recycling
theme_raw: FENIX VNIR-SWIR hyperspectral cubes, RGB images and masks of shredded electrolyzer components
coverage: 55 close-range scenes of shredded electrolyzer samples on a conveyor-belt setup
license: public RODARE dataset; associated Scientific Data article under CC BY 4.0
formats: IMG/HDR hyperspectral cubes, JPG RGB images, PNG masks, ZIP archive
access: RODARE DOI record with direct downloadable ZIP
time_span: RODARE record published 2025-04-07; Scientific Data article 2025-11-19
notes: Found through the HZDR/RODARE hyperspectral recycling paper chain; close-range industrial benchmark, not airborne/spaceborne remote sensing.
desc_zh: Electrolyzers-HSI 是面向电解槽部件回收识别的 RGB-高光谱多场景 benchmark。RODARE 数据记录包含 55 个粉碎电解槽样品场景，每个子文件夹提供 RGB 图像、GT 分割掩膜以及 FENIX 高光谱 .img/.hdr 立方体；高光谱覆盖 VNIR-SWIR 约 400-2500 nm、450 个波段。它适合材料级分类、RGB-HSI 融合和工业回收场景下的高光谱模型鲁棒性研究。
desc_en: Electrolyzers-HSI is a multi-scene RGB-hyperspectral benchmark for recognizing electrolyzer components in recycling streams. The RODARE record contains 55 scenes of shredded electrolyzer samples, each providing an RGB image, GT segmentation mask, and FENIX hyperspectral .img/.hdr cube; the hyperspectral data cover the VNIR-SWIR range around 400-2500 nm with 450 bands. It supports material-level classification, RGB-HSI fusion, and robustness testing for hyperspectral models in industrial recycling scenarios.
openness: public RODARE dataset; Scientific Data article under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range RGB-hyperspectral recycling benchmark
evidence: RODARE states Electrolyzers-HSI contains 55 scenes, RGB images, GT masks and HSI .img/.hdr cubes acquired with a FENIX spectrometer over 400-2500 nm with 450 bands; the Scientific Data article states 55 co-registered RGB/HSI triplets and 424,169 labeled pixels.
scale: 55 close-range scenes
feature_count: 55 RGB-HSI-mask triplets

name: Multisensor Hyperspectral Intimate Mixtures Benchmark Dataset
provider: University of Antwerp Vision Lab / VisionlabHyperspectral GitHub
theme_canonical: Multisensor nonlinear unmixing benchmark
theme_raw: reflectance spectra and hyperspectral sensor data for 325 clay-powder intimate mixtures acquired by 13 sensors
coverage: laboratory mixtures of five clay powders, including binary, ternary, quaternary and quinary compositions
license: GitHub repository without explicit license; use according to cited paper and repository terms
formats: downloadable dataset linked from GitHub; PDF documentation
access: GitHub repository with Google Drive data link
notes: Found via a third-hop chain from Scheunders/Antwerp unmixing bibliography to the arXiv paper and then the GitHub repository. This is laboratory material spectroscopy/imaging for unmixing, not Earth-observation imagery.
desc_zh: 该数据集是面向非线性解混和光谱可变性研究的多传感器高光谱 benchmark。作者将 Kaolin、Roof clay、Red clay、mixed clay 和 Calcium hydroxide 五种黏土粉末混合，制备 325 个二元、三元、四元和五元混合样品，并用 13 种高光谱/光谱传感器在 350-15385 nm 范围采集反射率。数据的组成丰度由配方确定，适合验证材料组成估计、非线性解混和跨传感器泛化。
desc_en: This dataset is a multisensor hyperspectral benchmark for nonlinear unmixing and spectral-variability research. The authors mixed five clay powders, Kaolin, Roof clay, Red clay, mixed clay, and Calcium hydroxide, into 325 binary, ternary, quaternary, and quinary samples, then acquired reflectance with 13 hyperspectral/spectral sensors over 350-15385 nm. The composition ground truth is known by construction, making the data useful for material-composition estimation, nonlinear unmixing, and cross-sensor generalization studies.
openness: public GitHub repository with external data link
source_type: multisensor hyperspectral material-unmixing benchmark
evidence: The GitHub repository states it generated 325 mixtures from five clay powders across all binary, ternary, quaternary and quinary combinations, cites IEEE Sensors Journal DOI 10.1109/JSEN.2023.3343552, and provides a Google Drive download link; the arXiv/paper abstract states 13 hyperspectral sensors covered 350-15385 nm.
scale: 325 laboratory mixture samples
feature_count: 325 mixtures across 13 sensors

desc_en: This figshare repository preserves the UAV hyperspectral snow dataset used for the Senator Beck Basin snow grain-size and albedo study. It combines Resonon NIR-HSI UAV products, ASD field spectra, Landsat 9 comparison data and analysis scripts to support retrieval and multiscale validation of snow properties.
openness: downloadable figshare repository under CC BY 4.0
source_type: UAV hyperspectral snow remote-sensing repository
evidence: The Frontiers article data-availability statement points to figshare DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.20779072.v4; the figshare API identifies a dataset, CC BY 4.0 license, a 1.26 GB ZIP repository, and description folders for field spectrometer, UAV-HSI and related scripts.

provider: Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology / Mendeley Data

desc_en: Agriculture Forest Hyperspectral Data is an open Mendeley dataset for a mixed tropical forest and agricultural setting in India. The record describes high-resolution hyperspectral imagery with associated ground-truth spectra of vegetable crops, supporting forest-agriculture discrimination, spectral matching, classification and sub-pixel target detection.
openness: downloadable Mendeley Data record under CC BY 4.0
source_type: high-resolution hyperspectral imagery with ground spectra
evidence: The Mendeley page lists DOI 10.17632/p7n6ktjdx7.1, CC BY 4.0, categories including Remote Sensing and Hyperspectral Imaging, and describes high-resolution hyperspectral imagery with associated ground-truth spectra for tropical forest/agriculture analysis.

desc_en: TERRA-REF is an open reference dataset for plant phenotyping, genomics and imaging sensors. The Zenodo/Dryad record describes a field scanner that captured color, thermal, hyperspectral, active fluorescence, 3D structure and environmental observations linked to field and trait measurements, making it useful for sensor fusion and crop-trait retrieval research.

evidence: The Zenodo/Dryad record states the ARPA-E TERRA-REF field scanner captures color, thermal, hyperspectral and active fluorescence imagery plus 3D structure; files include metadata, sensor_data_catalogs and trait_data, and the license is CC0.

access: Mendeley Data DOI record with Download All option
notes: Found by following the 2026 Data in Brief data article to its Mendeley Data DOI. Wheat uses a Cubert ULTRIS X20 Plus hyperspectral imager; peach-tree data are multispectral, so the hyperspectral component is crop-specific.
desc_zh: 该 Mendeley 数据集保存了北京平谷小麦和桃树样地的多角度无人机反射率观测。Data in Brief 文章说明小麦样地使用 DJI Matrice 350 RTK 搭载 Cubert ULTRIS X20 Plus 高光谱成像仪，采集 350-1000 nm、164 波段数据；桃树样地使用多光谱相机。数据包含原始 DN、多角度几何参数和辐射定标后的反射率，适合 BRDF、植被指数角度效应和作物遥感参数反演研究。
desc_en: This Mendeley dataset provides multi-angle UAV reflectance observations for wheat and peach-tree plots in Pinggu, Beijing. The linked Data in Brief article reports that the wheat plot used a DJI Matrice 350 RTK with a Cubert ULTRIS X20 Plus hyperspectral imager, while the peach plot used a multispectral camera; the release includes raw DN values, viewing/solar geometry and calibrated reflectance products.
openness: downloadable Mendeley Data record under CC BY 4.0
source_type: multi-angle UAV hyperspectral crop reflectance dataset
evidence: The Data in Brief article gives repository DOI 10.17632/hgcbnmxvr8.5 and direct URL; the Mendeley page lists Version 5, CC BY 4.0, raw and processed multi-angle remote-sensing data, and ENVI-based reflectance extraction.
scale: two 10 m x 10 m crop plots
feature_count: more than 1,800 multi-angle samples reported in the article

name: Madingley Raw Leaf Trait and Hyperspectral Spectral Data
provider: University of Cambridge / figshare
theme_canonical: Seasonal leaf-trait hyperspectral reflectance library
theme_raw: raw leaf trait measurements and spectral reflectance of eight temperate broadleaf deciduous tree species
coverage: Madingley Wood and adjacent area, Cambridge, United Kingdom
formats: CSV raw trait table and CSV raw spectral table
access: figshare DOI record with downloadable CSV files
time_span: leaf measurements across the 2017 growing season; dataset published 2021-10-31
notes: Found through a Cambridge repository article relationship that links the RSE paper to the figshare supplement. This is leaf-level spectroscopy rather than airborne/spaceborne imagery, but it is directly tied to imaging-spectroscopy trait retrieval.
desc_zh: 该 figshare 数据集提供 Madingley Wood 八种温带阔叶树在 2017 年生长季内的叶片性状和高光谱反射率原始数据。它与 Remote Sensing of Environment 论文配套，用于研究跨物种、跨月份的叶片功能性状反演模型是否可泛化。数据是叶片尺度光谱库，不是成像场景，但可作为森林成像光谱性状制图的校准和机理支撑资料。
desc_en: This figshare dataset provides raw leaf traits and hyperspectral reflectance spectra for eight temperate broadleaf tree species sampled through the 2017 growing season at Madingley Wood. It supplements a Remote Sensing of Environment study on seasonal trait prediction from spectroscopy and is best treated as a leaf-level calibration library rather than an airborne or satellite image archive.
openness: downloadable figshare CSV files under CC BY 4.0
source_type: leaf-level hyperspectral reflectance library
evidence: The Cambridge repository marks the figshare DOI as supplementary material for the RSE article; the figshare API identifies two files, Raw trait data.csv and Raw spectral data.csv, with CC BY 4.0 license and a Madingley Wood description.
scale: leaf spectroscopy site dataset
feature_count: eight tree species; 21 leaf traits

name: Avocado Olive Vineyard Multispectral and Hyperspectral Health Dataset
provider: Juan Sebastian Estrada / figshare
theme_canonical: Close-range crop health multispectral imagery and hyperspectral reflectance
theme_raw: multispectral leaf images, 350-2500 nm hyperspectral reflectance, moisture, chlorophyll and nitrogen measurements
coverage: Avocado, olive and vineyard leaf samples under dehydration stages
formats: RAR archive with multispectral TIFF images and hyperspectral reflectance measurements
access: figshare DOI record with 1.1 GB downloadable archive
notes: Found as a second-hop related figshare dataset while checking plant hyperspectral reflectance supplements. This is close-range laboratory/leaf data, not airborne or satellite remote sensing.
desc_zh: 该 figshare 数据集面向鳄梨、橄榄和葡萄叶片健康状态评估，包含多光谱叶片图像、350-2500 nm 高光谱反射率、重量、叶片含水量、叶绿素和氮含量等数据。样本覆盖三类作物叶片并经历多个脱水阶段，可用于作物胁迫、叶片水分/色素反演和近距离高光谱-多光谱融合研究。它属于近距离/实验室尺度资料，不应视为机载或卫星影像档案。
desc_en: This figshare dataset supports health assessment of avocado, olive and grapevine leaves with multispectral images, 350-2500 nm hyperspectral reflectance, weight, leaf moisture, chlorophyll and nitrogen measurements. It is useful for close-range crop-stress and spectral-fusion studies, but should be treated as laboratory/proximal data rather than airborne or satellite imagery.
openness: downloadable figshare archive under CC BY 4.0
source_type: close-range multispectral imagery and hyperspectral reflectance dataset
evidence: The figshare page and API list DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.26950660.v2, a 1.1 GB DATASET.rar archive, CC BY 4.0 license, and description of multispectral images plus 350-2500 nm hyperspectral reflectance for avocado, olive and vineyard leaves.
scale: proximal leaf sample dataset
feature_count: 106 avocado leaves, 111 vineyard leaves and 106 grape leaves reported on the figshare page

desc_en: This LP DAAC product provides multi-day airborne hyperspectral reflectance for the Indian Cave ForestGeo plot in Nebraska. It was collected by the Nebraska Earth Observatory with a Specim AisaKESTREL pushbroom sensor on a Piper Saratoga aircraft and provides 1 m imagery over 400-1000 nm with 178 corrected hyperspectral bands for forest biodiversity and function studies.

feature_count: 178 bands

feature_count: 64 bands
