theme_raw: DHPC Mer Bleue Peatland hyperspectral point cloud
coverage: Mer Bleue Peatland near Ottawa, Ontario, Canada
formats: zipped text hyperspectral point cloud, metadata text file, thumbnail PNG
access: Zenodo direct download
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.4694950; Inamdar et al. 2021 Frontiers in Remote Sensing DOI 10.3389/frsen.2021.675323
time_span: example dataset published 2021; acquisition date in metadata file
notes: Non-raster but remote-sensing hyperspectral dataset: directly georeferenced hyperspectral point cloud preserving 288-band spectral information. Included because it is an airborne/UAV remote sensing hyperspectral data product with open files and metadata.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供加拿大 Ottawa 附近 Mer Bleue Peatland 的直接地理参考高光谱点云（DHPC）示例数据。记录说明主文件 `HPC_288band_xyz_final.txt.zip` 约 1.9 GB，并附带元数据文件，记录采集仪器、时间、平台、光谱单位、波长和 FWHM 等参数。它不是传统栅格影像，而是保留高光谱成像数据完整性的点云式遥感高光谱数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides a directly georeferenced hyperspectral point cloud (DHPC) example from the Mer Bleue Peatland near Ottawa, Canada. The record lists the main `HPC_288band_xyz_final.txt.zip` file at about 1.9 GB plus a metadata file describing acquisition instrument, time, platform, spectral units, wavelengths, and FWHM values. It is not a conventional raster image but is a remote-sensing hyperspectral point-cloud product designed to preserve imaging spectroscopy information.
openness: public Zenodo dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: airborne/UAV hyperspectral point cloud
evidence: Zenodo record 4694950 cites DOI 10.5281/zenodo.4694950 and describes a Directly-Georeferenced Hyperspectral Point Cloud from Mer Bleue Peatland near Ottawa, with HPC_288band_xyz_final.txt.zip, metadata text file, thumbnail, acquisition/platform/spectral metadata, and related Frontiers in Remote Sensing paper DOI 10.3389/frsen.2021.675323.
scale: example point cloud dataset; data volume 1.9 GB main zip
feature_count: 288-band hyperspectral point cloud with wavelength/FWHM metadata
file_size: HPC_288band_xyz_final.txt.zip 1.9 GB; metadata 8.7 kB; thumbnail 2.1 MB

name: ASPA 135 UAV Hyperspectral Moss Health and Lichen Classification Maps
provider: Australian Antarctic Data Centre / QUT / University of Wollongong / Scientific Reports authors
theme_canonical: UAV hyperspectral Antarctic vegetation classification products
theme_raw: AAS_4628_UAS_ASPA135_AERIAL_HSISEG_MOSSLICHEN_2023
coverage: Antarctic Specially Protected Area 135, Windmill Islands, East Antarctica
formats: GeoTIFF and ENVI classification rasters, class legend, transect boundaries, per-class statistics, summary tables, model metrics
access: Research Data Australia / Australian Antarctic Data Centre download
doi_or_citation: AADC metadata ID AAS_4628_UAS_ASPA135_AERIAL_HSISEG_MOSSLICHEN_2023; related article DOI 10.1038/s41598-025-11535-4
notes: Derived classification product from drone-mounted hyperspectral imaging surveys at 30 m and 70 m AGL. Included as hyperspectral-derived raster products, not raw HSI cubes. Download endpoint observed at https://data.aad.gov.au/eds/6097/download.
desc_zh: 该数据集提供东南极 Windmill Islands 的 ASPA 135 区域 UAV 高光谱调查派生的苔藓健康与地衣分类图。Research Data Australia 说明数据来自 2023 年 1 月 28 日至 2 月 2 日、30 m 和 70 m AGL 的无人机高光谱影像，产品包括 GeoTIFF/ENVI 分类栅格、类别图例、样线边界、面积统计、论文汇总表和模型性能指标。它是极地生态高光谱遥感的派生分类产品。
desc_en: This dataset provides moss health and lichen classification maps derived from UAV hyperspectral surveys over ASPA 135 in the Windmill Islands, East Antarctica. Research Data Australia describes drone-mounted HSI acquisitions from 28 January to 2 February 2023 at 30 m and 70 m AGL, with GeoTIFF/ENVI classification rasters, legends, transect boundaries, area statistics, publication summary tables, and model metrics. It is a derived polar-ecology hyperspectral remote-sensing classification product.
openness: public AADC/Research Data Australia record under CC BY 4.0
source_type: derived UAV hyperspectral classification rasters
evidence: Research Data Australia record 3752635 describes georeferenced vegetation classification maps derived from drone-mounted hyperspectral imaging surveys over ASPA 135, acquired 2023-01-28 to 2023-02-02 at about 30 m and 70 m AGL. It lists GeoTIFF and ENVI classification rasters, class legend, transect boundaries, per-class area statistics, summary tables, model metrics, CC BY 4.0 license, AADC metadata identifier AAS_4628_UAS_ASPA135_AERIAL_HSISEG_MOSSLICHEN_2023, and related Scientific Reports DOI 10.1038/s41598-025-11535-4.
scale: about 6.5 ha of transect areas; 30 m and 70 m AGL UAV products
feature_count: derived classes: Healthy, Stressed, Moribund moss, Lichen, Non-vegetated surfaces

name: GHISACASIA Global Hyperspectral Imaging Spectral-library of Agricultural Crops for Central Asia V001
provider: LP DAAC / USGS WGSC
theme_canonical: Spectral library derived from hyperspectral imagery
theme_raw: GHISA Central Asia
coverage: Galaba and Kuva farm fields, Syr Darya river basin, Central Asia; bounding box N45 S36 E77 W56
license: US Government Works / public NASA Earthdata use
formats: Excel spectral library (.xlsx), SAS code ZIP, PDF documentation
access: Earthdata / LP DAAC cloud collection
time_span: 2006-04-30 to 2007-08-28; Hyperion imagery acquired in 2007 and ASD field spectra acquired in 2006-2007
notes: Earthdata and CMR describe GHISACASIA as a GHISA Version 1 spectral library for dominant crops in Central Asia, derived from EO-1 Hyperion imagery and field ASD spectroradiometer data. It provides crop labels, image information, plot IDs, study area, instrument, acquisition date and crop type labels in .xlsx files, with processing algorithms in SAS code.
desc_zh: GHISACASIA 是 GHISA 系列中面向中亚农业区的高光谱作物光谱库，由 NASA LP DAAC 与 USGS WGSC 发布。它基于 2007 年 EO-1 Hyperion 高光谱影像和 2006-2007 年 ASD 地面光谱仪观测，覆盖 Syr Darya 河流域 Galaba 和 Kuva 灌溉农田中的小麦、水稻、玉米、苜蓿和棉花等主导作物。数据以 Excel 光谱库和文档/代码形式发布，适合作物识别、农业物候、高光谱窄波段分析和跨区域作物光谱迁移研究。
desc_en: GHISACASIA is the Central Asia member of the GHISA hyperspectral agricultural-crop spectral-library series, published through NASA LP DAAC and USGS WGSC. It is derived from 2007 EO-1 Hyperion imagery and 2006-2007 ASD field spectroradiometer observations over the Galaba and Kuva irrigated farm fields in the Syr Darya river basin, covering dominant crops such as wheat, rice, corn, alfalfa and cotton. The product is distributed as Excel spectral-library files with documentation and SAS processing code, supporting crop identification, phenology, hyperspectral narrowband analysis and cross-region crop spectral-transfer research.
openness: NASA Earthdata page states the dataset is openly shared without restriction according to EOSDIS data use and citation guidance; data.gov marks public access level public and license as government works.
source_type: spectral library derived from satellite hyperspectral imagery and field spectroscopy
evidence: NASA Earthdata catalog lists DOI 10.5067/Community/GHISA/GHISACASIA.001, CMR concept C2763264649-LPCLOUD, Excel format, 1.7 MB and 3.3 MB dataset size, EO-1 and field-survey platforms, Hyperion and field spectroradiometer instruments, two granules, and a 2006-04-30 to 2007-08-28 temporal extent. Data.gov and CMR state the library is extracted from three Hyperion images plus several thousand ASD spectra, with 1,232 sample points and .xlsx files containing crop labels and acquisition metadata.
scale: regional crop spectral library
feature_count: 2 granules; 1,232 field sample points; extracted from three EO-1 Hyperion images plus several thousand ASD spectra
file_size: 1.7 MB and 3.3 MB according to Earthdata catalog

name: HYEP Toulouse Urban Area 2015 HySpex Hyperspectral Research Products
provider: ANR HYEP project / CNRS UMR TETIS / ONERA / Zenodo
theme_canonical: urban airborne hyperspectral research products
theme_raw: HySpex Toulouse urban area 2015, HYPXIM simulated products and field samples
coverage: Toulouse urban area, France
license: Zenodo open dataset; see record terms
formats: 7z archives; ENVI standard hyperspectral image; ESRI Shapefile samples; simulated HYPXIM and Sentinel-2 products
access: Zenodo record with direct file downloads
time_span: July 2015 acquisition campaign; Zenodo record published 2022-03-09
notes: Independent from the later Toulouse Hyperspectral Data Set entry: this Zenodo record is an ANR HYEP 2015 research-products package containing a full HySpex VNIR-SWIR urban image and simulated HYPXIM/Sentinel-2 products, plus tree field samples and multiple project publication links.
desc_zh: HYEP Toulouse Urban Area 2015 是法国 ANR HYEP 项目的城市高光谱研究产品包，基于 2015 年 7 月 Toulouse 城市区域 HySpex 航空采集数据生成。Zenodo 记录提供 HySPex-2m 全 VNIR-SWIR ENVI 标准影像、HYPXIM 4 m/8 m 模拟高光谱产品、Sentinel-2 模拟/配套产品和 600 株城市树木样本结果。它适合城市地物分类、树种识别、空间/光谱降尺度、传感器模拟和多源融合研究。
desc_en: HYEP Toulouse Urban Area 2015 is an urban hyperspectral research-products package from the French ANR HYEP project, derived from a July 2015 HySpex airborne acquisition over Toulouse, France. The Zenodo record provides a full HySPex-2m VNIR-SWIR ENVI-standard image, HYPXIM 4 m and 8 m simulated hyperspectral products, a Sentinel-2 companion product, and field-sample products for 600 urban trees. It supports urban land-cover classification, tree-species mapping, spatial/spectral degradation studies, sensor simulation and multimodal fusion research.
openness: Zenodo record is marked Dataset Open and provides downloadable files with DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3611240
source_type: airborne hyperspectral urban research-products dataset
evidence: Zenodo record 3611240 states the HYEP project produced five research outputs from a July 2015 Toulouse HySpex acquisition with 408 spectral bands from 0.4-2.5 micrometers and about 2 m spatial resolution; files total 981 MB and include HySPex-2m.7z (855.2 MB), HYPXIM-4m.7z, HYPXIM-8m.7z, Sentinel2-10m.7z and Fields_samples.7z, with DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3611240.
scale: urban airborne hyperspectral image and derived products
feature_count: 408 spectral bands; HySPex-2m archive plus HYPXIM 4 m/8 m simulated products and 600 urban tree samples
file_size: 981.0 MB total; HySPex-2m.7z 855.2 MB according to Zenodo

name: AisaDUAL Airborne Spectrometer Boreal Snow-Covered Landscape Dataset
provider: Finnish Environment Institute / Finnish Meteorological Institute / Zenodo
theme_canonical: airborne hyperspectral boreal snow reflectance
theme_raw: AisaDUAL boreal snow-covered landscape March 2010
coverage: Sodankylä, northern Finland
formats: GeoTIFF reflectance mosaics and PNG preview
access: Zenodo record with direct file downloads
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.3580451; concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3048419; earlier version DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3048420; related ESSD paper DOI 10.5194/essd-12-719-2020
notes: Companion to the already cataloged 2011 AisaDUAL spring snow melt dataset, but this is a separate 2010 snow-covered boreal landscape record with its own DOI and files. It focuses on forest-canopy effects on optical signals for snow-covered surfaces. Phase401 concept-level dedup: retained latest Zenodo record 3580451 for concept 3048419; removed older version record 3048420 and preserved its DOI here.
desc_zh: 该 AisaDUAL 数据集提供芬兰 Sodankylä 北方森林雪覆盖景观的航空高光谱反射率影像，用于研究森林冠层对雪覆盖地表光学遥感信号的影响。数据由 AisaDUAL 成像光谱仪在 2010 年 3 月 18 日和 21 日采集，原始空间分辨率约 80 cm，重采样为 10 m 像元，并发布 555、645、859 和 1640 nm 等选定波段的 GeoTIFF 镶嵌图。它是 ESSD 2020 北方春季多尺度光谱反射率数据论文中的独立航空数据 DOI。
desc_en: This AisaDUAL dataset provides airborne hyperspectral reflectance mosaics over snow-covered boreal forest near Sodankylä, northern Finland, for studying forest-canopy effects on optical remote-sensing signals over snow. The imagery was acquired with an AisaDUAL imaging spectrometer on 18 and 21 March 2010, with original 80 cm spatial resolution resampled to 10 m pixels, and distributed as selected-band GeoTIFF mosaics at 555, 645, 859 and 1640 nm. It is an independent airborne-data DOI from the ESSD 2020 boreal spring spectral-reflectance data collection.
openness: Zenodo record is CC BY 4.0 and provides direct downloadable GeoTIFF files
source_type: airborne hyperspectral reflectance dataset
evidence: Zenodo API metadata for record 3580451 states the dataset contains 10 m reflectance data from a boreal snow-covered landscape, acquired with an AisaDUAL imaging spectrometer on 2010-03-18 and 2010-03-21 in Sodankylä, Finland, with DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3580451 and CC BY 4.0 license. The files include GeoTIFF mosaics for 555, 645, 859 and 1640 nm bands plus a PNG preview. ESSD 2020 lists this DOI as one of the airborne datasets in the boreal spring reflectance collection. Zenodo API check 2026-06-12 shows records 3048419/3048420/3580451 share conceptrecid 3048419; 3580451 is the latest concept landing record with 9 files.
scale: selected-band airborne hyperspectral reflectance mosaics
feature_count: selected AisaDUAL mosaics at 555, 645, 859 and 1640 nm for two March 2010 acquisition days
file_size: about 19 MB across listed Zenodo files

name: EO4CerealStress EnMAP and Sentinel-2 Wheat Lodging Detection Maps
provider: DANS Data Station Physical and Technical Sciences / University of Twente
theme_canonical: EnMAP-derived wheat lodging raster prediction maps
theme_raw: EO4CerealStress biophysical indicators from EnMAP and Sentinel-2
coverage: Italy wheat study area, EO4CS_IT_2023 file set
formats: GeoTIFF raster prediction maps
access: DANS Data Station dataset page and DOI; downloadable ZIP and individual GeoTIFF files
notes: Boundary-style record: this is not raw EnMAP hyperspectral imagery. It is a set of georeferenced raster prediction maps derived from EnMAP hyperspectral imagery and Sentinel-2 using multiple regression approaches for wheat lodging detection.
desc_zh: EO4CerealStress 数据集提供基于 EnMAP 高光谱影像和 Sentinel-2 多光谱影像生成的小麦倒伏检测栅格预测图。DANS/Dataverse 元数据说明，产品使用梯度提升、随机森林、多层感知机、PLSR、PCA 相关回归等多种模型生成，并以 UTM 投影 GeoTIFF 发布，保留 NaN 作为无数据区域。该记录不是原始高光谱影像，而是开放机构发布的 EnMAP 派生农业遥感高光谱产品，适合小麦倒伏制图、模型对比和高光谱/多光谱农业指标研究。
desc_en: EO4CerealStress provides wheat-lodging detection raster prediction maps derived from EnMAP hyperspectral imagery and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery. The DANS/Dataverse metadata states that the products were generated with multiple regression approaches, including gradient boosting, random forest, multilayer perceptron, PLSR and PCA-based regressors, and are distributed as projected GeoTIFFs with masked no-data areas preserved as NaN. This is not raw hyperspectral imagery, but it is an open EnMAP-derived agricultural hyperspectral remote-sensing product useful for lodging mapping, model comparison and hyperspectral/multispectral crop-indicator studies.
openness: DANS dataset with DOI 10.17026/PT/TFYJBN and CC-BY-ND-4.0 license; dataset page offers ZIP and individual file access.
source_type: derived EnMAP hyperspectral raster prediction maps
evidence: DANS/Dataverse API for DOI 10.17026/PT/TFYJBN reports title EO4CerealStress Biophysical Indicators: Wheat lodging detection maps from EnMAP and Sentinel-2, CC-BY-ND-4.0 license, deposited 2026-03-05, and description stating georeferenced raster prediction maps derived from EnMAP hyperspectral imagery and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery. The API lists 14 image/tiff files, including seven EnMAP-based lodging score maps for GBR, GPR, MLP, MLR, PLSR, RFR and SVR regressors.
scale: 14 GeoTIFF prediction maps, including 7 EnMAP-derived maps and 7 Sentinel-2-derived maps
feature_count: 14 GeoTIFF files; 7 EnMAP-based wheat lodging score maps
file_size: about 4.0 MB ZIP according to DANS page; individual EnMAP GeoTIFFs about 183.7 KB each

name: NeonTreeEvaluation Benchmark RGB Hyperspectral LiDAR and CHM Dataset
provider: Weecology / University of Florida / NEON / Zenodo
theme_canonical: NEON multimodal canopy crown detection benchmark with hyperspectral tiles
theme_raw: NeonTreeEvaluation benchmark training and evaluation data
coverage: Multiple NEON sites across the United States
formats: ZIP archives with RGB, Hyperspectral, LiDAR and CHM folders; annotations ZIP
access: Zenodo record with direct training/evaluation/annotation downloads
time_span: NEON AOP sensor data mainly 2018-2019 according to related paper; Zenodo record published 2022
notes: Task-focused benchmark, not a new sensor acquisition. It is retained because the standalone Zenodo archive packages aligned NEON RGB, hyperspectral, LiDAR and CHM data plus crown annotations for individual-tree detection and delineation evaluation.
desc_zh: NeonTreeEvaluation Benchmark 是基于 NEON AOP 航空遥感产品构建的个体树冠检测与勾绘评测数据包。Zenodo 记录说明，每个地理站点最多包含 RGB 图像、LiDAR tile、426 波段高光谱文件和 1 m 冠层高度文件，training.zip 和 evaluation.zip 均包含 RGB、Hyperspectral、LiDAR 与 CHM 文件夹，并另有 annotation.zip 标注。它适合评估树冠检测、树冠分割、多模态融合和高光谱辅助个体树识别算法。
desc_en: The NeonTreeEvaluation Benchmark is an individual-tree crown detection and delineation benchmark built from NEON AOP airborne remote-sensing products. The Zenodo record states that each geographic site can include an RGB image, a LiDAR tile, a 426-band hyperspectral file and a 1 m canopy-height file; training.zip and evaluation.zip share RGB, Hyperspectral, LiDAR and CHM folders, with annotations in a separate archive. It supports crown detection, crown segmentation, multimodal fusion and hyperspectral-assisted individual-tree recognition evaluation.
openness: Zenodo dataset DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5914554 with CC BY 4.0 license and direct ZIP downloads.
source_type: task benchmark with NEON hyperspectral remote-sensing tiles
evidence: Zenodo API for record 5914554 reports title Data for the NeonTreeEvaluation Benchmark, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5914554, CC BY 4.0 license, and files training.zip, evaluation.zip and annotations.zip. The description states the benchmark includes RGB images, LiDAR tiles, 426-band hyperspectral files and 1 m canopy-height files, with RGB/Hyperspectral/LiDAR/CHM folders in both training and evaluation archives. The related PLOS Computational Biology paper describes co-registered NEON RGB, LiDAR and hyperspectral imagery with over 6,000 image-annotated crowns and additional field annotations.
scale: training/evaluation benchmark across multiple NEON sites
feature_count: training.zip, evaluation.zip and annotations.zip; related paper reports over 6,000 image-annotated crowns plus field annotations and stem points
file_size: training.zip about 4.50 GB; evaluation.zip about 3.85 GB; annotations.zip about 0.56 MB according to Zenodo API

name: NEON Tree Species Predictions from Hyperspectral RGB LiDAR Canopy Data
provider: Weecology / University of Florida / NEON / Zenodo
theme_canonical: NEON HSI-derived individual canopy tree species prediction maps
theme_raw: NEON Tree Species Predictions for 100 million trees
coverage: 24 NEON sites across the United States according to PLOS Biology paper
formats: Site-level ZIP archives with individual tree prediction products and shapefiles/crops
access: Zenodo record with direct site ZIP downloads
time_span: NEON AOP and field data used in 2024 PLOS Biology species-map release
notes: Derived prediction dataset, not raw hyperspectral imagery. Retained because the PLOS Biology data-availability and methods state that the workflow used NEON hyperspectral surface reflectance (DP1.30006.001) together with RGB, CHM and field vegetation data to predict individual canopy tree species over 100 million trees. v230 evidence update: Zenodo metadata also states that crops.zip contains pre-cropped training files, including 369-band hyperspectral numpy arrays and RGB .tif crops, with annotations.csv listing 30,042 individuals; this strengthens the row as an accessible hyperspectral training-data package as well as a derived prediction product.
desc_zh: NEON Tree Species Predictions 是面向 NEON 站点的个体树种预测与训练数据集，发布了超过一亿棵冠层树的树种、冠层位置、冠幅、高度等派生产品，并提供训练裁剪数据。Zenodo 描述说明 `crops.zip` 包含预裁剪文件，其中高光谱文件是 369 个波段的 numpy 数组，RGB 裁剪为 `.tif`，`annotations.csv` 含 30,042 个个体；论文工作流还使用 NEON RGB、冠层高度模型、高光谱表面反射率和植被结构数据。该条目既是高光谱参与生成的大规模生态遥感派生产品，也是可下载的 NEON 个体树高光谱训练数据入口。
desc_en: NEON Tree Species Predictions provides individual canopy-tree species predictions and training data across NEON sites, including species, crown location, crown area and height for more than 100 million trees. The Zenodo description states that `crops.zip` contains pre-cropped training files, with 369-band hyperspectral numpy arrays, RGB `.tif` crops and an `annotations.csv` file covering 30,042 individuals; the associated workflow also used NEON RGB imagery, canopy-height models, hyperspectral surface reflectance and vegetation-structure data. This row is therefore both a hyperspectral-informed ecological derivative and an accessible NEON individual-tree hyperspectral training-data package.
openness: Zenodo dataset DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10926344 with CC0 license and site ZIP downloads.
source_type: derived NEON hyperspectral-informed tree species prediction dataset
evidence: Zenodo API for record 10926344 reports title NEON Tree Species Predictions, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10926344, CC0 license, 24 files totaling about 9.76 GiB, and site ZIP files plus crops.zip. The record description states crops.zip contains pre-cropped training files, 369-band hyperspectral numpy arrays, RGB .tif crops, train-test split CSVs, annotations.csv with 30,042 individuals, and image_path fields readable by numpy.load. The PLOS Biology paper data availability points to this Zenodo record and its methods state that the workflow used NEON hyperspectral surface reflectance with RGB, canopy height and vegetation structure data.
scale: 100 million individual tree predictions across 24 NEON sites
feature_count: predictions for over 100 million individual trees and 81 canopy tree species; training crops include 30,042 annotated individuals with 369-band HSI numpy arrays and RGB crops
file_size: Zenodo API phase237: 24 files totaling 10,476,235,108 bytes (about 9.76 GiB); crops.zip 2,051,291,440 bytes

name: Berlin as Seen by EnMAP HYPERedu L1B Hyperspectral Dataset
provider: DLR / ESA / GFZ Data Services / HYPERedu
theme_canonical: spaceborne EnMAP L1B hyperspectral preprocessing tutorial dataset
theme_raw: Berlin as seen by EnMAP HYPERedu MOOC dataset
coverage: Berlin, Germany and surrounding areas
license: GFZ Data Services dataset; EnMAP data license applies, companion DEM/Sentinel-2 have separate licenses
formats: BSQ VNIR/SWIR files, XML history and metadata, TIF quality masks, DEM and Sentinel-2 reference data
access: GFZ Data Services DOI landing page
notes: Open educational EnMAP dataset for HYPERedu preprocessing exercises. The GFZ page states companion DEM and Sentinel-2 reference data are included but not under the same license as the EnMAP data.
desc_zh: Berlin as Seen by EnMAP 是 HYPERedu MOOC 发布的真实星载 EnMAP 高光谱预处理教学数据，覆盖德国 Berlin 及周边地区，采集日期为 2022 年 7 月 24 日。GFZ Data Services 页面说明数据为 Level 1B，分别提供 VNIR 和 SWIR 传感器的 BSQ 文件，并包含历史/元数据 XML、cirrus、classes、cloud、cloud shadow、haze、snow 等质量 mask、质量测试标志和像元 mask。它适合 EnMAP 预处理、几何/大气校正、质量 mask 处理和教学复现实验。
desc_en: Berlin as Seen by EnMAP is a real spaceborne EnMAP hyperspectral preprocessing dataset released for the HYPERedu MOOC, covering Berlin, Germany and surrounding areas on 24 July 2022. The GFZ Data Services page states that the product is Level 1B, with separate BSQ files for the VNIR and SWIR sensors, XML history and metadata, quality masks for cirrus, classes, cloud, cloud shadow, haze and snow, plus quality-test flags and pixel masks. It supports EnMAP preprocessing, geometric and atmospheric correction, quality-mask handling and reproducible teaching exercises.
openness: GFZ Data Services DOI landing page states the dataset is publicly available as part of the HYPERedu MOOC; companion DEM/Sentinel-2 reference data have separate licenses.
source_type: spaceborne EnMAP hyperspectral L1B tutorial dataset
evidence: GFZ Data Services page for DOI 10.5880/enmap.2023.001 states the dataset contains a spaceborne hyperspectral image acquired by EnMAP over Berlin and surroundings on 2022-07-24, preprocessed to Level 1B format, provided as separate VNIR and SWIR BSQ files, accompanied by history and metadata XML, six quality masks in TIF format, quality-test flags and pixel masks, plus DEM and Sentinel-2 reference data for EnPT preprocessing.
scale: single EnMAP Berlin L1B scene with companion reference data
feature_count: VNIR and SWIR BSQ files plus six quality mask classes and ancillary/reference files

name: Three Annotated Anomaly Detection Datasets for Line-Scan Algorithms
provider: HyperAD authors / Zenodo
theme_canonical: hyperspectral and multispectral anomaly-detection benchmark
theme_raw: Beach HSI, synthetic AVIRIS HSI and SNP SentinelHub MSI anomaly masks
coverage: Beach scene, synthetic AVIRIS-derived scene and SentinelHub SNP scene; mixed benchmark coverage
formats: NumPy .npy image arrays, binary masks, PNG previews
access: Zenodo record with direct file downloads
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.13370800; related arXiv ERX anomaly detection paper
notes: Task-focused anomaly-detection benchmark rather than a single raw remote-sensing archive. It is included because two of the three image datasets are hyperspectral, one synthetic dataset is derived from AVIRIS, and the record provides annotated pixel masks for anomaly detection evaluation.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供三个带像素级异常掩膜的线扫异常检测 benchmark，其中包含两个高光谱数据和一个多光谱数据。元数据说明数据以 NumPy .npy 格式发布，影像数组维度为 height、width、channels，并提供对应二值 mask；Synthetic 数据来自 AVIRIS，SNP 数据由 SentinelHub 收集，Beach 数据提供 HSI 文件与 mask。它适合高光谱异常检测、实时线扫算法、mask 评估和 ERX/HyperAD 相关算法复现实验。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides three annotated anomaly-detection benchmark images for line-scan algorithms, including two hyperspectral datasets and one multispectral dataset. The metadata states that data are distributed as NumPy .npy arrays with image dimensions ordered as height, width and channels, with corresponding binary pixel masks; the synthetic dataset was collected from AVIRIS, the SNP dataset from SentinelHub, and the Beach dataset includes HSI data and masks. It supports hyperspectral anomaly detection, real-time line-scanning methods, mask-based evaluation and ERX/HyperAD reproducibility.
openness: Zenodo dataset DOI 10.5281/zenodo.13370800 with CC BY 4.0 license and direct file downloads.
source_type: task benchmark with hyperspectral anomaly-detection arrays and masks
evidence: Zenodo API for record 13370800 reports title Three Annotated Anomaly Detection Datasets for Line-Scan Algorithms, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.13370800, CC BY 4.0 license, two hyperspectral and one multispectral anomaly detection images with binary masks, .npy format, Synthetic data from AVIRIS, SNP data from SentinelHub, and files including beach_hsi.npy (about 599.9 MB), synthetic_hsi.npy (about 1.04 GB), snp_msi.npy, mask .npy files and PNG previews.
scale: three annotated anomaly-detection images
feature_count: two hyperspectral image arrays, one multispectral image array and associated binary masks
file_size: beach_hsi.npy about 599.9 MB; synthetic_hsi.npy about 1.04 GB; snp_msi.npy about 72.5 MB

name: GEWDiff EnMAP Champion and MDAS Hyperspectral Super-Resolution Dataset
provider: GEWDiff authors / Hugging Face
theme_canonical: hyperspectral super-resolution and restoration benchmark
theme_raw: GEWDiff LQ-GT pairs derived from EnMAP Champion and MDAS
coverage: Derived from EnMAP Champion and MDAS hyperspectral datasets; includes MDAS test subsets and WDC subset according to dataset card
formats: GeoTIFF LQ/GT images, NumPy masks and edges, checkpoint file
access: Hugging Face dataset repository with direct file access
time_span: Hugging Face dataset checked 2026-06-05; paper preprint 2025
notes: Derived/task dataset, not a new airborne or satellite acquisition. It is retained because it packages preprocessed hyperspectral LQ/GT pairs from EnMAP Champion and MDAS for super-resolution, restoration and generative remote-sensing experiments.
desc_zh: GEWDiff Training & Evaluation Dataset 是面向高光谱图像超分辨率、增强、修复和生成式遥感任务的开放数据包。Hugging Face 数据卡说明，数据由 EnMAP Champion 和 MDAS 高光谱数据派生，经过空间切片、光谱波段统一和光谱响应函数近邻近似匹配，提供低质量/低分辨率 LQ 图像、对应高质量 GT 图像，以及部分 mask 和 edge 结构信息。它不是新的原始传感器采集，但适合高光谱遥感超分、复原和扩散模型评测。
desc_en: The GEWDiff Training & Evaluation Dataset is an open benchmark for hyperspectral image super-resolution, enhancement, restoration and generative remote-sensing tasks. The Hugging Face dataset card states that it is derived from the EnMAP Champion and MDAS hyperspectral datasets, with spatial tiling, spectral band unification and nearest-neighbor spectral-response harmonization, providing LQ low-resolution inputs, GT high-quality targets, and partial masks/edges. It is not a new raw acquisition, but it is useful for hyperspectral remote-sensing super-resolution, restoration and diffusion-model evaluation.
openness: Hugging Face dataset is public with CC BY 4.0 license and file-level access.
source_type: derived hyperspectral super-resolution benchmark
evidence: Hugging Face API for zhang5566/GEWDiff_training_dataset reports public dataset, CC BY 4.0 license, 24,568 repository siblings, tags including super-resolution, hyperspectral and remote_sensing, and files such as test_mdas1/gt/*.tif, test_mdas1/lq/*.tif, masks and edge .npy files. The README states the dataset is derived from EnMAP Champion and MDAS hyperspectral datasets and contains LQ/GT pairs for super-resolution, restoration and generative remote-sensing tasks.
scale: 1K-10K samples according to Hugging Face size category; 24,568 repository file entries in API
feature_count: paired LQ/GT hyperspectral tiles with optional masks and edges
file_size: not fully summed; Hugging Face repository contains many GeoTIFF and NumPy files plus epoch_200.pth

name: BTC-Net V2 AVIRIS Hyperspectral Compression Patch Dataset
provider: BTC-Net V2 authors / Hugging Face
theme_canonical: AVIRIS hyperspectral image compression and reconstruction benchmark
theme_raw: BTC-Net V2 processed AVIRIS HSI patches
coverage: AVIRIS-derived HSI patches; exact scene coverage not specified in checked dataset card
formats: ZIP chunks containing MATLAB .mat hyperspectral patch files
access: Hugging Face dataset repository with direct ZIP chunks
doi_or_citation: BTC-Net V2 dataset card and linked GitHub repository
notes: Task-focused compression dataset, not a new AVIRIS archive. Included because the dataset card explicitly states the samples are processed hyperspectral image data from the AVIRIS sensor, distributed as 1,195 HSI patches/sub-volumes for reconstruction and compression experiments.
desc_zh: BTC-Net V2 Dataset 是面向高光谱图像压缩、空间优先分层重建和深度学习传输研究的 AVIRIS 派生 patch 数据集。Hugging Face 数据卡说明，数据包含 1,195 个处理后的 HSI patch/sub-volume，以 MATLAB .mat 文件形式打包在 12 个 zip 分块中，总大小约 8.82 GB。它不是新的 AVIRIS 原始影像档案，但对高光谱压缩、重建、带宽受限传输和算法复现实验有直接价值。
desc_en: BTC-Net V2 is an AVIRIS-derived hyperspectral patch dataset for hyperspectral image compression, spatial-priority hierarchical reconstruction and deep-learning-based transmission research. The Hugging Face dataset card states that it contains 1,195 processed HSI patches or sub-volumes, stored as MATLAB .mat files across 12 zip chunks with a total size of about 8.82 GB. It is not a new raw AVIRIS archive, but it is directly useful for hyperspectral compression, reconstruction, bandwidth-constrained transmission and reproducible algorithm experiments.
openness: Hugging Face dataset is public with MIT license and direct zip chunk downloads.
source_type: processed AVIRIS hyperspectral compression benchmark
evidence: Hugging Face API for the-bad-one/BTC-Net-V2 reports public dataset, MIT license, image-to-image/other tasks, tags including hyperspectral-imaging, hsi-compression and image-reconstruction, and siblings aviris_mat_chunk_01.zip through aviris_mat_chunk_12.zip. The README states the dataset consists of processed HSI data specifically from the AVIRIS sensor, with 1,195 processed HSI patches/sub-volumes in MATLAB .mat files and total file size about 8.82 GB.
scale: 1,195 processed HSI patches/sub-volumes
feature_count: 12 zip chunks with about 100 MATLAB .mat files per chunk
file_size: about 8.82 GB according to Hugging Face dataset card

name: UAS Table Beet Multimodal Hyperspectral Yield Dataset
provider: Rochester Institute of Technology / Cornell University / Mendeley Data
theme_canonical: UAS hyperspectral, multispectral, LiDAR and yield data for table beet precision agriculture
theme_raw: Data for advancing harvest table beet root yield estimation via UAS multi-modal sensing
coverage: Table beet field plots in 2021 and 2022 growing seasons; 2022 includes three plot locations/treatments
formats: Mendeley Data files with UAS hyperspectral imagery, five-band multispectral imagery, canopy height models, harvest/ground-truth tables and meteorological data
access: Mendeley Data repository with open dataset DOI and public landing page
notes: Added from 2-hop chain: ScienceDirect article data availability points to Mendeley dataset d9d5h5xbr5/1; DataCite confirms open dataset metadata, CC BY 4.0 rights and remote-sensing/UAV subjects. Included because the dataset is field/UAS remote sensing and explicitly contains high-resolution hyperspectral imagery, not a laboratory-only proximal imaging collection.
desc_zh: 该 Mendeley Data 数据集记录了 2021 和 2022 年桌甜菜田间小区的 UAS 多模态遥感观测，用于根产量估计与精细农业建模。DataCite 元数据和论文数据可用性说明均确认数据包含高分辨率高光谱影像、五波段多光谱影像、LiDAR/摄影测量冠层高度模型、收获实测和气象数据。它适合用于作物产量预测、传感器对比、多源融合和机器学习回归实验。
desc_en: This Mendeley Data record provides UAS multimodal remote-sensing observations of table beet field plots across the 2021 and 2022 growing seasons for root-yield estimation. The DataCite metadata and linked article data-availability statement confirm high-resolution hyperspectral imagery, five-band multispectral imagery, LiDAR/photogrammetric canopy-height models, harvest ground truth and meteorological data. It is useful for crop-yield prediction, sensor comparison, multimodal fusion and machine-learning regression experiments.
openness: DataCite reports open access and Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International rights for DOI 10.17632/d9d5h5xbr5.1.
source_type: UAS agricultural hyperspectral and multimodal field dataset
evidence: Web search found the 2026 ISPRS JPRS article data-availability statement pointing to https://data.mendeley.com/datasets/d9d5h5xbr5/1. DataCite API for DOI 10.17632/d9d5h5xbr5.1 reports title, Mendeley Data publisher, 2025 publication year, CC BY 4.0 rights, subjects including Remote Sensing and Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, and a description listing high-resolution hyperspectral imagery, five-band multispectral imagery, CHMs, yield measurements and meteorological data.
scale: Multiple UAS acquisitions over 2021 and 2022 table beet growing seasons
feature_count: High-resolution hyperspectral imagery plus multispectral imagery, CHM products, harvest measurements and meteorological context; exact file count not exposed by DataCite
file_size: not reported in checked DataCite metadata

name: Beech Bark Disease Airborne Hyperspectral Crown Dataset
provider: Université de Sherbrooke / Université de Montréal / Dryad
theme_canonical: Airborne and leaf-level hyperspectral data for beech bark disease assessment
theme_raw: Data from: Hyperspectral imaging has a limited ability to remotely sense the onset of beech bark disease
coverage: Mont-Saint-Bruno National Park, Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, Canada
formats: CSV hyperspectral band tables, GeoPackage crown geometries, disease assessment tables and metadata
access: Dryad public dataset landing page and API download endpoint
doi_or_citation: Dataset DOI 10.5061/dryad.jq2bvq8js; primary article DOI 10.1002/rse2.70013; preprint DOI 10.1101/2024.09.20.614150
time_span: Airborne data collected September 2018; Dryad dataset published 2024
notes: Dryad distributes processed canopy-level and leaf-level hyperspectral tables plus crown geometries and disease assessments; complete CABO raw hyperspectral imagery for the whole park is noted as available on request, so this row describes the open derived research dataset rather than a full raw-flight archive.
desc_zh: 该 Dryad 数据集来自加拿大魁北克 Mont-Saint-Bruno 国家公园的山毛榉树冠病害遥感研究，包含航空高光谱与叶片级高光谱数据。公开元数据说明航空数据覆盖可见光、近红外和短波红外共 344 个波段，并与 126 株山毛榉树冠、GeoPackage 冠层几何和 160 株树的病害评估数据关联。它适合用于森林病害遥感、冠层光谱特征分析和生态高光谱建模，但完整公园级 CABO 原始影像需要另行请求。
desc_en: This Dryad dataset supports a beech bark disease remote-sensing study in Mont-Saint-Bruno National Park, Quebec, Canada. The public metadata describe airborne hyperspectral data spanning 344 visible, near-infrared and shortwave-infrared wavelengths, linked to 126 beech crowns, GeoPackage crown geometries and disease assessments for 160 trees, together with leaf-level spectra for selected trees. It is useful for forest-pathogen remote sensing, crown-level spectral analysis and ecological hyperspectral modeling, while the complete park-wide CABO raw imagery remains request-based.
openness: Dryad API reports public visibility and CC0 1.0 license for DOI 10.5061/dryad.jq2bvq8js.
source_type: processed airborne hyperspectral crown/leaf spectral dataset with geospatial geometries
evidence: Dryad API for DOI 10.5061/dryad.jq2bvq8js reports public dataset, CC0 license, storage size 2,422,202 bytes, methods linking CABO airborne hyperspectral data collected in September 2018 at 2 m resolution, 344 wavelengths across VIS/NIR/SWIR, 126 canopy-level samples, 37 leaf-level samples, 160 disease assessments, beech_crown_geometries.gpkg with EPSG:32618, related preprint DOI 10.1101/2024.09.20.614150 and primary article DOI 10.1002/rse2.70013.
scale: 126 canopy-level airborne hyperspectral samples, 37 leaf-level spectral samples and 160 disease assessment records
feature_count: Four open dataset sections: canopy hyperspectral band table, crown geometries, leaf-level hyperspectral bands and beech bark disease assessment table

name: Potsdam Soil Moisture Observatory Airborne Hyperspectral Imagery
provider: University of Potsdam / UFZ / EUDAT B2Share / ESSD authors
theme_canonical: Airborne CASI/SASI hyperspectral surface-reflectance cube for soil-moisture observatory research
theme_raw: The Potsdam Soil Moisture Observatory: High-coverage reference observations at kilometer scale
coverage: Potsdam Soil Moisture Observatory (PoSMO), Potsdam region, Germany; UTM Zone 33N / ETRS89
formats: ZIP archives containing airborne hyperspectral imagery products; final hyperspectral cube described as Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF; companion ground hyperspectral reflectance archive
access: EUDAT B2Share public record with public files and DOI
time_span: Two-year observatory record; airborne hyperspectral survey acquired 2024-09-05; dataset published 2025-12-12; ESSD article 2026
notes: Added through a 3-hop data-availability chain from the ESSD article to B2Share DOI/API and back to the paper technical section. This is a broad hydrology observatory archive; the row is scoped specifically to its open airborne hyperspectral imagery subset and companion hyperspectral reflectance data, not to CRNS, LiDAR, groundwater, ERT or other non-HSI components.
desc_zh: Potsdam Soil Moisture Observatory 数据档案是一个面向土壤水分与水文观测的综合开放数据集，其中包含直接可下载的航空高光谱影像子集。ESSD 论文第 3.8 节说明，2024 年 9 月 5 日航空调查使用 Cessna 208B 搭载 CASI-1500 与 SASI-600 推扫成像光谱仪，覆盖 VNIR 380-1050 nm 与 SWIR 950-2450 nm，经辐射、几何、大气校正和 VNIR/SWIR 配准后生成 Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF 高光谱 cube。B2Share API 确认记录和文件公开，包含约 4.90 GB 的 `3_8_hyperspectral_airborne_imagery.zip` 以及约 5.30 GB 的地面高光谱反射率 archive。
desc_en: The Potsdam Soil Moisture Observatory archive is a comprehensive open hydrology and soil-moisture dataset that includes a directly downloadable airborne hyperspectral imagery subset. Section 3.8 of the ESSD paper states that a 5 September 2024 airborne campaign used CASI-1500 and SASI-600 pushbroom imaging spectrometers on a Cessna 208B, covering VNIR 380-1050 nm and SWIR 950-2450 nm, then applying radiometric, geometric, atmospheric and VNIR/SWIR co-registration steps to generate a Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF hyperspectral cube. The B2Share API confirms public record and file access, including the 4.90 GB `3_8_hyperspectral_airborne_imagery.zip` and a 5.30 GB ground hyperspectral reflectance archive.
openness: B2Share API reports public record access, public file access, inactive embargo and CC BY 4.0 rights.
source_type: airborne hyperspectral COG image-cube subset inside hydrology observatory archive
evidence: ESSD article data-availability section links the archive DOI https://doi.org/10.23728/b2share.bxamy-4zh85. B2Share API for record bxamy-4zh85 reports title, CC BY 4.0 rights, public record/files access, subjects including remote sensing, imaging spectroscopy, surface reflectance and hyperspectral image cube. Files API lists 17 files including 3_8_hyperspectral_airborne_imagery.zip (4,902,316,780 bytes) and 3_7_hyperspectral_reflectance.zip (5,296,567,048 bytes). ESSD Sect. 3.8 describes CASI-1500 VNIR 380-1050 nm and SASI-600 SWIR 950-2450 nm airborne acquisition, native 25 cm/63 cm resolutions, 15 CASI bands and 100 SASI bands, ATCOR-4 correction, AROSICS co-registration, SWIR resampling and COG output.
scale: two flight lines over PoSMO; airborne hyperspectral COG generated from CASI-1500 and SASI-600 imagery
feature_count: B2Share files include 3_8_hyperspectral_airborne_imagery.zip plus 3_7_hyperspectral_reflectance.zip among 17 archive components
file_size: 4,902,316,780 bytes for airborne hyperspectral imagery zip; 5,296,567,048 bytes for ground hyperspectral reflectance zip

name: PANGAEA MOSAiC ROV Under-Ice Spectral Solar Radiation Series V2
provider: Alfred Wegener Institute / MOSAiC expedition / PANGAEA
theme_canonical: ROV-based hyperspectral solar irradiance and radiance over and under Arctic sea ice
theme_raw: Spectral solar radiation over and under sea ice during the MOSAiC expedition 2019/20, version 2.0
coverage: Central Arctic drifting MOSAiC expedition sea-ice floe and ROV survey stations
formats: PANGAEA tab-delimited datasets with spectral radiance/irradiance, ROV position, depth and survey metadata
access: PANGAEA dataset publication series with downloadable child datasets
time_span: MOSAiC expedition observations from 2019-11 to 2020-09; version 2.0 published 2025-06-10
notes: Added as a series-level record rather than hundreds of individual station/survey DOI rows. The PANGAEA series describes RAMSES hyperspectral radiometers on a remotely operated vehicle under drifting sea ice plus an incident irradiance reference sensor on sea ice. It is a geolocated hyperspectral radiometry dataset for sea-ice/ocean optical remote sensing, not an image cube; included consistently with existing water-colour and sea-ice hyperspectral radiometry records.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 系列数据集汇总了 MOSAiC 2019/20 北极漂流海冰考察期间，ROV 搭载 TriOS RAMSES 高光谱辐射计在海冰上方和下方测得的太阳辐照度与辐亮度观测。系列说明数据按完整光谱分辨率并插值到 1 nm，覆盖 320-950 nm，同时给出 PAR 和全波段积分量，并包含 ROV 声学定位与深度信息。它不是二维影像 cube，但对海冰透光性、水下光场、海冰-海洋光学过程和卫星/航空水色遥感验证具有价值。
desc_en: This PANGAEA series compiles solar irradiance and radiance observations measured over and under Arctic drifting sea ice during the MOSAiC 2019/20 expedition using TriOS RAMSES hyperspectral radiometers mounted on a remotely operated vehicle and a sea-ice reference irradiance sensor. The series description states that spectra are provided at full spectral resolution interpolated to 1 nm over 320-950 nm, with PAR and broadband integrated quantities, ROV acoustic positioning and depth. It is not a 2D image cube, but it is valuable for sea-ice transmittance, under-ice light fields, sea-ice/ocean optics and validation of optical remote-sensing products.
openness: PANGAEA metadata reports CC BY 4.0 and downloadable tab-delimited child datasets.
source_type: geolocated hyperspectral radiometer series for sea-ice optical remote sensing
evidence: PANGAEA DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.979856 page states that solar radiation over and under drifting sea ice was measured using RAMSES hyper-spectral radiometers on the MOSAiC ROV between November 2019 and September 2020; spectra are provided at full spectral resolution interpolated to 1.0 nm, broadband 320-950 nm and PAR 400-700 nm integrations are included, ROV positions came from acoustic Long Base Line positioning and depth from a pressure sensor. The page is a dataset publication series, exposes many child PANGAEA IDs, and includes CC BY 4.0 license metadata.
scale: series-level MOSAiC ROV sea-ice surveys; PANGAEA page exposes hundreds of child datasets
feature_count: PANGAEA series page text indicates 440 datasets; child datasets include transmitted radiance, irradiance and transflectance records by station/survey
file_size: not summarized at series level by checked page

name: PANGAEA RV SONNE SO267/2 Pacific Above-Water Hyperspectral Radiometry
provider: University of Oldenburg / RV SONNE SO267/2 / PANGAEA
theme_canonical: shipborne above-water hyperspectral ocean-colour radiometry
theme_raw: Hyperspectral above-water radiometric quantities observed during cruise SO267/2 aboard RV SONNE
coverage: International waters of the Pacific Ocean, Suva Fiji to Manzanillo Mexico cruise track
formats: PANGAEA bundled tab-delimited datasets; ZIP download containing Ed, Lsfc and Lsky child datasets
access: PANGAEA bundled publication with child DOI datasets and ZIP download
notes: Added as bundled-publication level record, not as three separate child rows. It provides geolocated shipborne hyperspectral radiometry for ocean-colour remote sensing rather than image cubes. Raw data are noted as available on request, while processed calibrated tabular datasets are downloadable from PANGAEA.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA bundled publication 收录 RV SONNE SO267/2 太平洋航次期间的连续船载水面高光谱辐射观测。页面说明船首安装 TriOS RAMSES-ACC 和 RAMSES-ARC 高光谱辐射计，测量入射太阳辐照度、45° 天底方向海表离水辐亮度和 45° 天顶方向天空辐亮度，处理数据在 320-950 nm 范围内插值到 1 nm。它适合用于海洋水色遥感、船载 Rrs/辐射校验和海洋漂浮物/光学水质研究。
desc_en: This PANGAEA bundled publication provides continuous shipborne above-water hyperspectral radiometry from RV SONNE cruise SO267/2 across international Pacific waters. The page states that TriOS RAMSES-ACC and RAMSES-ARC radiometers mounted at the bow measured incoming solar irradiance, 45-degree nadir sea-surface leaving radiance and 45-degree zenith sky radiance, with processed spectra interpolated to 1 nm from 320-950 nm. It is useful for ocean-colour remote sensing, shipborne radiometric validation, remote-sensing reflectance workflows and marine optical-water-quality studies.
openness: PANGAEA and DataCite report CC BY 4.0; bundled page provides ZIP download of 3 child datasets.
source_type: shipborne hyperspectral ocean-colour radiometry bundled dataset
evidence: PANGAEA DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.924038 states automated continuous above-water hyperspectral calibrated radiometric quantities were observed during SO267/2 aboard RV SONNE, using two TriOS RAMSES-ACC irradiance meters and four TriOS RAMSES-ARC radiance meters; processed data were interpolated to 1 nm between 320 and 950 nm. Coverage is 2019-02-03 to 2019-02-13, Pacific Ocean cruise track, CC BY 4.0, size 3 datasets, with child DOIs for Ed, Lsfc and Lsky.
scale: Pacific Ocean SO267/2 cruise segment, 2019-02-03 to 2019-02-13
feature_count: 3 child datasets: downward irradiance, upward sea-surface leaving radiance and downward sky radiance
file_size: not summarized at bundled page level

name: PANGAEA Lake Victoria RV IBIS Above-Water Hyperspectral Radiometry
provider: Makerere University / University of Oldenburg / RV IBIS / PANGAEA
theme_canonical: shipborne inland-water hyperspectral radiometry for Lake Victoria optical remote sensing
theme_raw: Hyperspectral above-water radiometric quantities observed during cruise aboard RV IBIS on Lake Victoria, Uganda
coverage: Ugandan portion of Lake Victoria, near Napoleon Bay/Jinja, Uganda
formats: PANGAEA bundled tab-delimited datasets; ZIP download containing Ed, Lsfc and Lsky child datasets
access: PANGAEA bundled publication with child DOI datasets and ZIP download
notes: Added as bundled-publication level record. The data are geolocated hyperspectral radiometric quantities for inland-water remote sensing and floating plastic/optical water quality work, not a raster image cube. The PANGAEA page states only data with valid GPS coordinates and low spectral contribution threshold are included; remaining data are available on request.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA bundled publication 收录乌干达 Lake Victoria 上 RV IBIS 三日航次的船载高光谱水色辐射观测。页面说明船首安装 TriOS RAMSES-ACC 与 RAMSES-ARC 高光谱辐射计，测量入射太阳辐照度、45° 天底方向海表离水辐亮度和 45° 天顶方向天空辐亮度，处理数据在 320-950 nm 范围内插值到 1 nm。它适合用于内陆湖泊水色遥感、光学水质、漂浮塑料监测和卫星水色产品验证。
desc_en: This PANGAEA bundled publication provides shipborne above-water hyperspectral radiometry from a three-day RV IBIS campaign in the Ugandan portion of Lake Victoria. The page states that TriOS RAMSES-ACC and RAMSES-ARC radiometers measured incoming solar irradiance, 45-degree nadir sea-surface leaving radiance and 45-degree zenith sky radiance, with processed data interpolated to 1 nm from 320-950 nm. It is useful for inland-water ocean-colour style remote sensing, optical water quality, floating plastic monitoring and validation of satellite water products.
openness: PANGAEA and DataCite report CC BY 4.0; bundled page provides ZIP download of 3 child datasets.
source_type: shipborne inland-water hyperspectral radiometry bundled dataset
evidence: PANGAEA DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.947714 states hyperspectral radiometric quantities were observed every 5 minutes in the Ugandan portion of Lake Victoria aboard RV IBIS from 2021-10-13 to 2021-10-15 using TriOS RAMSES-ACC and RAMSES-ARC radiometers. Processed data were interpolated to 1 nm between 320 and 950 nm; page gives coverage bounds, CC BY 4.0 license, size 3 datasets, ZIP download and child DOIs for Ed, Lsfc and Lsky.
scale: Lake Victoria RV IBIS field campaign, 2021-10-13 to 2021-10-15
feature_count: 3 child datasets: downward irradiance, upward sea-surface leaving radiance and downward sky radiance
file_size: not summarized at bundled page level

name: Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta Airborne Spectroscopy Classification Maps
provider: California Department of Fish and Wildlife / UC Davis / UC Merced / KNB Data Repository
theme_canonical: airborne imaging spectroscopy derived aquatic vegetation and land-cover classification maps
theme_raw: The Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta genus and community level classification maps derived from airborne spectroscopy data
coverage: Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, northern California, USA; approximately -121.81 to -121.26 longitude and 37.76 to 38.56 latitude
formats: 14 spatialRaster ZIP files plus PDF reports; annual classification rasters derived from HyMap, AVIRIS-NG and SpecTIR airborne spectroscopy
access: KNB/DataONE dataset DOI with public read access and downloadable spatialRaster ZIP objects
doi_or_citation: 10.5063/F1HH6HJX; Khanna et al. 2024 KNB Data Repository dataset
time_span: Airborne hyperspectral imagery and derived maps for 2004-2008, 2014-2018, 2019-2022; dataset published 2024
notes: Added as an open hyperspectral-derived raster product rather than raw hyperspectral flightline cubes. The EML states that airborne hyperspectral imagery has been acquired since 2004, with 60-70 flightlines per year processed to surface reflectance/georegistered imagery and transformed with spectral angle mapper, spectral mixture analysis, spectral indexes and continuum removal before Random Forest classification. The released files are annual classification map ZIPs, not the full reflectance image archive.
desc_zh: 该 KNB/DataONE 数据集发布了 Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta 多年份航空成像光谱派生分类图。元数据说明，自 2004 年起研究团队使用 HyMap、AVIRIS-NG 和 SpecTIR 等航空高光谱影像，经大气校正、地理配准、光谱映射和随机森林分类，生成水体、沉水植被、挺水沼泽、水葫芦、水报春等类别图。开放文件是 14 个年度 spatialRaster ZIP 分类栅格和报告，适合用于入侵水生植被、湿地变化和高光谱遥感制图产品分析，但不是原始高光谱反射率 cube。
desc_en: This KNB/DataONE dataset publishes multi-year Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta classification maps derived from airborne imaging spectroscopy. The metadata states that HyMap, AVIRIS-NG and SpecTIR hyperspectral flightlines were atmospherically corrected, georegistered, transformed with spectral mapping methods and classified with Random Forest models into classes such as water, submerged aquatic vegetation, emergent marsh, water hyacinth and water primrose. The open release contains 14 annual spatialRaster ZIP classification products and reports, making it useful for invasive aquatic vegetation, wetland change and hyperspectral remote-sensing map-product analysis, but it is not a raw hyperspectral reflectance cube archive.
openness: DataONE system metadata grants public read access; EML intellectualRights states Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.
source_type: airborne hyperspectral derived raster classification maps
evidence: DOI resolves to KNB record doi:10.5063/F1HH6HJX. DataONE EML identifies 14 spatialRaster ZIP entities for annual Delta classification maps plus 3 PDF reports, public read access, CC BY 4.0, and methods using HyMap, AVIRIS-NG and SpecTIR airborne spectroscopy processed to surface reflectance, georegistered, transformed by spectral mapping methods and classified using Random Forests.
scale: Delta-wide airborne survey, about 2220 square kilometers and 60-70 flightlines per acquisition year
feature_count: 14 annual spatialRaster ZIP files; 3 PDF reports
file_size: spatialRaster ZIP files about 4.2-17.9 MB each; roughly 130 MB total for raster ZIPs

theme_raw: Vegetation classification map and covariates associated with NEON AOP survey, East River, CO 2018

doi_or_citation: 10.15485/1602034; Falco et al. 2024 EcoImaging supporting geospatial products
time_span: NEON AOP airborne survey June 2018; latest parsed EML publication date 2026-03-25
notes: Added using the latest 2026 ESS-DIVE metadata for the DOI series. Earlier 2020 Upper Colorado four-catchment metadata was found, but its referenced ZIP data object returned 401 from the ESS-DIVE member-node object endpoint, so that older version was not added as a separate row. This row covers the open East River classification/covariate package derived from NEON AOP airborne hyperspectral and LiDAR data, not the original NEON reflectance cubes.
desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE 数据集发布 East River 流域基于 NEON AOP 航空高光谱与 LiDAR 数据生成的植被分类图和环境协变量。最新 EML 说明这些地理空间产品服务于 Falco 等 2024 年 EcoImaging 研究，数据源为 2018 年夏季 NEON AOP 航空观测，并结合野外调查，用于分析地形和近地表土壤等环境梯度如何影响优势植物群落的空间格局。开放对象包括分类图、植被指数、LiDAR 派生产品、城市掩膜和分类参考数据等 ZIP 文件；它是高光谱派生制图产品，不是原始反射率 cube。
desc_en: This ESS-DIVE dataset publishes vegetation classification maps and environmental covariates for the East River watershed derived from NEON AOP airborne hyperspectral and LiDAR data. The latest EML states that the geospatial products support the Falco et al. 2024 EcoImaging study and were developed from summer 2018 NEON AOP observations together with field data to examine how topography and near-surface soil properties structure dominant plant communities. Open objects include ZIP packages for the classification map, vegetation indices, LiDAR-derived products, an urban mask and classification reference data; it is a hyperspectral-derived map product rather than a raw reflectance cube archive.
openness: EML states CC BY 4.0. DataONE system metadata for key ZIP objects is public; ESS-DIVE member-node direct GET for Classification_map.zip returned a valid ZIP header.
source_type: airborne hyperspectral and LiDAR derived vegetation classification/covariate rasters
evidence: DataONE Solr seriesId doi:10.15485/1602034 exposes latest EML ess-dive-46699da2f774f20-20260325T221506346. EML lists five ZIP entities: Vegetation_indices.zip, Urban_mask.zip, LiDAR_derived_products.zip, Classification_map.zip and Classification_reference_data.zip. System metadata reports Classification_map.zip as application/zip size 130,406,531 bytes and Vegetation_indices.zip as application/zip size 2,635,122,086 bytes; ESS-DIVE member-node object endpoint returned PK ZIP bytes for Classification_map.zip.
scale: East River watershed NEON AOP survey area; airborne survey June 2018
feature_count: 5 ZIP entity packages in latest EML
file_size: System metadata confirms at least 2.77 GB across three checked ZIP objects; Classification_map.zip is 130,406,531 bytes and Vegetation_indices.zip is 2,635,122,086 bytes

name: Seward Peninsula AVIRIS-NG Arctic Plant Functional Type Maps
provider: NGEE Arctic / Oak Ridge National Laboratory / ESS-DIVE

theme_raw: Integrating Very-High-Resolution UAS Data and Airborne Imaging Spectroscopy to Map the Fractional Composition of Arctic Plant Functional Types in Western Alaska: Supporting Data
coverage: NGEE Arctic Council watershed, Seward Peninsula near Mile Marker 71, Alaska, USA
formats: GeoTIFF, ENVI DAT/HDR, KMZ, PDF quicklooks, CSV file-level metadata and text metadata
access: ESS-DIVE DOI landing page and member-node DataONE objects; sample GeoTIFF direct GET verified
time_span: AVIRIS-NG imagery collected 2019-07-09; dataset published 2022-12-22, metadata updated 2024-10-11
notes: Added as an AVIRIS-NG derived raster product. The package provides PFT fractional cover, dominant PFT and uncertainty maps produced from topography/BRDF corrected AVIRIS-NG imagery linked with 5 cm UAS-derived ground-truthed PFT cover. It is not a full AVIRIS-NG radiance/reflectance cube archive.
desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE 数据集发布阿拉斯加 Seward Peninsula NGEE Arctic Council 流域的植物功能型比例覆盖、优势功能型和不确定性地图。元数据说明产品由 NASA AVIRIS-NG 成像光谱数据生成，AVIRIS-NG 覆盖 380-2510 nm，采集于 2019 年 7 月 9 日，并与 5 cm 分辨率 UAS 影像提取的地面真值功能型覆盖相结合建模。开放文件包括 GeoTIFF、ENVI DAT/HDR、KMZ、PDF quicklook 和元数据文件，适合北极植被功能型制图、冻土生态系统和航空高光谱派生产品研究。
desc_en: This ESS-DIVE dataset provides plant functional type fractional cover, dominant PFT and uncertainty maps for the NGEE Arctic Council watershed on the Seward Peninsula, Alaska. The metadata states that the products were derived from NASA AVIRIS-NG imaging spectroscopy collected on 2019-07-09 over 380-2510 nm and linked with 5 cm UAS-derived ground-truthed PFT cover. Open files include GeoTIFF, ENVI DAT/HDR, KMZ, PDF quicklooks and metadata, making the dataset useful for Arctic vegetation functional mapping, permafrost ecosystem studies and airborne hyperspectral derived product analysis.
openness: EML states CC BY 4.0; DataONE/ESS-DIVE metadata grants public read access and member-node object endpoint returned valid bytes for a GeoTIFF object.
source_type: AVIRIS-NG derived Arctic plant functional type raster maps
evidence: DataONE EML ess-dive-c79d1ae7f247469-20241011T153612029 lists 18 entities including fcover, pft and uncertainty products in .dat/.hdr/.tif plus KMZ/PDF quicklooks. Methods state topography and BRDF corrected AVIRIS-NG imagery from the AVIRIS-NG data portal was used to produce PFT fractional cover, uncertainty and dominant PFT maps; AVIRIS-NG data are described as 380-2510 nm. System metadata reports ang20190709t195734_corr_v2v2_fcover.tif as image/tiff size 25,923,842 bytes, and ESS-DIVE member-node object endpoint returned a GDAL TIFF header for that file.
scale: NGEE Arctic Council watershed, Seward Peninsula, Alaska; single AVIRIS-NG flightline/map set from 2019-07-09
feature_count: 18 listed ESS-DIVE entities, including fcover, dominant PFT and uncertainty map products
file_size: Checked system metadata reports fcover GeoTIFF 25,923,842 bytes and pft ENVI DAT 6,009,472 bytes; total package size not fully enumerated because CN metadata timed out for some objects

coverage: Upper East River / Crested Butte, Colorado, USA
formats: GeoTIFF with internal tiling and LZW compression
access: ESS-DIVE DOI landing page and DataONE/ESS-DIVE member-node objects; representative GeoTIFF direct GET verified
time_span: NEON AOP visible-to-shortwave-infrared imaging spectroscopy acquired in 2018; dataset pubDate 2021-11-01
notes: Added as a hyperspectral-derived GeoTIFF map product. The package provides aspen ploidy and cover/distribution maps derived from NEON Airborne Observation Platform visible-to-shortwave-infrared imaging spectroscopy, not raw NEON reflectance cubes. Use notes in EML indicate non-aspen and shaded pixels should be masked for ploidy-map use.
desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE 数据集发布科罗拉多 Crested Butte / Upper East River 区域的 aspen 倍性和覆盖图。元数据说明产品由 2018 年 NEON Airborne Observation Platform 的可见光到短波红外成像光谱数据生成，并提供 aspen 分布和用于筛选 aspen 像元的其他土地覆盖图。文件为带内部切片和 LZW 压缩的 GeoTIFF，适合研究树种遗传/功能属性、植被分布和航空高光谱派生制图产品。
desc_en: This ESS-DIVE dataset provides aspen ploidy and cover maps for the Crested Butte / Upper East River area of Colorado. The metadata states that the products were developed from 2018 NEON Airborne Observation Platform visible-to-shortwave-infrared imaging spectroscopy, with additional aspen distribution and land-cover maps used to screen aspen-dominated pixels. The files are tiled, LZW-compressed GeoTIFFs and are useful for studying tree genetic or functional attributes, vegetation distribution and airborne hyperspectral derived map products.
openness: EML states CC BY 4.0; DataONE/ESS-DIVE metadata is public and member-node object endpoint returned a TIFF header for min_phase_cover.tif.
source_type: NEON AOP imaging spectroscopy derived GeoTIFF maps
evidence: DataONE seriesId doi:10.15485/1779716 latest EML ess-dive-4c295e233d89c73-20230407T225627990860 lists two image/tiff entities: min_phase_cover.tif and min_phase_cytotype_medfilt_seived.tif. Direct GET of min_phase_cover.tif through ESS-DIVE member-node returned TIFF bytes; system metadata reports sizes 64,626,579 and 12,186,614 bytes.
scale: Crested Butte / Upper East River NEON AOP survey area
feature_count: 2 GeoTIFF entities
file_size: 64,626,579 bytes and 12,186,614 bytes for the two checked GeoTIFF objects

name: Seward Peninsula AVIRIS-NG Plant Community GeoTIFF Maps
provider: NGEE Arctic / ESS-DIVE

theme_raw: Hyperspectral remote sensing-based plant community map for region around NGEE-Arctic intensive research watersheds at Seward Peninsula, Alaska, 2017-2019
coverage: Region around NGEE-Arctic intensive research watersheds at Seward Peninsula, Alaska, USA
formats: GeoTIFF, PNG visualization, CSV file-level metadata, PDF readme and TXT class labels
access: ESS-DIVE DOI landing page and member-node DataONE objects; representative GeoTIFF direct GET verified
time_span: AVIRIS-NG data collected across 2017-2019; metadata updated 2024-07-18
notes: Added as AVIRIS-NG derived plant-community map product. The EML states AVIRIS-NG data from 2017-2019 were used to train deep neural networks with NGEE-Arctic vegetation plot observations at Kougarok, Council and Teller; two 5 m GeoTIFF maps provide Level I and Level II plant community classes.
desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE 数据集发布阿拉斯加 Seward Peninsula NGEE-Arctic 重点流域周边区域的高分辨率植物群落分布图。元数据说明研究使用 2017-2019 年 NASA AVIRIS-NG 航空成像光谱数据，并结合 Kougarok、Council 和 Teller 的植被样方观测训练深度神经网络，生成 5 m 分辨率的 Level I 和 Level II 植物群落 GeoTIFF 地图。该数据适合北极植被群落制图、生态系统模型参数化和航空高光谱分类产品分析。
desc_en: This ESS-DIVE dataset provides high-resolution plant community distribution maps for the region around NGEE-Arctic intensive research watersheds on Alaska’s Seward Peninsula. The metadata states that NASA AVIRIS-NG airborne imaging spectroscopy from 2017-2019 was used to train deep neural networks with vegetation plot observations from Kougarok, Council and Teller, producing 5 m Level I and Level II plant community GeoTIFF maps. It is useful for Arctic vegetation community mapping, ecosystem-model parameterization and analysis of airborne hyperspectral classification products.
openness: EML metadata is public and licensed CC BY 4.0; member-node object endpoint returned TIFF bytes for a representative GeoTIFF.
source_type: AVIRIS-NG derived plant community GeoTIFF maps
evidence: ESS-DIVE member-node EML ess-dive-399aad96bb4cfe4-20240718T002620902 lists seven entities including seward_regional_vegetation_community_level1.tif and level2.tif. Direct GET of level1.tif returned TIFF bytes; system metadata reports both GeoTIFFs as image/tiff with sizes 730,351,850 and 730,351,842 bytes.
scale: Regional 5 m plant community maps around NGEE-Arctic intensive watersheds, Seward Peninsula
feature_count: 7 entities; 2 main GeoTIFF maps plus labels/readme/visualization
file_size: Two main GeoTIFFs are about 730,351,850 and 730,351,842 bytes

theme_raw: Maps of Arctic vegetation leaf nitrogen concentration, albedo and plant functional type (PFT) derived from imaging spectroscopy data, Council watershed, Seward Peninsula, Alaska, 2019
coverage: Council watershed, Seward Peninsula, Alaska, USA

access: ESS-DIVE DOI landing page and member-node DataONE objects; representative ENVI binary direct GET verified

notes: Added as an AVIRIS-NG derived demonstration/product package. EML warns that the AVIRIS-NG leaf nitrogen product is preliminary and not recommended for scientific applications; that limitation is preserved here.
desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE 数据集发布阿拉斯加 Seward Peninsula Council 流域的地表反照率、叶片氮含量和植物功能型地图。元数据说明产品由 Brookhaven National Laboratory TEST group 基于 NASA AVIRIS-NG 成像光谱数据生成，AVIRIS-NG 覆盖 380-2510 nm，采集于 2019 年 7 月 9 日。开放文件包括 ENVI 风格二进制影像及 HDR、PNG quicklook、KML、PDF 和 CSV 元数据；其中叶片氮产品被标注为初步演示产品，不建议直接用于科学应用。
desc_en: This ESS-DIVE dataset provides surface albedo, leaf nitrogen concentration and plant functional type maps for the Council watershed on Alaska’s Seward Peninsula. The metadata states that the products were generated by the Brookhaven National Laboratory TEST group from NASA AVIRIS-NG imaging spectroscopy collected on 2019-07-09 over 380-2510 nm. Open files include ENVI-style binary images with HDR files, PNG quicklooks, KML, PDF and CSV metadata; the leaf nitrogen product is explicitly described as preliminary and not recommended for scientific applications.
openness: EML states CC BY 4.0; DataONE/ESS-DIVE metadata grants public read access and member-node object endpoint returned bytes for the albedo binary object.
source_type: AVIRIS-NG derived albedo, leaf nitrogen and PFT map products
evidence: DataONE EML ess-dive-05e2fff0d1b506c-20241014T142552593 lists 14 entities including albedo, nitrogen and PFT binary objects with HDR/PNG companions. Direct GET of ang20190709t195734_albedo returned binary data bytes; system metadata reports albedo, nitrogen and pft binary objects each as 6,009,472 bytes. EML states AVIRIS-NG imaging spectroscopy data over 380-2510 nm. CMR NGA273 / concept C2234858258-SCIOPS cross-index; NGEE direct data/documentation links failed lychee in v241 candidate check.
scale: Council watershed, Seward Peninsula; single 2019-07-09 AVIRIS-NG scene/product set
feature_count: 14 entities including albedo, nitrogen and PFT image products and companion metadata
file_size: Representative albedo, nitrogen and PFT binary objects are each 6,009,472 bytes; companion HDR/PNG/KML/PDF/CSV files also listed

name: USGS Nabesna Alaska 2014 HyMap Raw Airborne Imaging Spectroscopy Data
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Mineral Resources Program / ScienceBase / Science Data Catalog
theme_canonical: raw airborne HyMap imaging spectroscopy for mineral-resource characterization
theme_raw: Airborne imaging spectrometer data collected for characterizing mineral resources near Nabesna, Alaska, 2014
coverage: Nabesna Area of Interest, eastern Alaska Range, Alaska, USA; ScienceBase bounding box about -143.3752 to -140.9444 longitude and 61.9624 to 62.4354 latitude
formats: HyMap flight-line imaging spectroscopy data and associated report/FGDC metadata; ENVI-related keywords in metadata; ScienceBase main item currently exposes report PDF and FGDC XML, while DataONE/SDC expose CAL/Run child metadata
access: Official USGS data-release page with DOI link, ScienceBase item JSON, DataONE/USGS SDC public metadata and Science Data Catalog child records
doi_or_citation: 10.5066/F7DN435W; Kokaly, Hoefen, King/Ridley and Johnson 2017 USGS data release
time_span: HyMap acquisitions on 2014-07-14 and 2014-07-21; data release published 2017-09-22
notes: Added at collection level to avoid splitting one USGS data release into multiple CAL/Run rows. DataONE and USGS SDC expose the main record plus child records such as CAL01, CAL03 and Run04-Run10, all sharing the same DOI and abstract. During this pass, the ScienceBase main item JSON was accessible, but the ScienceBase collection-list endpoint intermittently returned a server-side 500, so individual flightline file download URLs were not fully enumerated; public DOI/SDC/FGDC metadata and USGS page support inclusion as an open institutional data release. The catalog URL was set to the official USGS data page because lychee GET checks against the DOI currently follow to a ScienceBase HTML 500, while the USGS page and ScienceBase JSON item remain reachable.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布收录 2014 年 7 月 14 日和 7 月 21 日在阿拉斯加东部 Alaska Range 的 Nabesna AOI 采集的 HyMap 航空成像光谱数据。元数据说明 HyMap 传感器在 0.4-2.5 微米范围内记录 126 个窄波段反射太阳光，名义地面瞬时视场约 6 m，总采集面积约 1900 km²，目标是支持斑岩矿床等矿产资源制图和方法研究。该条按 USGS 主 DOI 集合级收录，子记录 CAL/Run 不拆分为重复条目。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides HyMap airborne imaging spectroscopy collected over the Nabesna Area of Interest in the eastern Alaska Range on 2014-07-14 and 2014-07-21. The metadata states that HyMap measured reflected sunlight in 126 narrow channels from 0.4-2.5 microns, at a nominal 6 m ground instantaneous field of view, over about 1,900 square kilometers for mineral-resource mapping and method development around exposed porphyry deposits. The catalog records the release at the main DOI collection level, with CAL/Run child records treated as part of the same release rather than separate duplicate rows.
openness: USGS page marks the work CC0 1.0 Universal; USGS Science Data Catalog child pages report Access public and public-domain license; FGDC metadata access constraints are none.
source_type: raw airborne HyMap hyperspectral flight-line data release
evidence: USGS official data page and DataCite identify DOI 10.5066/F7DN435W as a 2017 USGS data release. ScienceBase item JSON summary states HyMap imaging spectrometer data over Nabesna AOI, 126 narrow channels spanning 0.4-2.5 microns, nominal 6 m GIFOV and 1,900 square kilometers. DataONE/USGS SDC records list public readPermission and FGDC metadata access constraints none. USGS SDC child page for CAL01 reports Access public and license public-domain label; USGS page reports CC0 1.0 Universal.
scale: About 1,900 square kilometers; 6 m nominal GIFOV; 126 spectral channels; 0.4-2.5 microns
feature_count: Collection-level DOI plus at least 9 child CAL/Run records discovered in DataONE/SDC: CAL01, CAL03 and Run04-Run10
file_size: Main ScienceBase item exposes 91,507-byte report PDF and 28,007-byte FGDC XML; flightline file sizes not fully enumerated because ScienceBase collection-list endpoint returned 500 during this pass

name: ORNL DAAC AVIRIS-NG Hyperspectral Imagery for Idaho and California Sites 2014-2015
provider: ORNL DAAC / NASA Earthdata / Reynolds Creek CZO HydroShare lead
theme_canonical: AVIRIS-NG surface reflectance flightline imagery over Idaho and California research sites
theme_raw: Hyperspectral Imagery from AVIRIS-NG for Sites in ID and CA, USA, 2014 and 2015
coverage: Reynolds Creek Experimental Watershed and Hollister in Idaho, plus Big Pine/Inyo National Forest area in California, USA
license: NASA/ORNL DAAC open data; HydroShare pointer page CC BY 4.0
formats: Protected ORNL DAAC granule tar.gz packages with AVIRIS-NG surface reflectance products; public documentation, boundaries, quicklooks and checksums; Earthdata/S3 access paths
access: ORNL DAAC DOI and NASA CMR/Earthdata collection with protected HTTPS/S3 granules requiring Earthdata access; HydroShare resource provides a discoverable pointer to ORNL DAAC DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1533
doi_or_citation: 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1533; Dashti, Glenn, Spaete and Ilangakoon 2018 ORNL DAAC dataset
notes: HydroShare resource 32c70ec7339e4e529302534cf04a01e5 was used as a 2-hop lead but was not added separately because it stores only a tiny pointer resource and links to the authoritative ORNL DAAC DOI. CMR identifies this as ORNL_CLOUD collection C2765511853-ORNL_CLOUD with 72 granules; granule data links are protected Earthdata/ORNL DAAC HTTPS and S3 objects, with public checksums and documentation links.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集提供 2014-2015 年 AVIRIS-NG 在美国 Idaho 和 California 多个研究区采集的地表反射率影像。DataCite/CMR 元数据说明，AVIRIS-NG 以约 5 nm 光谱间隔覆盖 380-2510 nm，可见光到短波红外范围，产品经过辐射和几何校正，空间分辨率为 1 m，包含 Reynolds Creek Experimental Watershed、Hollister 以及 California Big Pine/Inyo National Forest 附近的 72 条 flight line。数据通过 ORNL DAAC/NASA Earthdata 访问，适合生态水文、植被、地表反射率和航空高光谱算法研究。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset provides AVIRIS-NG surface reflectance imagery acquired over research sites in Idaho and California during 2014-2015. DataCite and CMR metadata state that AVIRIS-NG measured visible-to-shortwave-infrared reflectance from 380-2510 nm at about 5 nm spectral intervals, with radiometric and geometric calibration and 1 m spatial resolution. The collection includes 72 flight lines over Reynolds Creek Experimental Watershed, Hollister and the Big Pine/Inyo National Forest area, and is accessible through ORNL DAAC/NASA Earthdata for ecohydrology, vegetation, surface reflectance and airborne hyperspectral algorithm studies.
openness: CMR online_access_flag is true and exposes Earthdata Search, protected HTTPS/S3 granule links, public checksums and documentation; authoritative DOI is ORNL DAAC 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1533. HydroShare pointer page is discoverable and CC BY 4.0 but not the authoritative data archive.
source_type: AVIRIS-NG airborne surface reflectance flightline imagery
evidence: DataCite DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/1533 and CMR collection C2765511853-ORNL_CLOUD identify the dataset as AVIRIS-NG surface reflectance for ID/CA sites, 380-2510 nm, 5 nm intervals, 1 m spatial resolution and 72 flight lines. CMR granule search reports 72 granules and sample granules expose protected ORNL DAAC tar.gz data links, public .sha256 files, S3 links and documentation. HydroShare resource 32c70ec7339e4e529302534cf04a01e5 points to the same ORNL DAAC DOI.
scale: 72 AVIRIS-NG flight lines; 1 m spatial resolution; 380-2510 nm; sites in Idaho and California
feature_count: 72 CMR granules
file_size: Not fully summed; sample granules are protected tar.gz flightline packages with public .sha256 checksum links

CMR collection C3274546970-NSIDC_CPRD is cloud hosted and exposes protected HTTPS/S3 .hdr/.img granules plus public extended metadata XML.
desc_zh: 该 NSIDC DAAC 数据集提供 SnowEx 2021 任务期间 Colorado Senator Beck Basin 和 Grand Mesa 两个积雪研究区的 AVIRIS-NG 表观地表光谱反射率影像。NSIDC 页面说明数据用于展示不同地形和植被条件下的雪反照率及雪光学性质演化，采集于 2021 年春季三个日期，空间分辨率为 4 m，平台为 B-200，传感器为 AVIRIS-NG，格式包括 BIL 和 ASCII。数据通过 NASA Earthdata/NSIDC 云端访问，需要免费的 Earthdata Login。
desc_en: This NSIDC DAAC dataset provides AVIRIS-NG apparent surface spectral reflectance imagery for the SnowEx 2021 Senator Beck Basin and Grand Mesa study sites in Colorado. The NSIDC page states that the data document snow albedo and snow optical property evolution across two contrasting snow-covered environments over three spring 2021 acquisition dates, with 4 m spatial resolution, B-200 platform, AVIRIS-NG sensor and BIL/ASCII formats. Data are available through NASA Earthdata/NSIDC cloud access and require a free Earthdata Login.
openness: NASA Open Data and NSIDC report public access; NSIDC states free Earthdata Login is required for data access. CMR online_access_flag is true and cloud_hosted is true.
source_type: AVIRIS-NG airborne snow surface spectral reflectance imagery
evidence: NSIDC page SNEX21_SSR Version 1 lists DOI 10.5067/ZAI3M64WWN5V, platform B-200, sensor AVIRIS-NG, parameter REFLECTANCE, BIL/ASCII formats, temporal coverage 2021-03-19 to 2021-04-29, 4 m spatial resolution and Earthdata Login requirement. CMR collection C3274546970-NSIDC_CPRD reports cloud_hosted true, online_access_flag true and 24 granules; sample granules expose protected .hdr/.img HTTPS and S3 links plus public XML metadata links.
scale: 24 CMR granules over Senator Beck Basin and Grand Mesa; 4 m spatial resolution; SnowEx 2021 time series
feature_count: 24 CMR granules
file_size: Not fully summed; sample granules expose paired .hdr and .img files plus XML metadata in NSIDC Earthdata Cloud

CMR states that imagery was collected on 2023-04-20 across snow-covered boreal forest environments near Fairbanks, including Farmers Loop Creamers Field and Caribou Poker Creek Research Watershed, to study snow albedo and optical properties. Public metadata and browse files are available, while BSQ/HDR data objects are provided through protected NASA Earthdata Cloud access.

desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集发布 NASA SHIFT 活动期间 AVIRIS-NG 采集的 L2A 未正射校正地表反射率影像。CMR 摘要说明 SHIFT 在 2022 年 2-5 月以近周尺度覆盖 California Santa Barbara County 及近岸海域约 1656 km²，用于评估高频 VSWIR 重访的科学价值和植被群落变化。数据以 BIN/HDR granule 形式通过 ORNL DAAC/NASA Earthdata 访问，适合高光谱反射率、时间序列和预处理算法研究。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset publishes AVIRIS-NG L2A unrectified surface reflectance imagery from the NASA SHIFT campaign. CMR states that SHIFT collected VSWIR data at approximately weekly cadence from February to May 2022 over about 1,656 square kilometers of Santa Barbara County, California and nearby coastal Pacific waters to assess the science value of high-frequency VSWIR revisits. Data are provided as BIN/HDR granules through ORNL DAAC/NASA Earthdata and are useful for hyperspectral reflectance, time-series and preprocessing research.

provider: ORNL DAAC / NASA Delta-X campaign

theme_raw: Delta-X AVIRIS-NG L3 Derived Vegetation Types, MRD, Louisiana, USA
coverage: Atchafalaya and Terrebonne basins, Mississippi River Delta, coastal Louisiana, USA

access: ORNL DAAC DOI and CMR/Earthdata protected COG GeoTIFF granules
notes: Added as an AVIRIS-NG L3 derived map product. CMR summary states vegetation types were classified from Level-2B BRDF-adjusted surface reflectance using field sample and CRMS plot data, then applied to AVIRIS-NG mosaic imagery.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集发布 Delta-X 2021 活动中基于 AVIRIS-NG 影像生成的 Mississippi River Delta 植被类型图。CMR 摘要说明产品覆盖 Louisiana Atchafalaya 和 Terrebonne 流域，利用 Level-2B BRDF 校正地表反射率、草本植被样点和 Louisiana CRMS 植被样方数据训练机器学习模型，并应用到 AVIRIS-NG mosaic 上生成植被类型分类图。数据以 COG GeoTIFF 形式提供，是高光谱派生生态制图产品。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset provides Delta-X 2021 vegetation type maps derived from AVIRIS-NG imagery over the Atchafalaya and Terrebonne basins of the Mississippi River Delta, Louisiana. CMR states that vegetation types were classified from Level-2B BRDF-adjusted surface reflectance using field samples and Louisiana CRMS vegetation plot data to train a machine-learning model, which was then applied to AVIRIS-NG mosaic imagery. The data are provided as COG GeoTIFFs and represent a hyperspectral-derived ecological mapping product.

feature_count: 2 CMR granules

provider: ORNL DAAC / NASA Pre-Delta-X
