name: UC-HSI UAV-Based Crop Hyperspectral Imaging Benchmark Dataset
provider: IIT Hyderabad / TiHAN / ICRISAT / CrHyperS GitHub repository
theme_canonical: UAV-borne crop hyperspectral image benchmark for crop classification and growth-stage annotation
theme_raw: UC-HSI: UAV-Based Crop Hyperspectral Imaging Datasets and Machine Learning Benchmark Results
coverage: ICRISAT Hyderabad agricultural fields; ten crop classes including sorghum, pearl millet, maize, groundnut, cowpea, common bean, lima bean, mung bean, chickpea and pigeon pea
license: GitHub code/repository public; dataset access via TiHAN request form and data usage agreement; explicit dataset license not stated in README
formats: UAV hyperspectral image patches / benchmark samples; README lists patch size 11 x 11 x 300 and 69,514 total samples; TiHAN page lists UAV HSI data in 282 spectral channels
access: Public GitHub metadata/code repository plus TiHAN request-access workflow; fill Google form and receive download link after approval
doi_or_citation: 10.1109/LGRS.2024.3431644; Sankararao, Rajalakshmi and Choudhary (2024), UC-HSI: UAV-Based Crop Hyperspectral Imaging Datasets and Machine Learning Benchmark Results, IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters, 21, 5508005
time_span: paper and repository 2024; URL and TiHAN access page verified 2026-06-12
notes: Phase394 UC-HSI 2-hop follow-up from UAV hyperspectral review and GitHub repository. GitHub README states the dataset is hosted on TiHAN servers due to file size and access policy; TiHAN page says requesters fill a Google form, receive confirmation, and then a download link, with datasets subject to a data usage agreement. Therefore access is recorded as request/approval rather than direct open download. TiHAN URL: https://tihan.iith.ac.in/TiAND.html
desc_zh: UC-HSI 是面向农业作物分类和生育期识别的 UAV 高光谱遥感 benchmark。GitHub README 描述数据由 DJI Matrice-600 Pro 搭载 Resonon Pika-L VNIR 传感器采集，覆盖 385-1021 nm、300 个波段，包含 10 类重要作物、69,514 个样本和 11 x 11 x 300 patch；TiHAN 页面也说明农业高光谱影像为 400-1000 nm、约 282 个光谱通道。数据入口公开，但下载需要通过 TiHAN 表单申请并同意数据使用协议。
desc_en: UC-HSI is a UAV-borne crop hyperspectral benchmark for crop classification and growth-stage recognition. The GitHub README describes data collected from a DJI Matrice-600 Pro with a Resonon Pika-L VNIR sensor, spanning 385-1021 nm with 300 bands, ten crop classes, 69,514 samples and 11 x 11 x 300 patches; the TiHAN access page similarly lists agricultural HSI data across 400-1000 nm with about 282 spectral channels. The metadata and access workflow are public, but dataset download requires TiHAN request approval and a data usage agreement.
openness: public repository and public TiHAN request workflow; download link granted after approval; dataset usage agreement required; explicit dataset license not visible
source_type: UAV crop hyperspectral image benchmark dataset
evidence: GitHub README states UC-HSI is a high-resolution UAV hyperspectral dataset over ten crops, with Resonon Pika-L sensor, 300 bands from 385-1021 nm, 69,514 total samples, patch size 11 x 11 x 300, and TiHAN-hosted access. TiHAN page lists Hyperspectral Imaging (Aerial) agriculture datasets with 282 spectral channels across 400-1000 nm and a request-access/download-after-approval process.
scale: ten crop classes; 533 training images and 353 test images for crop classification; 69,514 total samples reported by README
feature_count: 300-band UAV HSI patches; 11 x 11 x 300 samples; TiHAN page reports 282 spectral channels for aerial agriculture HSI
file_size: not reported; README says hosted on TiHAN servers due to file size and access policy

time_span: ICA 2024 paper published online 2025-02-04; URL and TiHAN page verified 2026-06-12

This is a separate pearl-millet water-stress benchmark from the broader UC-HSI crop classification repository, but it uses the same TiHAN request/approval distribution pathway. desc_en: This dataset is a standalone UAV hyperspectral benchmark for pearl millet canopy water-stress recognition within the UC-HSI/TiHAN ecosystem. The GitHub README describes Resonon Pika-L imagery from a DJI Matrice-600 Pro, spanning 400-1000 nm with 282 bands, 350 genotypes, and well-watered, WS1 and WS2 water-stress treatments. Samples are organized as 25 x 25 x 282 hyperspectral patches, with dataset download provided through TiHAN request approval.
openness: public repository and public TiHAN request workflow; download link granted after approval; dataset usage agreement required; explicit dataset license not visible
source_type: UAV crop water-stress hyperspectral image benchmark dataset
evidence: GitHub README states crop pearl millet, Resonon Pika-L HSI camera, 282 bands from 400-1000 nm, DJI Matrice-600 Pro UAV, WW/WS1/WS2 stress levels, 350 genotypes, 25 x 25 x 282 patches, and train/test sample counts. Springer/ICRISAT pages identify the DOI 10.1007/978-3-031-74440-2_19; TiHAN page lists aerial hyperspectral agriculture datasets and request-access download workflow.

feature_count: 25 x 25 x 282 HSI patches; 282 bands over 400-1000 nm; train/test splits for WW vs WS1 and WW vs WS2

desc_en: This GitHub data repository supports the Remote Sensing paper “UAV-Based Hyperspectral Imaging for River Algae Pigment Estimation” and provides UAV/field hyperspectral reflectance data with algal pigment and biomass measurements. It contains Gold Creek hoop spectral text files, Bear Gulch and Gold Creek downwelling-correction files, and an Excel table for chlorophyll-a and phycocyanin biomass. The repository is CC0-licensed and is useful for aquatic hyperspectral remote sensing, river algae monitoring and pigment retrieval validation.
openness: public GitHub repository, direct file access, CC0-1.0 license
source_type: UAV/field river algae hyperspectral pigment-estimation dataset
evidence: GitHub repository is titled as the data repository for the Remote Sensing paper and lists multiple 2021-09-09 Gold Creek hoop spectral TXT files, BearGulch/GoldCreek downwelling-correction files, Biomass_Data_Chla-Phycocyanin.xlsx and a CC0-1.0 license. The paper data availability statement points to this repository and DOI 10.3390/rs15123148.
scale: Upper Clark Fork River algae hoop sites; at least 20 Gold Creek hoop spectral files plus Bear Gulch/Gold Creek correction files
feature_count: spectral TXT files and Chl-a/phycocyanin biomass spreadsheet; file count visible in GitHub repository

desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供 Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta 的船载高分辨率水质测绘、垂向剖面、水样和水面上方手持光谱仪高光谱野外光谱数据。调查区域包括 South Delta Tidal Transition Zone 中的 Discovery Bay、Old River 和 Middle River，2024 年 6 月与 9 月各开展一天测绘，并继续补充 2025 年数据。页面明确标注 CC0 许可和 DOI；同时说明数据未来会修订，因此这里按 USGS 公开数据发布但“持续更新/修订”记录。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides broad-area boat-based water-quality mapping, vertical profiler measurements, discrete water-quality samples and above-water hyperspectral field spectroscopy from handheld spectroradiometers in the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta. The surveys covered Discovery Bay and Old and Middle Rivers in the South Delta Tidal Transition Zone during June and September 2024, with additional 2025 data collection. The landing page lists a DOI and CC0 rights, and notes that the dataset will be revised as the final dataset is completed.
openness: public USGS data release, DOI 10.5066/P1353UA8, CC0 1.0 Universal rights
source_type: above-water aquatic hyperspectral field spectroscopy and water-quality validation dataset
evidence: USGS landing page states DOI 10.5066/P1353UA8, CC0 rights, publication year 2025, regions Discovery Bay and Old/Middle Rivers, June and September 2024 boat-based mapping surveys, and future inclusion of field-based hyperspectral data collected with a handheld spectroradiometer plus water-quality data.
scale: South Delta Tidal Transition Zone mapping surveys in June and September 2024 plus additional 2025 water-quality/hyperspectral field data
feature_count: boat-based high-resolution water-quality mapping, vertical profiler data, handheld hyperspectral field spectroscopy and discrete water-quality samples
file_size: not reported on extracted USGS landing page

desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集收录 BOREAS RSS-19 团队 1994 年使用 Chieftain Navaho 飞机搭载 CASI 传感器获取的机载辐射与反射率影像。影像覆盖 Manitoba 的 Thompson 和 Saskatchewan 的 Prince Albert 附近通量塔/研究站点，时间从 1994 年 2 月到 9 月，目标是观测北方森林景观辐射反射特性的季节变化。CMR 记录提供 Earthdata Search、DOI、ORNL 受保护打包数据和公开 companion 文件入口。

The images cover BOREAS flux-tower and study sites near Thompson, Manitoba, and Prince Albert, Saskatchewan, and were collected from February to September to observe seasonal changes in boreal-forest radiometric reflectance. CMR exposes Earthdata Search, DOI, ORNL protected bundle and public companion-file links.
openness: open NASA EOSDIS metadata and documentation; Earthdata login may be needed for protected bundle/data objects
source_type: observed airborne CASI hyperspectral/narrowband radiance and reflectance imagery

feature_count: CMR granules available

desc_en: This ORNL DAAC collection provides BOREAS RSS-02 ASAS Level-1b at-sensor radiance images in BSQ binary image format. desc_en: This figshare dataset supports a study on detecting the invasive riparian species Japanese knotweed using proximal leaf-level spectroscopy and functional traits. It contains in-situ ASD HandHeld 2 reflectance from 325-1075 nm and biochemical/structural traits such as nutrients, height, biomass and leaf mass per area. It supports invasive plant detection and spectroscopy-trait fusion modeling.

evidence: DataCite metadata for DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.30576353.v2 describes ASD hyperspectral reflectance and functional traits.

desc_en: This Dryad dataset supports modeling plant species distributions in French and Swiss alpine grasslands using airborne imaging spectroscopy. It includes high spectral and spatial resolution reflectance data and optical indices sensitive to chlorophyll, water content and leaf area index. It is a useful ecological hyperspectral dataset for species distribution modeling.

It includes 66 Lophelia pertusa coral samples exposed to different 2-methylnaphthalene concentrations and imaged hyperspectrally. It is a boundary but valuable proximal underwater HSI source for coral health classification and ecosystem monitoring research.

desc_en: This USGS data release supports development of an Earth-observation monitoring system for harmful algal blooms in north Texas reservoirs. It includes site locations, water-quality data, phytoplankton taxonomy and hyperspectral water-surface reflectance from 39 sites across 16 reservoirs and one stream site during 2019-2020. It is useful for HAB monitoring, aquatic algorithms and inland-water remote-sensing validation.

evidence: DataCite DOI 10.5066/P9X1R5IM and ScienceBase API item 5fe38f8ed34ea5387deb4923 list spectroradiometric, water-quality and phytoplankton files.

desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset provides a synthesized hyperspectral bio-optical dataset for algorithm development. It contains inherent and apparent optical properties at 1 nm resolution from 350-800 nm, with IOPs generated from bio-optical relationships and AOPs simulated using Hydrolight-Ecolight 5.1. It supports aquatic algorithm testing, sensitivity analysis and hyperspectral water retrieval development.

It demonstrates reprojection of planar hyperspectral scans into real 3D point-cloud representations and includes tutorial resources. It supports mineral exploration, complex-terrain geological mapping and hyperspectral-3D workflow development.

It is a proximal agricultural HSI source for crop disease detection and early warning models.

name: Zenodo Bad Lauchstaedt Grassland Trait Retrieval Spectral Field Campaign Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Ecological Informatics companion data
theme_canonical: Grassland hyperspectral trait retrieval field campaign
theme_raw: field spectral reflectance, soil spectra, plant traits and leaf pigment lab spectra for grassland management and phenology study
coverage: Bad Lauchstaedt, Germany
license: Zenodo record license; check landing page for exact terms
formats: CSV, RData, TAR.GZ spectral/lab data
access: Zenodo direct-download dataset record
notes: Zenodo API shows six files totaling ~26 MB including reflectances_obs_0504-1011_all.csv, plant trait measurements, soil reflectance RData and lab spectra archive.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集来自德国 Bad Lauchstaedt 2023 年草地管理与物候野外试验，包含样地光谱反射率、植物性状、干/湿/中等湿度土壤光谱，以及叶片样品实验室光谱数据。它用于研究草地管理和物候对遥感性状反演精度的影响，适合草地高光谱反演、性状建模和地面验证。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset comes from a 2023 field campaign at Bad Lauchstaedt, Germany, studying how grassland management and phenology affect trait retrieval from remote sensing observations. It includes site reflectance measurements, plant traits, soil reflectance under different wetness states and lab spectra for leaf pigment estimation. It supports grassland hyperspectral retrieval, trait modeling and ground validation.
openness: open Zenodo dataset with downloadable files
source_type: grassland hyperspectral field spectroscopy and trait dataset
evidence: DataCite and Zenodo API for DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14777369 describe field spectral reflectance, plant traits, soil spectra and lab spectra files.
scale: Bad Lauchstaedt grassland field campaign
feature_count: reflectance observations, measured plant traits, soil spectra and leaf pigment lab spectra

name: Dryad Tropical Landscape Species-Level Tree Crown Maps from Hyperspectral Imagery
provider: Dryad
theme_canonical: Hyperspectral tree crown maps and forest recruitment
theme_raw: species-specific tree crown maps generated from hyperspectral imagery for tropical forest recovery modeling
coverage: fragmented tropical agricultural landscape, study context in Dryad record
license: Dryad open dataset license; check landing page for exact terms
formats: Dryad-hosted data files for tree crown maps, field recruits and property boundaries
access: Dryad direct-download dataset record
notes: DataCite describes species-specific tree crown maps generated from hyperspectral imagery, integrated with property boundaries and field tree recruit data to predict landscape-scale recruitment.
desc_zh: 该 Dryad 数据集围绕热带农业景观中的森林恢复和树木补充，包含由高光谱影像生成的物种级树冠图，并与地块边界和野外树苗分布数据结合。它适合树种制图、森林恢复建模、种源-补充关系分析和生态遥感研究。
desc_en: This Dryad dataset supports forest recovery modeling in a fragmented tropical agricultural landscape. It includes species-specific tree crown maps generated from hyperspectral imagery, combined with property boundaries and field data on tree recruits. It is useful for tree species mapping, restoration modeling, seed-source analysis and ecological remote sensing.
openness: open Dryad dataset landing page with downloadable files
source_type: derived hyperspectral tree crown map and ecological modeling dataset
evidence: DataCite metadata for DOI 10.5061/dryad.dr7sqvb0d describes hyperspectral-derived species-specific tree crown maps and field recruitment data.
scale: tropical agricultural landscape study area
feature_count: species-level crown maps, property boundaries and field recruit data

name: Dryad UAV Hyperspectral Plant-Soil Feedback Trait Response Dataset
provider: Dryad / Utrecht University / Wageningen University & Research
theme_canonical: UAV hyperspectral crop trait retrieval and plant-soil feedback
theme_raw: UAV-based hyperspectral and EGB sensor data with in situ oat crop traits for plant-soil feedback field experiment
coverage: agro-ecological field experiment in the Netherlands / Wageningen research context
license: Dryad open reuse license; check landing page for exact terms
formats: PDF README, XLSX tabular data
access: Dryad direct-download dataset record
time_span: 2018 Dryad release; field experiment described in Biogeosciences 2017
notes: Dryad page lists two downloadable files totaling ~651 KB and describes UAV hyperspectral data used to calibrate and validate regression models for plant height, chlorophyll, nitrogen content and fresh biomass in oat crop plant-soil feedback experiments.
seen_in_passes: hyperspectral-2026-06; phase-v391-dryad-plant-traits; 2-hop from Dryad/remote-sensing plant trait references
desc_zh: 该 Dryad 数据集来自植物-土壤反馈田间试验，包含用于燕麦作物性状反演的 UAV 高光谱与 EGB 传感器相关数据，并配套株高、叶绿素、氮含量和鲜重等实测性状。它适合用于农业高光谱性状反演、UAV 遥感验证、植物-土壤反馈生态机制分析和回归建模基准。
desc_en: This Dryad dataset supports a field-scale plant-soil feedback experiment using UAV-based optical sensors. The record describes hyperspectral data used to calibrate and validate regression models for oat plant height, chlorophyll, nitrogen content and fresh biomass, alongside in situ measurements. It is useful for agricultural hyperspectral trait retrieval, UAV remote-sensing validation and ecological regression modeling.
openness: open Dryad dataset landing page with downloadable files
source_type: UAV hyperspectral field experiment and crop trait dataset
evidence: Dryad DOI 10.5061/dryad.75k1d lists downloadable README/XLSX files and states that hyperspectral data were acquired before destructive sampling for calibration and independent validation of plant-trait retrieval models.
scale: field experiment; five randomized field blocks; tabular dataset package
feature_count: two Dryad files; UAV hyperspectral-derived trait model data and in situ measurements

name: Zenodo Olive Tree Raw Hyperspectral Plant Stress Detection Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Aristotle University of Thessaloniki
theme_canonical: Olive tree raw hyperspectral plant stress detection
theme_raw: raw Cubert S-185 hyperspectral images, olive tree sample IDs and ground-truth stress labels
coverage: olive field in Halkidiki, Northern Greece
formats: TIFF hyperspectral cubes, XLSX ground truth/statistics table
access: Zenodo direct-download dataset record
notes: Zenodo API and landing page list nine files totaling about 7.5 MB: eight session_*_cube.tiff hyperspectral cubes and merged_hs_olive_gr_tr.xlsx. The record states that Cubert S-185 data include 138 spectral bands and ground truth for Verticillium dahliae, Pleospora herbarum and other stressors.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集包含希腊 Halkidiki 橄榄园中由 Cubert S-185 采集的原始高光谱影像，并配套橄榄树样本编号、138 个光谱波段的分区统计以及病害/胁迫地面真值。它适合用于植物病害检测、作物胁迫识别、近地/田间高光谱分类和小样本高光谱建模。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides raw Cubert S-185 hyperspectral images from an olive field in Halkidiki, Northern Greece, together with sample identifiers, multiband zonal statistics and ground-truth stress labels. The record documents 138 spectral bands and stress categories including Verticillium dahliae, Pleospora herbarum and other stressors. It is useful for plant disease detection, crop stress recognition and field hyperspectral classification.
openness: open Zenodo dataset with CC BY 4.0 license and downloadable files
source_type: raw field hyperspectral image cubes and plant stress labels
evidence: Zenodo record/API for DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10336545 lists TIFF cube files, an XLSX table, Cubert S-185 acquisition, 138 bands and CC BY 4.0 rights.
scale: eight hyperspectral cube TIFF files plus label/statistics workbook; olive field samples
feature_count: 8 raw hyperspectral TIFF cubes; 1 merged XLSX ground-truth/statistics file
file_size: about 7.5 MB by Zenodo API file sizes; landing page reports cumulative download volume separately

name: Figshare South Moravia Waterlogged Agricultural Land Hyperspectral Indices Dataset
provider: Figshare / Agriculture journal companion data
theme_canonical: Hyperspectral survey indices for waterlogged agricultural land
theme_raw: MAXENT model outputs and parameter-setting analysis for hyperspectral indices identifying waterlogged agricultural areas
coverage: South Moravia, Czech Republic
formats: PDF model reports and related Figshare files
access: Figshare direct-download dataset record
time_span: aerial survey periods include 2016, 2017 and 2020 cases; Figshare posted 2022-12-12
notes: Figshare API lists 14 files totaling about 11.16 MB. The companion article states that hyperspectral aerial surveying and 33 spectral indices were used with MAXENT/EnmEvaluate to identify waterlogged agricultural land.
desc_zh: 该 Figshare 数据集是南摩拉维亚农田水涝识别研究的补充数据，包含基于高光谱航测指数的 MAXENT 模型结果和模型参数设置分析。它不是原始影像包，而是文献可追溯的高光谱指数/模型输出数据，适合用于水涝农田识别、光谱指数筛选和农业遥感模型复现实验。
desc_en: This Figshare dataset contains companion outputs for identifying waterlogged agricultural land in South Moravia using hyperspectral aerial-survey indices. The related article describes 33 spectral indices and MAXENT/EnmEvaluate modeling, while the Figshare record provides downloadable model-output files. It is a literature-linked derived dataset rather than raw imagery, useful for spectral-index screening, waterlogging detection and agricultural remote-sensing reproducibility.
openness: open Figshare dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: derived hyperspectral-index model outputs for agricultural waterlogging mapping
evidence: Figshare API for article 21710309 lists DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.21710309.v1, CC BY 4.0, 14 downloadable files and tags Remote sensing/Hyperspectral survey/Maxent; MDPI article links the repository and describes the hyperspectral aerial survey.
scale: 14 Figshare files; multiple site/season MAXENT model outputs
feature_count: 14 downloadable files
file_size: 11.16 MB by Figshare API total file size

name: PLOS CASI Marine Benthic Biodiversity Hyperspectral Optical Variability Dataset
provider: PLOS Figshare / PLOS ONE companion data
theme_canonical: CASI hyperspectral optical variability and marine benthic biodiversity
theme_raw: supplementary tables for spectral variation hypothesis analysis using CASI hyperspectral imagery and benthic biodiversity data
coverage: marine benthic habitats, study area described in PLOS ONE article
formats: DOC supplementary tables
access: PLOS Figshare direct-download companion dataset
time_span: PLOS Figshare posted 2013-02-06; article/data from CASI hyperspectral biodiversity study
notes: PLOS Figshare API lists two supplementary table files totaling ~122 kB under CC BY 4.0. The landing-page abstract states that the study used Compact Airborne Spectrographic Imager (CASI) hyperspectral imagery to test the spectral variation hypothesis for marine benthic biodiversity.
desc_zh: 该 PLOS Figshare 记录提供使用 CASI 高光谱影像研究海洋底栖生物多样性光谱变异假说的补充表格数据。它不是原始 CASI 影像下载包，而是开放论文配套的高光谱遥感分析数据，可用于追踪海洋底栖生物多样性、光谱变异和遥感生态指标之间的关系。
desc_en: This PLOS Figshare record provides supplementary table data for a study relating remotely sensed optical variability to marine benthic biodiversity using Compact Airborne Spectrographic Imager (CASI) hyperspectral imagery. It is not a raw-image archive, but it is a citable open companion dataset for literature-traceable hyperspectral ecological analysis. It supports work on spectral variation, marine benthic biodiversity and remote-sensing ecological indicators.
openness: open PLOS Figshare dataset under CC BY 4.0
source_type: literature-linked supplementary tables derived from CASI hyperspectral imagery analysis
evidence: PLOS Figshare API for article 155965 lists DOI 10.1371/journal.pone.0055624, CC BY 4.0 and two downloadable supplementary files; the landing page states the analysis used CASI hyperspectral imagery.
scale: two supplementary table files; marine benthic biodiversity and spectral variability analysis
feature_count: 2 downloadable supplementary files
file_size: about 122 kB by Figshare API total file size

name: PANGAEA Central Portugal Mediterranean Grassland Hyperspectral Reflectance Dataset
provider: PANGAEA / Biogeosciences companion data
theme_canonical: Grassland hyperspectral reflectance for GPP and fertilization regime analysis
theme_raw: in situ hyperspectral reflectance at 350 nm onward in grassland plots under fertilization treatments
coverage: Companhia das Lezirias, Central Portugal (38.829 N, -8.791 E)
formats: PANGAEA tab-delimited table download; metadata page; Google Earth/map links
access: PANGAEA direct-download dataset page
notes: This is the hyperspectral-reflectance member of PANGAEA publication series 10.1594/PANGAEA.892837. The PANGAEA page lists reflectance parameters at 1 nm wavelengths beginning at 350 nm, linked to grassland plots under different fertilization regimes and GPP estimation.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集是葡萄牙中部地中海草地施肥试验的原位高光谱反射率数据，记录了草地样地在 2016 年春季到初夏期间的连续波段反射率。它是 Biogeosciences 论文配套数据系列中的高光谱反射率子集，可用于草地 GPP 估算、施肥处理响应、植被指数构建和卫星波段模拟验证。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset provides in situ hyperspectral reflectance for Mediterranean grassland plots in Central Portugal under different fertilization regimes. It is the reflectance-specific member of a broader PANGAEA publication series supporting a Biogeosciences study on GPP estimation from vegetation indices and hyperspectral reflectance. It is useful for grassland productivity modeling, fertilization response analysis, vegetation-index design and satellite-band simulation.
openness: open PANGAEA dataset under CC BY 3.0
source_type: in situ hyperspectral reflectance table for grassland remote-sensing validation
evidence: PANGAEA DOI page 10.1594/PANGAEA.892839 lists the dataset title, coverage, dates, CC BY 3.0 license and reflectance parameters by wavelength; parent series 10.1594/PANGAEA.892837 links the Biogeosciences article and related gas exchange/meteorology datasets.
scale: single PANGAEA reflectance table with wavelength columns and plot/date observations
feature_count: reflectance parameters from 350 nm onward plus plot/date metadata
file_size: PANGAEA tabular dataset; exact byte size not shown on landing page

name: PANGAEA SeaSWIR Turbid Estuarine SWIR Hyperspectral Water Reflectance Dataset
provider: PANGAEA / SeaSWIR project / ESSD companion data
theme_canonical: SWIR hyperspectral marine reflectance for turbid waters
theme_raw: ASD marine reflectance, total suspended matter and turbidity from Gironde, Rio de la Plata and Scheldt estuaries
coverage: Gironde estuary, France; Rio de la Plata, Argentina/Uruguay; Scheldt estuary, Belgium/Netherlands
formats: PANGAEA dataset publication series with tab-delimited child datasets
access: PANGAEA direct-download dataset publication series
notes: DataCite describes 137 ASD marine reflectances, 137 TSM measurements and 97 turbidity measurements from three turbid estuarine sites. The PANGAEA series includes child datasets for ASD plaque radiance, sky radiance, total upwelling radiance, ASD water reflectance, TRIOS quantities and suspended matter/turbidity.
desc_zh: SeaSWIR 是面向高浑浊河口水体的短波红外高光谱水体反射率数据集，覆盖 Gironde、Rio de la Plata 和 Scheldt 三个河口。数据包含 ASD 海洋反射率、悬浮物和浊度观测，并配套多个 PANGAEA 子表，适合水色遥感大气校正、SWIR 总悬浮物算法和 PACE/EnMAP/PRISMA 等高光谱水体应用验证。
desc_en: SeaSWIR is a PANGAEA dataset publication series for hyperspectral SWIR water reflectance in highly turbid estuarine waters. It includes ASD marine reflectances, total suspended matter and turbidity measurements from the Gironde, Rio de la Plata and Scheldt estuaries, with linked child tables for radiometric quantities and water reflectance. It supports aquatic atmospheric correction, SWIR TSM retrieval and validation for hyperspectral ocean-color missions.
openness: open PANGAEA collection under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0
source_type: hyperspectral above-water reflectance and bio-optical water dataset publication series
evidence: DataCite metadata for DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.886287 and the PANGAEA page list the SeaSWIR publication series, child DOIs, ASD water reflectance, TRIOS measurements, TSM and turbidity; ESSD article states public availability and SWIR hyperspectral purpose.
scale: 137 ASD marine reflectance measurements, 137 TSM measurements and 97 turbidity measurements; multiple PANGAEA child datasets
feature_count: 16 PANGAEA child datasets in the series including ASD/TRIOS radiometric quantities and water reflectance
file_size: PANGAEA series; exact aggregate byte size not shown on landing page

name: Zenodo Baltic Sea Shipborne Hyperspectral Remote-Sensing Reflectance 2016 Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Finnish Environment Institute SYKE / Plymouth Marine Laboratory
theme_canonical: Baltic Sea shipborne hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance
theme_raw: quality-controlled and spectrally filtered shipborne hyperspectral Rrs from BONUS FerryScope merchant-vessel observations
coverage: Baltic Sea merchant vessel routes, Finnmaid and Transpaper observations
formats: CSV
access: Zenodo direct-download dataset record
notes: Zenodo API lists one file, rrs-bal-filtered.csv, about 15.1 MB. The record describes hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance collected by SYKE within BONUS FerryScope and quality-checked/spectrally filtered by Plymouth Marine Laboratory.
seen_in_passes: hyperspectral-2026-06; phase-v394-aquatic-review-to-Zenodo; water-chromatic-index-reference and Zenodo/DataCite 2-hop verification
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集包含 2016 年 Baltic Sea 商船平台采集的船载高光谱遥感反射率 Rrs，数据由芬兰环境研究所 SYKE 在 BONUS FerryScope 项目中采集，并由 Plymouth Marine Laboratory 做质量控制和光谱过滤。它适合水色大气校正验证、Baltic Sea 高 CDOM 水体算法评估、Sentinel-3/OLCI 与 Sentinel-2 MSI 水色产品对比，以及船载连续高光谱采样方法研究。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides shipborne hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance from the Baltic Sea in 2016. The observations were collected by SYKE within the BONUS FerryScope project from merchant vessels and quality-checked and spectrally filtered at Plymouth Marine Laboratory. It is useful for ocean-colour atmospheric-correction validation, Baltic Sea high-CDOM water algorithm assessment and comparison with Sentinel-3 OLCI or Sentinel-2 MSI products.
openness: open Zenodo dataset under CC BY-NC 4.0
source_type: shipborne hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance CSV dataset
evidence: Zenodo API and DataCite metadata for DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5572537 list title, license, rrs-bal-filtered.csv file and description of hyperspectral Rrs collected in the Baltic Sea by SYKE/FerryScope.
scale: one filtered CSV file of Baltic Sea shipborne Rrs records from 2016
feature_count: 1 CSV file, rrs-bal-filtered.csv

name: USGS Franks Tract Above-Water Hyperspectral Field Spectroscopy Dataset
provider: U.S. Geological Survey / California Water Science Center / ScienceBase
theme_canonical: Above-water hyperspectral field spectroscopy and water quality for satellite validation
theme_raw: hand-held hyperspectral radiance measurements, water/sky/plaque observations, water quality, phytoplankton and Sentinel-3 OLCI rho_s comparison data
coverage: Franks Tract, Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta, California, USA
license: U.S. Government public domain / USGS data release; see landing page
formats: CSV, XML, DOCX, XLSX
access: ScienceBase direct-download data release
notes: ScienceBase API lists 14 files including three SVC spectra CSV files (~24-25 MB each), field verification/water-quality tables, phytoplankton enumeration, OLCI rho_s data and metadata/method files. Measurements were collected at 16 sites within 7 OLCI pixels.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布包含 Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta 的 Franks Tract 2022 年船载手持高光谱现场观测，记录水体、天空和反射板辐亮度，用于计算水离水辐亮度/反射率。数据还配套叶绿素 a、溶解有机碳、浮游植物显微镜枚举，以及 Sentinel-3 OLCI rho_s 对比数据，适合水色遥感验证、HAB/蓝藻监测和内陆三角洲高光谱水质算法研究。
desc_en: This USGS ScienceBase release provides above-water handheld hyperspectral field spectroscopy from Franks Tract in the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta. It includes water, sky and reflectance-plaque radiance measurements, water-quality samples, phytoplankton enumeration and Sentinel-3 OLCI rho_s comparison data. The dataset supports inland water-colour validation, harmful algal bloom studies and hyperspectral water-quality algorithm development.
openness: public USGS ScienceBase data release
source_type: above-water hyperspectral field spectroscopy and water-quality validation dataset
evidence: USGS Science Data Catalog and DataCite DOI 10.5066/P13J77JK describe boat-based hyperspectral spectroscopy co-located with OLCI pixels; ScienceBase API lists 14 downloadable files including SVC spectra CSVs and water-quality tables.
scale: 16 sites within 7 Sentinel-3 OLCI pixels; three sampling dates; 14 ScienceBase files
feature_count: 14 files including 3 large SVC spectra CSVs, water-quality and phytoplankton tables, satellite rho_s data and metadata
file_size: about 75 MB for the three SVC spectra CSV files plus ancillary files by ScienceBase API sizes

name: NASA SeaBASS Tara Oceans Expedition Hyperspectral Reflectance Dataset

theme_raw: SeaBASS Tara Oceans expedition, surface hyper-spectral reflectance, particulate absorption and attenuation, HPLC pigments, rrs/ap/cp measurements
coverage: Global ocean transect, Tara Oceans expedition route, approximately 64.35S to 47.15N and 159.08W to 73.91E
license: NASA open data / SeaBASS access policy; see landing page
formats: SeaBASS .sb text files, TGZ documentation bundles, CMR granule metadata
access: SeaBASS experiment page and NASA Earthdata/CMR granule download links
notes: NASA CMR collection C1633360675-OB_DAAC and the SeaBASS experiment page describe Tara Oceans as a 2.5-year global ocean expedition with optical measurements of surface hyper-spectral particulate absorption and attenuation, hyper-spectral reflectance and HPLC pigments. CMR granule records expose direct oceandata getfile links for ACS ap/cp SeaBASS files and documentation bundles.
desc_zh: 该 NASA SeaBASS 数据集覆盖 Tara Oceans 2009-2012 年全球航次，包含表层高光谱颗粒吸收/衰减、高光谱反射率与 HPLC 色素等海洋光学观测。CMR granule 记录提供 SeaBASS .sb 文件和文档包下载入口，适合水色遥感算法、全球生物光学关系和 PACE/海色传感器验证研究。
desc_en: This NASA SeaBASS collection covers the Tara Oceans 2009-2012 global ocean expedition and includes surface hyperspectral particulate absorption and attenuation, hyperspectral reflectance and HPLC pigment observations. CMR granule metadata exposes direct SeaBASS .sb file downloads and documentation bundles, making it useful for ocean-color algorithm development, global bio-optical analysis and satellite validation.
openness: public NASA OB.DAAC/SeaBASS collection
source_type: shipborne ocean hyperspectral bio-optical field dataset
evidence: CMR DOI query for 10.5067/SeaBASS/TARA_OCEANS_EXPEDITION/DATA001 resolves to C1633360675-OB_DAAC; SeaBASS page lists data types pigment/flow_thru/mooring and parameters including ap, cp, es, lu and rrs; CMR granules show downloadable ACS ap/cp SeaBASS files plus documents.
scale: 2.5-year global Tara Oceans expedition; CMR collection with SeaBASS granules and direct getfile links
feature_count: CMR page-size check returned at least 20 granules, including ACS ap/cp SeaBASS files and documentation bundles
file_size: not summarized by CMR collection; individual granule files downloadable through oceandata getfile links

theme_raw: ClearLake_WQ, SVC HR-1024i above-water spectroradiometry, DESIS-coincident hyperspectral lake imagery, HPLC pigments, cyanobacteria/PCC algorithm support
coverage: Clear Lake, Northern California, USA
license: NASA open data / SeaBASS access policy; see landing page
formats: SeaBASS .sb text files, TGZ documentation and field-photo bundles, CMR granule metadata
access: SeaBASS experiment page and NASA Earthdata/CMR granule download links

notes: SeaBASS describes Clear Lake as a hypereutrophic HAB-prone lake campaign supporting phytoplankton community composition and cyanobacteria differentiation algorithms from remotely sensed hyperspectral measurements. Three sampling events were coincident with whole-lake DESIS hyperspectral images; CMR granules include SVC_Rrs SeaBASS files, HPLC files, documents and field-photo bundles.
desc_zh: 该 NASA SeaBASS 数据集记录了加州 Clear Lake 2021-2022 年的湖泊水质与高光谱水色观测，包含 SVC HR-1024i 水上光谱辐射计反射率、HPLC 浮游植物色素以及相关文档/照片包。部分采样与 DESIS 全湖高光谱影像同步，适合蓝藻/有害藻华、浮游植物群落组成和内陆湖泊高光谱算法验证研究。
desc_en: This NASA SeaBASS collection provides Clear Lake water-quality and hyperspectral ocean-color style observations from 2021-2022, including SVC HR-1024i above-water Rrs files, HPLC phytoplankton pigments and supporting documentation/photo bundles. Several sampling events were coincident with whole-lake DESIS hyperspectral imagery, supporting cyanobacteria, harmful algal bloom and phytoplankton community composition algorithm development.
openness: public NASA OB.DAAC/SeaBASS collection
source_type: inland-water above-water hyperspectral reflectance and pigment validation dataset
evidence: SeaBASS page lists DOI 10.5067/SeaBASS/ClearLake_WQ/DATA001, data types pigment/above_water and parameters including lsky, lt, lw and rrs. Page text states DESIS-coincident whole-lake hyperspectral images and SVC HR-1024i spectroradiometry; CMR C3457514542-OB_DAAC granules include SVC_Rrs and HPLC SeaBASS files with direct oceandata getfile links.
scale: Clear Lake stations from July 2021 to October 2022; CMR page-size check returned at least 20 granules
feature_count: CMR sample includes HPLC_ClearLake_2021 and multiple 2021 SVC_Rrs station files plus documents and field-photo bundles
file_size: not summarized at collection level; individual SeaBASS granule files downloadable through oceandata getfile links

name: USGS Merced and Tuolumne Rivers Hyperspectral-Derived Topo-Bathymetric DEMs
provider: U.S. Geological Survey / ScienceBase / USGS Science Data Catalog
theme_canonical: River topo-bathymetric elevation rasters derived from hyperspectral imagery and near-infrared lidar
theme_raw: CASI 1500 hyperspectral image-derived water depth, near-infrared lidar topography, 1 m topo-bathymetric DEMs, salmonid habitat reintroduction assessment
coverage: Upper Merced River and upper Tuolumne River, Sierra Nevada, California, USA
license: U.S. Government public domain / USGS data release; see ScienceBase landing page
formats: Raster DEM products and ScienceBase data-release files; exact file packaging on landing page
access: USGS Science Data Catalog and ScienceBase landing pages with downloadable child datasets
doi_or_citation: 10.5066/P9MUPT5X; ScienceBase parent item 5f86142482cebef40f14c732; DEM child item 5f861c8e82cebef40f14c750
notes: USGS Science Data Catalog describes 1 m digital elevation models derived by combining dry-area topography from near-infrared lidar with riverbed topography inferred from depth estimates calculated from hyperspectral image data. The ScienceBase DOI landing page states the child pages provide topo-bathymetric DEMs of river channels and adjacent floodplains produced by fusing lidar topography with water depth derived from hyperspectral images.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供加州 Sierra Nevada 上游 Merced River 和 Tuolumne River 的 1 米 topo-bathymetric DEM。水下河床高程由 CASI 1500 高光谱影像估算水深并与近红外 LiDAR 干区地形融合生成，适合河流遥感水深制图、栖息地连通性和鲑科鱼类重引入适宜性评估。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides 1 m topo-bathymetric DEMs for the upper Merced and Tuolumne Rivers in California. Submerged channel elevations were derived from hyperspectral image-based water-depth estimates and fused with near-infrared lidar topography of dry areas, supporting river bathymetry mapping, habitat connectivity and salmonid reintroduction assessments.
openness: public USGS ScienceBase data release
source_type: hyperspectral-derived river topo-bathymetric raster dataset
evidence: USGS Science Data Catalog title and abstract state that the DEMs are derived from hyperspectral image data and near-infrared LiDAR; ScienceBase DOI 10.5066/P9MUPT5X resolves to parent item 5f86142482cebef40f14c732, whose summary says child pages provide topo-bathymetric DEMs produced by fusing lidar topography with water depth derived from hyperspectral images.
scale: 1 m DEMs for upper Merced and Tuolumne River channels and adjacent floodplains
feature_count: topo-bathymetric raster DEM child dataset within a multi-part USGS ScienceBase data release
file_size: not summarized from landing-page HTML in this pass; downloadable files available through ScienceBase child page

name: HyperSL MultiSourceHSI Hyperspectral Foundation Model Training and Test Dataset
provider: HyperSL authors / GitHub kkweil/HyperSL / Hugging Face WeilKon/HyperSL
theme_canonical: Multi-source hyperspectral spectra for foundation-model pretraining and evaluation
theme_raw: HyperSL, spectral foundation model, MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5, Baidu training dataset, Hugging Face model repository, over 300 million spectra
coverage: Multi-source remote-sensing hyperspectral scenes and spectra compiled for HyperSL; source-scene geography varies by component dataset
license: MIT for Hugging Face repository metadata; check upstream component datasets and Baidu training data terms before redistribution
formats: HDF5 (.hdf5), PyTorch checkpoints (.pt), repository README and code/data pointers
access: Hugging Face public repository for MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 and checkpoints; Baidu Netdisk training-dataset link with extraction code from README
doi_or_citation: Kong et al. 2025, HyperSL: A Spectral Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Image Interpretation, IEEE TGRS, DOI 10.1109/TGRS.2025.3566205; GitHub kkweil/HyperSL
notes: Official HyperSL GitHub README provides Baidu Netdisk links for pretrained weights and the training dataset, plus a Hugging Face link. The Hugging Face repository is public and includes MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 (~10.5 GB) and three pretrained checkpoint files (~109 MB each). README states HyperSL was trained on over 300 million spectra from multiple sensors.
desc_zh: HyperSL 是 2025 年提出的高光谱光谱基础模型数据/权重入口。官方 README 提供训练数据和预训练权重下载链接，Hugging Face 仓库公开包含约 10.5GB 的 MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 和多个 checkpoint，适合研究多源高光谱光谱表征、跨传感器迁移和基础模型评测。
desc_en: HyperSL provides an official data and weight access point for a 2025 hyperspectral spectral foundation model. The project README links to the training dataset and pretrained weights, while the public Hugging Face repository includes a roughly 10.5 GB MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 file and multiple checkpoints, supporting research on multi-source spectral representation, cross-sensor transfer and foundation-model evaluation.
openness: public GitHub and Hugging Face repository; Baidu Netdisk training link with extraction code
source_type: foundation-model hyperspectral training/test data and model artifact repository
evidence: GitHub kkweil/HyperSL README lists Baidu Netdisk links and extraction codes for pretrained weights and the training dataset and cites DOI 10.1109/TGRS.2025.3566205. Hugging Face API for WeilKon/HyperSL lists a public repository with MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 size 10,525,722,420 bytes plus three checkpoint files around 109 MB each.
scale: over 300 million spectra stated by README; public HF test HDF5 is about 10.5 GB
feature_count: MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 plus three pretrained checkpoints and README/data pointers
file_size: MultiSourceHSI_test.hdf5 10,525,722,420 bytes; checkpoints about 109 MB each by Hugging Face API

name: USGS Upper Sacramento River Hyperspectral-Derived DEMs and Flow Velocity Dataset
provider: U.S. Geological Survey / Science Data Catalog / ScienceBase
theme_canonical: River digital elevation models and hydrodynamic-model validation data derived from hyperspectral bathymetry
theme_raw: Upper Sacramento River DEMs, CASI airborne hyperspectral bathymetry, Nano UAS hyperspectral bathymetry, WorldView-3 multispectral bathymetry, Delft3D-FM 2D/3D flow models, ADCP validation
coverage: Upper Sacramento River near Cottonwood Creek, northern California, USA
license: U.S. Government public domain / USGS data release; see landing page
formats: CSV text files with DEMs and velocity measurements; FGDC XML metadata
access: USGS Science Data Catalog and DOI landing page with Get Data and Metadata links
doi_or_citation: 10.5066/P946FW28; related remote-sensing data DOI 10.5066/F7Q52NZ1; related article DOI 10.1029/2022WR033097
time_span: derived from 2017 remote-sensing source bathymetries; data release 2022-04-04
notes: USGS metadata states that the release includes input topographic datasets, model parameters and validation field measurements for 2D/3D hydraulic models. DEMs were developed by combining spectrally based water-depth maps with lidar topography; depth maps came from airborne hyperspectral CASI, UAS-based hyperspectral Nano and WorldView-3 imagery. The metadata describes comma-delimited CSV files with DEMs and flow velocities in UTM Zone 10N / NAD83.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供上游 Sacramento River 河段的 DEM、模型参数和流速验证测量，用于构建和检验二维/三维水动力模型。DEM 由 CASI 航空高光谱、Nano UAS 高光谱以及 WorldView-3 派生水深图与 LiDAR 地形融合生成，适合河流高光谱水深制图、栖息地建模和遥感水动力模型不确定性研究。
desc_en: This USGS release provides DEMs, model parameters and flow-velocity validation measurements for 2D and 3D hydrodynamic models of an upper Sacramento River reach. The DEMs combine lidar topography with spectrally based bathymetry derived from airborne CASI hyperspectral imagery, UAS Nano hyperspectral imagery and WorldView-3 data, supporting river bathymetry mapping, habitat modeling and remote-sensing uncertainty studies.
openness: public USGS data release
source_type: hyperspectral-derived river DEM and hydrodynamic-model validation dataset
evidence: USGS FGDC XML metadata for USGS:624acef2d34e21f827635af1 lists DOI 10.5066/P946FW28 and states the DEMs were derived from airborne hyperspectral imagery (CASI), UAS-based hyperspectral imagery (Nano) and WorldView-3 source bathymetries. It also links related remote-sensing source data DOI 10.5066/F7Q52NZ1 and the associated Water Resources Research paper DOI 10.1029/2022WR033097.
scale: 1 m curvilinear-grid hydrodynamic model input topography for an upper Sacramento River reach; 2D and 3D Delft3D-FM model context
feature_count: CSV DEMs and field measurements of depth-averaged and near-bed flow velocities plus metadata
file_size: not summarized by USGS page HTML; metadata states comma-delimited CSV files for DEMs and flow measurements

name: BRAZA Brazil Coastal and Inland Waters Hyperspectral Rrs Bio-Optical Database
provider: BRAZA consortium / Scientific Data / Springer Nature Figshare
theme_canonical: Brazil coastal and inland water hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance and water-quality bio-optical measurements
theme_raw: BRAZA, Brazilian coastal and inland waters, Rrs 400-900 nm, chlorophyll-a, turbidity, water quality, bio-optical database, GLORIA-style quality flags
coverage: Brazil coastal and inland waters, multiple aquatic systems and stations
license: CC0 1.0 public domain dedication on Figshare
formats: XLSX and CSV (rrs.xlsx, stations.xlsx, flags.csv)
access: Springer Nature Figshare article with direct file downloads and Scientific Data article
doi_or_citation: 10.6084/m9.figshare.28566197.v1; Scientific Data article DOI 10.1038/s41597-025-05609-1; related code DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15690038
time_span: published 2025; field dates vary by station in stations.xlsx
notes: Scientific Data/PMC page states BRAZA is a curated bio-optical dataset for Brazilian coastal and inland waters. Data Records section says the Figshare dataset contains three files: rrs.xlsx with Rrs between 400 and 900 nm, stations.xlsx with station metadata and water-quality parameters, and flags.csv with quality-control flags. Figshare API reports CC0 license and files stations.xlsx, flags.csv and rrs.xlsx.
desc_zh: BRAZA 是巴西沿海和内陆水体的开放 bio-optical 数据库，提供 400-900 nm 遥感反射率 Rrs、水质参数、站点元数据和质量控制标记。数据以 Figshare CC0 发布，适合巴西及热带/复杂水体的水质遥感算法训练、验证和跨区域泛化研究。
desc_en: BRAZA is an open bio-optical database for Brazilian coastal and inland waters, providing hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance from 400 to 900 nm, station metadata, water-quality parameters and quality-control flags. Released on Figshare under CC0, it supports water-quality algorithm development, validation and regional generalization studies for optically complex tropical waters.
openness: CC0 Figshare open dataset
source_type: in situ hyperspectral aquatic remote-sensing reflectance and water-quality database
evidence: PMC full text for Scientific Data 2025 states BRAZA is available on Figshare and that rrs.xlsx contains Rrs between 400 and 900 nm. Figshare API for article 28566197 gives DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.28566197.v1, CC0 license and three downloadable files: stations.xlsx (585,491 bytes), flags.csv (62,629 bytes) and rrs.xlsx (16,759,172 bytes).
scale: Brazil coastal and inland water stations; exact station count in stations.xlsx
feature_count: 3 files: rrs.xlsx, stations.xlsx and flags.csv
file_size: about 17.4 MB total from Figshare API file sizes

name: Capivara Reservoir Airborne Hyperspectral Glint Dataset
provider: IEEE DataPort / Alisson Fernando Carmo / IEEE GRSS featured dataset
theme_canonical: Airborne hyperspectral inland-water imagery and mosaics for sunglint correction
theme_raw: Capivara reservoir, Paranapanema River, Tibagi confluence, Rikola FPI2014, 25 bands, 505-780 nm, nine mosaicking approaches, glint-affected inland water
coverage: Capivara Reservoir at the Tibagi and Paranapanema Rivers confluence, Brazil
license: IEEE DataPort open-access landing page; see dataset terms on landing page
formats: Processed airborne hyperspectral images and mosaics; exact archive/file formats on IEEE DataPort landing page
access: IEEE DataPort open-access DOI landing page
doi_or_citation: 10.21227/dxgz-3m95; IEEE DataPort, Processed airborne hyperspectral images and mosaics of a Paranapanema River region in Capivara reservoir, Brazil
time_span: September 2016 flight survey; IEEE GRSS featured release 2026
notes: IEEE DataPort page metadata states this is an image set from a strongly glint-affected inland-water region of Capivara Reservoir, Brazil, acquired during a September 2016 flight over the Tibagi and Paranapanema Rivers confluence with a Rikola FPI2014 hyperspectral camera. IEEE GRSS social release adds that it has 25 bands from 505-780 nm, precise georeferencing with dual-frequency GPS/bundle adjustment and nine mosaicking approaches.
desc_zh: Capivara 数据集提供巴西 Capivara 水库强太阳耀斑影响区域的航空高光谱影像和拼接结果，覆盖 Tibagi 与 Paranapanema 河流交汇附近水域。数据由 Rikola FPI2014 传感器于 2016 年飞行获取，适合内陆水体 sunglint 校正、拼接方法评估和水色/水质高光谱算法研究。
desc_en: The Capivara dataset provides airborne hyperspectral images and mosaics over a strongly sunglint-affected inland-water region of Capivara Reservoir, Brazil. Acquired with a Rikola FPI2014 sensor during a 2016 flight survey near the Tibagi-Paranapanema confluence, it is useful for sunglint correction, mosaicking-method evaluation and inland-water hyperspectral algorithm development.
openness: IEEE DataPort open-access page
source_type: airborne hyperspectral inland-water imagery and mosaics dataset
evidence: DOI 10.21227/dxgz-3m95 resolves to an IEEE DataPort open-access page whose schema.org metadata names the dataset and describes Capivara reservoir, Rikola FPI2014 and 25 spectral bands. IEEE GRSS announcement provides additional details: 25 bands at 505-780 nm, 2016 flight survey, dual-frequency GPS/bundle adjustment and nine mosaicking approaches.
scale: Capivara Reservoir glint-affected inland-water flight survey; 25 spectral bands
feature_count: processed airborne hyperspectral images and mosaics; nine mosaicking approaches noted by IEEE GRSS announcement
file_size: not exposed in fetched IEEE DataPort HTML

name: SnowEx23 Apr23 Spectrometer Snow Surface Reflectance and Albedo Dataset
provider: NASA NSIDC DAAC / SnowEx 2023 / Surunis et al.
theme_canonical: Ground spectroradiometer snow reflectance and albedo calibration data
theme_raw: SnowEx23 Apr23 Spectrometer Snow Surface Reflectance and Albedo, Version 1
coverage: three interior Alaska SnowEx 2023 study sites near Fairbanks, United States
license: NASA open data; cite NSIDC DAAC dataset
formats: CSV; metadata; Earthdata/HTTPS/S3 access services
access: NSIDC DAAC landing page, Earthdata Search, data access tool, HTTPS file system and S3 service
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/CPQGGQPAJJ1X; Surunis et al. (2026), SnowEx23 Apr23 Spectrometer Snow Surface Reflectance and Albedo, Version 1; CMR concept C3963476042-NSIDC_CPRD
notes: Boundary record: field-based spectroradiometer snow reflectance and broadband albedo rather than an image cube. Included because it is an open NASA SnowEx hyperspectral/spectroradiometer calibration and validation dataset linked to airborne snow remote-sensing campaigns.
desc_zh: 该 NSIDC DAAC 数据集发布 NASA SnowEx 2023 阿拉斯加内陆野外活动中的地面雪面反射率和宽波段雪反照率观测，数据覆盖 Fairbanks 附近三个代表闭合林冠、火烧迹地和开阔草甸的样区。页面给出 DOI 10.5067/CPQGGQPAJJ1X、CSV 格式、Earthdata Search、数据访问工具、HTTPS 文件系统和 S3 访问入口。它不是影像立方体，而是用于 SnowEx 航空/卫星雪反射率遥感校验的地面光谱仪数据，因此按高光谱/光谱校准数据收录。
desc_en: This NSIDC DAAC dataset provides field-based snow surface reflectance and broadband snow albedo measurements from the NASA SnowEx 2023 campaign in interior Alaska. The landing page gives DOI 10.5067/CPQGGQPAJJ1X, CSV data format, and access through Earthdata Search, the NSIDC data access tool, HTTPS file system and Earthdata Cloud S3. It is not an image-cube product; it is cataloged as ground spectroradiometer calibration and validation data for SnowEx airborne and satellite snow-reflectance remote-sensing studies.
openness: open NASA data; free Earthdata Login required for file access
source_type: ground spectroradiometer calibration/validation dataset
evidence: NSIDC landing page states dataset ID SNEX23_Albedo, DOI 10.5067/CPQGGQPAJJ1X, CSV format, April-May 2023 temporal coverage, ground-based spectroradiometers, and data access via Earthdata Search, Data Access Tool, HTTPS file system and S3. CMR query by DOI returned concept C3963476042-NSIDC_CPRD and short_name SNEX23_Albedo version 1.
scale: three SnowEx 2023 Alaska field sites
feature_count: field snow reflectance and albedo observations

name: GOES-R PLT Ivanpah ASD Surface Reflectance and Optical Depth Calibration Dataset
provider: NASA GHRC DAAC / GOES-R Post Launch Test field campaign
theme_canonical: Ground spectroradiometer calibration and validation data
theme_raw: GOES-R PLT Surface Radiance Ivanpah V1
coverage: Ivanpah Playa, Nevada/California border region, United States
license: NASA open data; cite GHRC DAAC DOI
formats: Excel XLSX, ASR auxiliary/calibration files, JPEG/PNG browse imagery, metadata
access: NASA Earthdata Search / GHRC DAAC protected and public links
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/GOESRPLT/DATA101; related AVIRIS-NG calibration article DOI 10.1016/j.rse.2018.07.003; CMR concept C1979115951-GHRC_DAAC
time_span: 2017-03-23 and 2017-03-28 within GOES-R PLT March-May 2017 campaign
notes: Boundary record: ground ASD/ASR surface reflectance and optical-depth calibration measurements, not airborne image cubes. Included because CMR states the dataset provides independent validation of AVIRIS-NG airborne instrument calibration and exposes downloadable campaign files.
desc_zh: 该 GHRC DAAC 数据集来自 GOES-R Post Launch Test 的 Ivanpah Playa 野外校准活动，包含 ASD 便携式光谱辐射计测得的地表反射率、ASR 总光学厚度资料以及浏览图像。CMR 元数据说明其目标是为 AVIRIS-NG 机载仪器校准提供独立验证，文件包括 Excel 表格、ASR 辅助/定标文件和 JPEG/PNG。它不是机载高光谱影像立方体，而是与 GOES-R PLT AVIRIS-NG/AVIRIS 数据配套的地面光谱校验数据。
desc_en: This GHRC DAAC dataset comes from the GOES-R Post Launch Test field campaign at Ivanpah Playa and contains ASD portable spectroradiometer surface-reflectance measurements, ASR total optical-depth data and browse imagery. CMR states that its main goal is independent validation of AVIRIS-NG airborne instrument calibration, with downloadable Excel, auxiliary/calibration and image files. It is not an airborne hyperspectral image cube; it is cataloged as ground spectral calibration data linked to the GOES-R PLT AVIRIS-NG/AVIRIS products.
openness: open NASA metadata; Earthdata login may be required for protected file access
source_type: ground spectroradiometer calibration/validation dataset
evidence: CMR query for C1979115951-GHRC_DAAC returned title GOES-R PLT Surface Radiance Ivanpah V1, DOI 10.5067/GOESRPLT/DATA101, summary describing ASD portable spectroradiometer surface reflectance, ASR optical depth, March 23 and 28 2017 Excel files, and independent validation of AVIRIS-NG airborne instrument calibration. Earlier CMR candidate matrix lists five granules including ASD AVIRIS-NG results XLSX and ASR analysis/calibration files.
scale: Ivanpah Playa calibration site; two campaign dates
feature_count: 5 CMR-listed files/granules in earlier matrix

name: ABoVE Peace-Athabasca Delta AVIRIS-NG Wetland Vegetation Classification Dataset
provider: NASA ORNL DAAC / ABoVE / Peace-Athabasca Delta wetland mapping team
theme_canonical: Hyperspectral-derived wetland vegetation classification maps
theme_raw: ABoVE: Wetland Vegetation Classification for Peace-Athabasca Delta, Canada, 2019
coverage: Peace-Athabasca Delta, Canada
license: NASA open data; cite ORNL DAAC DOI
formats: GeoTIFF classification maps, shapefile and KML footprints, bundled ZIP
access: ORNL DAAC protected bundle and Earthdata Search
notes: Boundary record: derived land-cover/vegetation maps rather than the original AVIRIS-NG spectra. Included because the open raster products are explicitly derived from AVIRIS-NG combinations and provide reusable hyperspectral-derived wetland mapping outputs.
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集提供加拿大 Peace-Athabasca Delta 的湿地和水体植被分类图，重点区分开放水体、挺水/湿地植被、陆地植被和森林等 10 个类别。CMR 元数据说明四套 5 m 分类图由 2019 年 7 月和 9 月获取的 AVIRIS-NG、UAVSAR 以及历史 LiDAR 数据的不同组合派生，并提供 GeoTIFF、shapefile 和 KML 足迹。它是高光谱派生产品而非原始影像立方体，适合湿地生态制图、多源遥感融合和 AVIRIS-NG 应用案例追踪。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset provides wetland and water/vegetation classification maps for the Peace-Athabasca Delta, Canada, distinguishing 10 classes including open water, emergent aquatic vegetation, terrestrial vegetation and forest. CMR states that four 5 m maps were derived from combinations of AVIRIS-NG, UAVSAR and historical LiDAR data acquired in July and September 2019, with GeoTIFF maps plus shapefile and KML footprints. It is cataloged as a hyperspectral-derived product rather than raw AVIRIS-NG image cubes, useful for wetland mapping, multisensor fusion and AVIRIS-NG application tracing.
openness: open NASA metadata; Earthdata login may be required for protected bundle access
source_type: hyperspectral-derived raster classification product
evidence: CMR query for C2308233855-ORNL_CLOUD returned DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2069 and summary stating four 5 m classification maps were derived from combinations of AVIRIS-NG, UAVSAR and LiDAR over Peace-Athabasca Delta; outputs include 10 land-cover classes, GeoTIFF maps, shapefile and KML footprints, with ORNL protected bundle Wetland_VegClassification_PAD_2069.zip.
scale: four 5 m classification maps over Peace-Athabasca Delta
feature_count: 10 land-cover classes

name: Adelaide Temperate-Coast Airborne Hyperspectral Seagrass Benthic Classification Maps
provider: University of Adelaide Figshare / Clarke et al. Scientific Reports
theme_canonical: airborne hyperspectral seagrass and benthic habitat classification maps
theme_raw: Benthic classification maps associated with Using hyperspectral imagery to investigate large-scale seagrass cover and genus distribution in a temperate coast
coverage: Adelaide metropolitan coast, South Australia; seagrass cover and genus-level benthic mapping products
formats: Figshare ZIP downloads containing ENVI-style .img/.aux classification products plus README text
access: open Figshare repository with DOI and direct file downloads
time_span: dataset published 2021-06-16; Figshare metadata modified 2026-02-18; imagery/field campaign described in Clarke et al. 2021
notes: Derived classification products rather than original hyperspectral image cubes. Product A maps benthic exposure/seagrass versus sand; Product B maps genus-level seagrass distribution. Included because the maps are generated from airborne hyperspectral imagery and provide reusable geospatial remote-sensing products.
desc_zh: 该 Figshare 数据包发布了澳大利亚南澳阿德莱德沿岸航空高光谱海草研究的底栖分类成果。数据由 Clarke 等人在 Scientific Reports 论文中说明，使用航空高光谱影像并结合野外调查，生成海草/沙底暴露分类和海草属级分布图。它不是原始高光谱影像立方体，而是可下载的派生分类图产品，适合海草覆盖、浅海底栖栖息地制图和高光谱生态遥感结果复用。
desc_en: This Figshare package publishes benthic classification products from an airborne hyperspectral seagrass study along the Adelaide metropolitan coast in South Australia. Clarke et al. describe the use of airborne hyperspectral imagery trained with field surveys to map seagrass cover and genus-level distributions. The package is a derived product, not the original hyperspectral image cube, but it provides reusable geospatial classification maps for seagrass cover, shallow benthic habitat mapping and coastal hyperspectral remote-sensing validation.
openness: open Figshare data under CC BY 4.0
source_type: derived airborne hyperspectral benthic classification maps
evidence: Figshare API for article 14752005 lists DOI 10.25909/14752005.v1, CC BY 4.0 license, tags including Hyperspectral Reflectance Imaging, downloadable ProductA/ProductB .img ZIP files and README. The Scientific Reports article data-availability section states that datasets generated during the study are available from Figshare DOI 10.25909/14752005.
scale: Three Figshare files totaling about 11.0 MB; two benthic/seagrass classification product ZIP files plus README
feature_count: Product A benthic exposure map; Product B genus-level seagrass map

name: CEDA HyperDrone 20210722 UAV VNIR-SWIR Hyperspectral Plastic Detection Dataset
provider: NERC Field Spectroscopy Facility / Plymouth Marine Laboratory / CEDA
theme_canonical: UAV hyperspectral imagery and in-situ radiometry for macroplastic detection
theme_raw: HyperDrone Flight 20210722 - hyperspectral in situ radiometry and hyperspectral imagery at different altitudes for plastics detection
coverage: Oban airport shore, Scotland; sandy and rocky shore with plastic targets; 2021-07-22
license: Open Government Licence v3.0; registered CEDA account required for access
formats: ENVI BIL hyperspectral imagery, BADC-CSV reflectance/radiometry tables, LiDAR-supported georectification metadata, uncertainty estimates, README
access: CEDA catalogue record; online dataset, register/login required for file access
doi_or_citation: 10.5285/e0f6a223220d463ea7a5d2a7520000cf; Plymouth Marine Laboratory and Mata (2023), HyperDrone Flight 20210722, CEDA
time_span: Data collected 2021-07-22; CEDA publication 2023-04-28; latest data update 2023-03-29
notes: Distinct from laboratory SWIR plastic-detection cube datasets: this is an outdoor UAV/drone hyperspectral imaging and in-situ radiometry collection over sandy/rocky shoreline plastic targets. Access is open under OGL but requires a registered CEDA account.
desc_zh: 该 CEDA 数据集来自 HyperDrone 2021 年 7 月 22 日飞行实验，在苏格兰 Oban 机场岸边布设多类塑料目标，采集不同高度的无人机高光谱影像和原位辐射测量。数据使用 NERC Field Spectroscopy Facility 的 Headwall 共轴 VNIR/SWIR 推扫成像仪，覆盖 450-2500 nm，并与 LiDAR 联合用于直接地理校正。数据规模约 103 个文件、256 GB，包含 ENVI BIL 影像、BADC-CSV 反射率/辐射数据和不确定性估计，适合宏塑料遥感检测、任务需求评估和海岸场景高光谱算法验证。
desc_en: This CEDA dataset comes from the HyperDrone flight experiment on 22 July 2021 at the shore near Oban airport, Scotland, where plastic targets were observed from a drone at different altitudes. It used the NERC Field Spectroscopy Facility Headwall co-aligned VNIR/SWIR pushbroom imager covering 450-2500 nm, with LiDAR support for direct georectification, and includes in-situ radiometry. The record reports 103 files and about 256 GB in ENVI BIL and BADC-CSV formats, with uncertainty information, making it useful for macroplastic detection, coastal hyperspectral algorithm testing and future mission requirement studies.
openness: Open Government Licence v3.0; CEDA registration/login needed for download
source_type: outdoor UAV hyperspectral imagery and in-situ radiometry data release
evidence: CEDA catalogue page states Permitted Use: Any, online/citable status, DOI 10.5285/e0f6a223220d463ea7a5d2a7520000cf, 103 files and 256 GB. It is cataloged as a simulation benchmark rather than observed imagery, useful for testing hyperspectral scaling and retrieval algorithms.

access: Open download via Zenodo
notes: Included as proximal/controlled-environment hyperspectral imagery supporting remote-sensing and phenotyping workflows, not as airborne or satellite remote sensing. Zenodo API reports one 4.03 GB data.zip file. Description states HySpex VNIR-1600 data from 400-988 nm with 160 bands and SWIR-384 data from 950-2500 nm with 288 bands, calibrated to reflectance.
desc_zh: 该数据集是 SiaPy 示例所用的 HySpex VNIR/SWIR 高光谱影像，来自斯洛文尼亚农业研究所温室中的马铃薯水分胁迫实验。数据使用 HySpex VNIR-1600 与 SWIR-384 推扫相机采集，记录页说明覆盖 400-988 nm 和 950-2500 nm，并已定标为反射率。它不是航空或卫星遥感影像，但可作为作物胁迫、近端成像光谱和遥感算法验证的支撑数据。
desc_en: This dataset provides HySpex VNIR/SWIR hyperspectral images used in SiaPy examples, acquired in a potato water-stress greenhouse experiment at the Agricultural Institute of Slovenia. The record describes VNIR-1600 imagery from 400-988 nm with 160 bands and SWIR-384 imagery from 950-2500 nm with 288 bands, already calibrated to reflectance. It is not airborne or satellite remote sensing, but it is useful as proximal imaging-spectroscopy support data for crop-stress and remote-sensing algorithm workflows.
openness: open CC-BY-4.0
source_type: proximal hyperspectral image dataset
evidence: Zenodo API phase v420: Dataset, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14534998, open CC-BY-4.0, file data.zip at 4.03 GB; description identifies HySpex VNIR-1600 and SWIR-384 calibrated reflectance imagery from a 2021 potato greenhouse experiment.
scale: greenhouse pot experiment
feature_count: VNIR 160 bands; SWIR 288 bands; IMG/HDR files described by label-camera naming scheme

name: CRA-MHIF Yellow River Estuary GF-5 AHSI Sentinel-2 Fusion Dataset
provider: CRA-MHIF authors / Zenodo
theme_canonical: Hyperspectral-multispectral image fusion / coastal remote sensing benchmark
theme_raw: Yellow River Estuary GF-5 AHSI and Sentinel-2A MSI dataset for CRA-MHIF
coverage: Yellow River Estuary, China
formats: ZIP archives with hyperspectral/multispectral fusion data and experiment figures
access: Open download via Zenodo
time_span: Published 2026-04-15; acquisition date not stated in API description
notes: Zenodo package supports the CRA-MHIF paper. It includes YRE-Dataset.zip with a real-world Yellow River Estuary remote-sensing pair: GF-5 AHSI hyperspectral image, 1400 x 1400 pixels, 280 bands, 400-2500 nm, 30 m; Sentinel-2A MSI image, 4200 x 4200 pixels, 4 bands, 10 m. It also includes experiment images and per-band PSNR data for CAVE, PaviaU and Washington DC Mall; those public benchmarks are not duplicated as separate rows here.
desc_zh: 该数据集是 CRA-MHIF 高光谱-多光谱融合论文的配套开放数据，其中最关键的是 Yellow River Estuary 真实遥感场景。记录说明 YRE 数据包含 GF-5 AHSI 高光谱影像（1400×1400 像元、280 波段、400-2500 nm、30 m）和 Sentinel-2A 多光谱影像（4200×4200 像元、4 波段、10 m）。它适合用于高光谱/多光谱融合、空间超分辨率和滨海区域遥感算法评测。
desc_en: This Zenodo package accompanies the CRA-MHIF hyperspectral-multispectral fusion paper, with the most relevant component being a real Yellow River Estuary remote-sensing scene. The record describes a GF-5 AHSI hyperspectral image with 1400 x 1400 pixels, 280 bands, 400-2500 nm coverage and 30 m resolution, paired with a Sentinel-2A MSI image of 4200 x 4200 pixels, 4 bands and 10 m resolution. It is useful for hyperspectral-multispectral fusion, spatial super-resolution and coastal remote-sensing benchmark work.
openness: open CC-BY-4.0
source_type: satellite hyperspectral-multispectral fusion benchmark
evidence: Zenodo API phase v420: Dataset, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19592354, open CC-BY-4.0; file list includes YRE-Dataset.zip, Experiment-imgs.zip, Experiment-Data.zip and details.txt. Description states the YRE dataset contains GF-5 AHSI HSI and Sentinel-2A MSI imagery.
scale: Yellow River Estuary scene; 1400 x 1400 GF-5 AHSI HSI paired with 4200 x 4200 Sentinel-2A MSI
feature_count: GF-5 AHSI 280 bands; Sentinel-2A 4 bands
file_size: about 366 MB for YRE-Dataset.zip; 375 MB total package

name: Bi-Hieronymi Optical Water Type Hyperspectral Rrs Training Dataset
provider: Helmholtz-Zentrum Hereon / Zenodo
theme_canonical: Aquatic optics / optical water type classification / hyperspectral Rrs simulation
theme_raw: OWT BH2024 hyperspectral and OLCI training data
coverage: Synthetic/global optical water type parameter space for ocean, coastal and inland waters
formats: NetCDF
access: Open download via Zenodo
time_span: Published 2024-07-24; synthetic training data rather than field acquisition time series
notes: Supplementary dataset for Bi and Hieronymi (2024) optical water type classification. Includes owt_BH2024_training_data_hyper.nc with 2 nm spectral resolution from 400-900 nm and owt_BH2024_training_data_olci.nc aligned to Sentinel-3 OLCI bands. The record states 10 optical water types with 10,000 samples each and variables including Rrs, absorption, backscattering, chlorophyll, suspended matter, salinity and temperature.
desc_zh: 该数据集是 Bi 和 Hieronymi 2024 年水体光学类型分类框架的配套训练数据，Zenodo 以 CC-BY-4.0 开放发布。核心文件 `owt_BH2024_training_data_hyper.nc` 提供 400-900 nm、2 nm 间隔的高光谱训练数据，另有一个按 Sentinel-3 OLCI 波段组织的版本。数据包含 Rrs、吸收、后向散射、叶绿素、悬浮物、盐度和温度等变量，适合水色算法、光学水体类型分类和高光谱水体模拟研究。
desc_en: This dataset provides the training data for the optical water type framework of Bi and Hieronymi (2024), released openly on Zenodo under CC-BY-4.0. The main hyperspectral NetCDF file covers 400-900 nm at 2 nm spectral resolution, with a second version aligned to Sentinel-3 OLCI bands. Variables include Rrs, absorption, backscattering, chlorophyll, suspended matter, salinity and temperature, making the dataset useful for aquatic remote-sensing algorithms, optical water type classification and hyperspectral water simulation studies.
openness: open CC-BY-4.0
source_type: synthetic / modelled aquatic hyperspectral Rrs and IOP training dataset
evidence: Zenodo API phase v422: Dataset, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.12803329, CC-BY-4.0, files owt_BH2024_training_data_hyper.nc and owt_BH2024_training_data_olci.nc; description states 2 nm 400-900 nm hyperspectral training data and 10 OWTs with 10,000 samples each.
scale: 10 optical water types x 10,000 samples each
feature_count: hyperspectral 400-900 nm at 2 nm resolution; OLCI-aligned companion file
file_size: 1.21 GB hyperspectral NetCDF plus 115 MB OLCI NetCDF

name: SEANOE GreenEdge Snow and Sea-Ice Hyperspectral Directional Reflectance Dataset
provider: SEANOE / GreenEdge Ice Camp authors
theme_canonical: Cryosphere / snow and sea-ice hyperspectral directional reflectance
theme_raw: High angular and spectral directional reflectance dataset of snow and sea-ice
coverage: GreenEdge Ice Camp 2015, Arctic sea-ice/snow/ponded ice stations
formats: CSV / DAT / ZIP tabular spectral-angular measurements
access: Open download via SEANOE DOI landing page
notes: DataCite and SEANOE metadata state that the dataset includes hyperspectral 400-1000 nm Hemispherical Directional Reflectance Factor (HDRF) measured with ASD over a full hemisphere at 30 degree angular resolution, hyperangular multispectral CE600 upwelling radiance, OCR downwelling irradiance, derived hyperangular HDRF, and aerosol optical depth. Included as cryosphere field/proximal reflectance data for satellite validation and snow/sea-ice BRDF/albedo studies, not as gridded imagery.
desc_zh: 该 SEANOE 数据集发布 GreenEdge Ice Camp 2015 期间雪、海冰和融池冰站点的高光谱/多角度方向反射率观测。核心数据包括 400-1000 nm 的 ASD 半球方向反射因子 HDRF、CE600 鱼眼相机多角度上行辐射、OCR 下行辐照度以及派生的多角度 HDRF。它适合雪冰 BRDF、反照率、PRISMA/EnMAP/AVIRIS 等成像光谱雪冰产品验证和极地遥感算法研究。
desc_en: This SEANOE dataset provides high-angular and hyperspectral directional reflectance observations from snow, sea-ice and ponded-ice stations at the 2015 GreenEdge Ice Camp. It includes ASD hyperspectral HDRF from 400-1000 nm over a full hemisphere, CE600 hyperangular multispectral upwelling radiance, OCR downwelling irradiance and derived hyperangular HDRF. It is useful for snow and sea-ice BRDF, albedo and satellite imaging-spectroscopy validation studies rather than gridded image analysis.
openness: open CC-BY-NC-4.0
source_type: cryosphere field/proximal hyperspectral directional reflectance dataset
evidence: DataCite API phase v425 for DOI 10.17882/55352 reports resourceTypeGeneral Dataset, SEANOE publisher, CC-BY-NC-4.0 rights, URL https://www.seanoe.org/data/00442/55352/, and a description of hyperspectral 400-1000 nm HDRF, CE600 radiance, OCR irradiance and AOD. SEANOE page JSON-LD lists downloadable DAT/ZIP files totaling roughly 12.5 MB.
scale: GreenEdge Ice Camp snow, sea-ice and ponded-ice field stations
feature_count: hyperspectral 400-1000 nm HDRF plus hyperangular multispectral radiance/irradiance products
file_size: about 12.5 MB across listed DAT/ZIP files

name: Alpine Meadow Noxious Weed Close-Range Hyperspectral Imaging Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Alpine meadow noxious weed hyperspectral imaging authors
theme_canonical: close-range hyperspectral weed identification and meadow plant spectra
theme_raw: Fine Identification of Noxious Weeds Based on Close-Range Hyperspectral Imaging and Spectral Features of Alpine Meadow Plants
coverage: alpine meadow plant samples; close-range hyperspectral imaging setting
formats: TIFF hyperspectral data subsets, shapefiles, feature descriptors, MATLAB/Python code, species metadata spreadsheets
access: public Zenodo record with file-level downloads
notes: Boundary proximal/close-range HSI record rather than airborne or satellite remote sensing. Included because the public payload contains TIFF hyperspectral image subsets and vector annotations for plant/weed identification, comparable to existing close-range agricultural HSI entries. Needs later domain review if the catalog scope is narrowed strictly to airborne/spaceborne remote sensing. Lychee v432 verified the Zenodo landing page reachable on 2026-06-13.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集面向高寒草甸有毒杂草的精细识别，提供近距高光谱成像数据子集、光谱类型、特征描述子、Mahalanobis 距离选带结果和矢量标注文件。Zenodo API 显示记录 17075237 为 Dataset，许可证 CC BY 4.0，包含 Dataset-A 到 Dataset-F 的 TIFF 文件、train/text shapefile、物种拉丁名表以及 MATLAB/Python 辅助代码。它不是航空或卫星遥感数据，但可作为农业/生态近地高光谱识别、杂草分类和光谱特征选择算法的开放边界数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports fine identification of noxious weeds in alpine meadow plants using close-range hyperspectral imaging and spectral features. The API identifies record 17075237 as a CC BY 4.0 Dataset with Dataset-A through Dataset-F TIFF files, train/text shapefiles, Latin species-name metadata and MATLAB/Python helper code. It is not airborne or satellite remote sensing, but it is a useful boundary open dataset for proximal agricultural/ecological HSI, weed classification and spectral feature-selection benchmarks.
openness: open
source_type: close-range vegetation hyperspectral imaging benchmark
evidence: Zenodo API phase v431 for record 17075237 returned DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17075237, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17075236, resource type Dataset, license cc-by-4.0, and files including Dataset-A.tif, Dataset-B.tif, Dataset-C.tif, Dataset-D.tif, Dataset-E.tif, Dataset-F.tif, train.shp, text.shp, Description.docx, HOG.m and mahaldistance.py. The description states the data are hyperspectral data subsets and spectral features derived from close-range hyperspectral imagery for fine-grained identification of alpine meadow vegetation and noxious weeds.
scale: six TIFF hyperspectral subsets plus shapefile annotations and code
feature_count: Dataset-A through Dataset-F TIFF subsets; train/text shapefiles; feature descriptors and selected-band outputs

name: CA-MDN EMIT Chlorophyll-a Hyperspectral Retrieval Model and GLORIA Training CSV Package
provider: Zenodo / Chapman University ESSDS Lab / CA-MDN authors
theme_canonical: EMIT hyperspectral aquatic chlorophyll-a retrieval model and training data package
theme_raw: Channel Attention Mixture Density Network (CA-MDN) Hyperspectral Retrievals of Chlorophyll-a Using EMIT
coverage: global GLORIA in situ aquatic reflectance/chlorophyll training data; EMIT coastal and inland water case-study workflow
formats: MDN.zip model/code package, six water-type CSV files with GLORIA training/testing data
access: public Zenodo record with file-level downloads
time_span: published 2025-04-29; article published in Ecological Informatics 94 (2026)
notes: Boundary algorithm-support record, not a new EMIT raw imagery archive. Included because it provides a citable open model/code package for EMIT hyperspectral chlorophyll-a retrieval plus GLORIA chlorophyll-a training/testing CSV files split by optical water type. GLORIA itself is already cataloged as a primary aquatic hyperspectral dataset. Lychee v433 verified the Zenodo landing page reachable on 2026-06-13.
seen_in_passes: v433 EMIT aquatic CA-MDN citation-chain follow-up
desc_zh: CA-MDN Zenodo 记录是一个面向 NASA EMIT 高光谱水体叶绿素 a 反演的开放模型与训练数据支撑包。记录说明其服务于 2026 年 Ecological Informatics 论文，提供 Channel Attention Mixture Density Network 的代码/预训练模型，并附带按 6 类光学水体类型拆分的 GLORIA 叶绿素 a 训练/测试 CSV。它不是新的 EMIT 原始影像数据集，而是可复现实验和算法迁移用的开放支撑数据，适合水质高光谱反演、模型验证和 EMIT 应用工作流复现。
desc_en: The CA-MDN Zenodo record is an open model and training-data support package for chlorophyll-a retrieval from NASA EMIT hyperspectral imagery. The record supports a 2026 Ecological Informatics article and provides Channel Attention Mixture Density Network code/pre-trained assets together with GLORIA chlorophyll-a training/testing CSV files split into six optical water types. It is not a new EMIT raw-imagery archive, but it is a reproducibility package for aquatic hyperspectral retrieval workflows, model validation and EMIT application experiments.
openness: open
source_type: EMIT aquatic hyperspectral retrieval model and training-data support package
evidence: Zenodo API for record 15305292 returned DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15305292, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15258507, resource type Model, license cc-by-4.0, and files MDN.zip plus WType_1.csv through WType_6.csv. The description states that the package supports the paper Global High-Resolution Hyperspectral Retrievals of Chlorophyll-a Using EMIT, allows users to process EMIT .nc files and estimate chlorophyll-a, and includes GLORIA chlorophyll-a training/testing data as CSV files by water type. Chapman Digital Commons identifies the related Ecological Informatics article DOI 10.1016/j.ecoinf.2026.103677.
scale: seven Zenodo files; model/code plus six water-type CSV training/testing files
feature_count: MDN.zip and WType_1.csv through WType_6.csv

name: USGS NYF Pegmatite VNIR-SWIR Reflectance and TIR Emissivity Spectral Dataset
provider: USGS ScienceBase / USGS mineral spectroscopy authors

coverage: Morefield Mine and other Amelia County, Virginia pegmatite/host-rock samples, plus related Virginia/North Carolina mineral samples and drill cores
license: USGS public domain/open data; ScienceBase data release terms apply
