access: USGS ScienceBase public data release with file-level downloads
notes: Boundary mineral spectroscopy/critical-minerals validation dataset rather than airborne/spaceborne imagery. Included because VNIR-SWIR reflectance and TIR emissivity spectra are directly useful as endmember/validation data for hyperspectral mineral mapping and imaging-spectroscopy critical mineral assessments. Lychee v437 verified the ScienceBase landing page reachable on 2026-06-13.
seen_in_passes: v437 USGS ScienceBase mineral/geology portal scan
desc_zh: 该 USGS ScienceBase 数据发布提供弗吉尼亚 Amelia County NYF pegmatite 与石榴石-黑云母片麻岩等岩石样品的 VNIR-SWIR 反射率、TIR 发射率、XRD 和 ATR-FTIR 数据。ScienceBase 摘要说明数据包含 53 个 pegmatite 和围岩样品，来源包括 Morefield Mine、Martin-Marietta Amelia Quarry、Piedmont-Carolina Mine、Virginia Energy 保存矿物样品和 Morefield Mine 钻芯。它不是航空/星载影像，但可作为关键矿产遥感、矿物端元识别和高光谱矿物制图验证的开放光谱库。
desc_en: This USGS ScienceBase data release provides VNIR-SWIR reflectance, TIR emissivity, XRD and ATR-FTIR measurements for NYF pegmatite and garnet-biotite gneiss rock samples from Amelia County, Virginia and related mineral samples. The release describes 53 pegmatite and host-rock samples from Morefield Mine, Martin-Marietta Amelia Quarry, Piedmont-Carolina Mine, Virginia Energy collections and Morefield drill cores. It is not airborne or satellite imagery, but it is a useful open spectral library for critical-mineral remote sensing, mineral endmember identification and hyperspectral mineral-mapping validation.
openness: open
source_type: USGS laboratory VNIR-SWIR/TIR mineral spectroscopy and critical-mineral validation dataset
evidence: ScienceBase item 686e5f59d4be026f4a0181d6 lists DOI 10.5066/P13ERF6P, publication date 2025-09-17, and files 1_Pegmatite_ASD_FieldSpec18336.csv, 2_Pegmatite_ASD_FieldSpec6056.csv, 3_Pegmatite_Nicolet.csv, 4_Pegmatite_XRD_Results.csv, 5_VAMM1_ATRFTIR.csv, 6_Sample_Descriptions.csv and Pegmatite_data_release_metadata.xml. The summary states these are laboratory measurements on 53 pegmatite and host-rock samples to spectrally characterize complex pegmatite minerals and support critical mineral resource assessments.
scale: 53 pegmatite/host-rock/mineral samples; seven ScienceBase files
feature_count: VNIR-SWIR reflectance, TIR emissivity, XRD, ATR-FTIR and sample description tables

doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.18476019; concept DOI/record 10.5281/zenodo.18476018; related JGR Atmospheres manuscript submitted

Included because the Zenodo metadata explicitly describes EMIT 60-m imaging spectroscopy mineralogy/soil-texture scenes and paired ground measurements, but files are not downloadable until 2027-02-03, so it should not be treated as presently open-download data.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 记录描述了南加州 Sonoran 与 Mojave 沙漠的 EMIT 60 m 地表矿物组成和土壤质地场景，并配套来自 Sonoran Desert 活跃尘源土壤样品的地面矿物学和粒径分布测量。它服务于一篇关于 EMIT 成像光谱矿物学与地面测量对比的 JGR Atmospheres 投稿研究。当前记录为 CC BY 4.0，但 Zenodo API 标注 access_right 为 embargoed，文件到 2027-02-03 才开放，因此本条按“已发现/计划开放”记录。
desc_en: This Zenodo record describes EMIT 60 m surface mineralogy and soil-texture scenes for the Sonoran and Mojave Deserts of southern California, paired with ground-based mineralogy and particle-size-distribution measurements from active dust-source soil samples in the Sonoran Desert. It supports a submitted JGR Atmospheres study comparing EMIT imaging-spectroscopy mineralogy with ground measurements. The record is licensed CC BY 4.0, but Zenodo marks the files as embargoed until 2027-02-03, so this entry is tracked as discovered/scheduled-open rather than currently open-download data.
openness: landing page public; metadata CC BY 4.0; files embargoed in Zenodo until 2027-02-03
source_type: EMIT satellite imaging spectroscopy mineralogy and soil texture validation dataset

The description states the dataset comprises EMIT 60-m surface mineralogy and soil texture scenes plus ground-based mineralogy and particle-size distribution measurements from active dust sources.

feature_count: EMIT 60-m surface mineralogy and soil texture scenes; ground mineralogy and particle-size distribution measurements; file count hidden during embargo

name: PANGAEA Atlantic Polarstern/Merian Phytoplankton Pigments and Hyperspectral Rrs Series
provider: PANGAEA / AWI-MARUM / Bracher et al. ocean-colour authors
theme_canonical: ship-based hyperspectral ocean-colour radiometry and phytoplankton pigment validation series
theme_raw: Phytoplankton pigments, hyperspectral downwelling irradiance and remote sensing reflectance during POLARSTERN cruises ANT-XXIII/1, ANT-XXIV/1, ANT-XXIV/4, ANT-XXVI/4, and Maria S. Merian cruise MSM18/3
coverage: Atlantic Ocean cruise stations from POLARSTERN ANT-XXIII/1, ANT-XXIV/1, ANT-XXIV/4, ANT-XXVI/4 and Maria S. Merian MSM18/3; south-bound -49.322, north-bound 49.728, west-bound -63.117, east-bound 10.450
formats: PANGAEA dataset publication series; ZIP download of 45 tab-delimited text datasets; HPLC pigments, hyperspectral downwelling irradiance, ship-based hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance
access: public PANGAEA landing page and ZIP download for all datasets
doi_or_citation: 10.1594/PANGAEA.847820; supplement to Ocean Science article DOI 10.5194/os-11-139-2015
notes: Ocean-colour validation and algorithm-development dataset series. Included as a publication-series level record because PANGAEA lists 45 component datasets spanning pigments, hyperspectral irradiance and remote-sensing reflectance; it complements but does not duplicate GLORIA, ARK-XXVI/3 Rrs, OC-CCI and later OLCI-matched absorption datasets already cataloged.
seen_in_passes: v439 PANGAEA aquatic hyperspectral Rrs citation-chain follow-up
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据出版系列汇集了 POLARSTERN 与 Maria S. Merian 多个大西洋航次中的浮游植物 HPLC 色素、不同深度的高光谱下行辐照度，以及船载高光谱水下辐射计反演的遥感反射率数据。PANGAEA 页面显示该系列含 45 个子数据集，可作为 ZIP 下载，并采用 CC BY 3.0 许可。该数据支撑 Bracher 等 Ocean Science 论文中用 remote-sensing reflectance 的 EOF 特征预测浮游植物色素浓度的方法，是水色高光谱算法验证和 PACE/海洋成像光谱研究的重要历史基准。
desc_en: This PANGAEA publication series brings together HPLC phytoplankton pigments, hyperspectral downwelling irradiance profiles and ship-based hyperspectral remote-sensing reflectance from several POLARSTERN and Maria S. Merian Atlantic cruises. The landing page lists 45 component datasets, offers a ZIP download and uses a CC BY 3.0 license. The data support the Bracher et al. Ocean Science study that predicted pigment concentrations from EOF features of remote-sensing reflectance, making the series a useful ocean-colour hyperspectral validation and algorithm-development benchmark.
openness: open PANGAEA dataset publication series under CC BY 3.0
source_type: ship-based hyperspectral aquatic remote-sensing reflectance and pigment validation dataset series
evidence: PANGAEA page for DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.847820 states the citation, supplement DOI 10.5194/os-11-139-2015, publication date 2015-07-07, CC BY 3.0 license, 45 datasets, ZIP download of all tab-delimited datasets, coverage from 2005-10-15 to 2011-07-18 across Atlantic cruise stations, and an abstract describing remote-sensing reflectance derived from ship-based hyperspectral underwater radiometry plus pigment measurements.
scale: 45 PANGAEA component datasets across five cruises and Atlantic stations
feature_count: phytoplankton pigment concentrations, hyperspectral downwelling spectral irradiance profiles, and remote-sensing reflectance component datasets
file_size: PANGAEA ZIP download available; aggregate size not stated on landing page

notes: New HyTES facility-level L2 archive discovered through the HyTES L1 guide reference chain. Distinct from the already cataloged SHIFT HyTES L2 March 2022 campaign product: this record covers the broader EOSDIS facility instrument archive for HyTES L2 emissivity and LST products across multiple campaigns/platforms.
seen_in_passes: v442 HyTES thermal infrared facility collection follow-up
desc_zh: 该 ORNL DAAC 数据集是 NASA HyTES 热红外高光谱仪的设施级 L2 发射率与陆表温度产品档案。页面说明数据包含 8-11.5 微米 HyTES 通道的标准发射率产品、7.4-12 微米 PCemis 模型发射率、由大气校正辐亮度反演的 LST，以及场景内大气校正产品，格式为 HDF-5。它覆盖 2014-07-03 到 2023-11-15 的 HyTES 多航次/多平台观测，补充了目录中已有的 SHIFT 单次 HyTES L2/L3 产品。
desc_en: This ORNL DAAC dataset is the facility-level Level 2 archive of emissivity and land-surface temperature products from NASA HyTES, an airborne thermal infrared hyperspectral imaging spectrometer. The landing page describes standard emissivity for HyTES channels from 8-11.5 micrometers, PCemis modeled emissivity from 7.4-12 micrometers, LST derived from atmospherically corrected radiance, and in-scene atmospheric corrections, all provided in HDF-5 format. It spans HyTES observations from 2014-07-03 to 2023-11-15 and complements the already cataloged SHIFT-specific HyTES L2/L3 products.
openness: public NASA ORNL DAAC dataset landing page; direct data access route via Earthdata Login; cite ORNL DAAC DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2460
source_type: airborne thermal infrared hyperspectral L2 emissivity and land-surface-temperature facility collection
evidence: ORNL DAAC dsviewer page ds_id=2460 returned title HyTES: Level 2 Emissivity and Land Surface Temperature, DOI 10.3334/ORNLDAAC/2460, publication date 2026-06-08, temporal coverage 2014-07-03/2023-11-15, spatial bounding box -34.9216 -160.027 68.571 26.2789, keywords including Hyperspectral Thermal Emission Spectrometer, land surface temperature and emissivity, and schema.org description stating the dataset contains L2 LST and emissivity from HyTES in HDF-5 format. Distribution metadata lists direct data access at https://daac.ornl.gov/daacdata/hytes/HyTES_L2_Emissivity_LST/ and a dataset bundle link.
scale: HyTES facility archive across 2014-2023 campaigns; global bounding box from ORNL metadata
feature_count: L2 standard emissivity, PCemis modeled emissivity, land surface temperature and in-scene atmospheric correction HDF-5 products
file_size: schema.org distribution reports 2.1 MB dataset bundle metadata; product files available through ORNL DAAC direct-access route

name: Arctic Data Center GreenTrACS Firn-Core NIR Hyperspectral Grain Size and Ice Layer Dataset
provider: NSF Arctic Data Center / University of Nevada Reno / GreenTrACS authors
theme_canonical: near-infrared hyperspectral firn-core grain-size and ice-layer validation dataset
theme_raw: Effective grain size and ice layer data from the top 10 meters of the 2016-2017 Greenland Traverse for Accumulation and Climate Studies (GreenTrACS) firn cores, western Greenland Ice Sheet
coverage: western percolation zone of the Greenland Ice Sheet along approximately the 2200 m elevation contour; 14 GreenTrACS firn cores
formats: CSV effective grain-size rasters, long-format grain-size tables, ice-layer stratigraphy tables, core locations, EML metadata
access: NSF Arctic Data Center public DOI landing page and DataONE object downloads
time_span: firn cores collected 2016-2017; dataset pubDate 2024; metadata temporal coverage 2016-2023
notes: Boundary laboratory/ice-core hyperspectral validation dataset rather than airborne or satellite imagery. Included because the associated The Cryosphere paper uses a near-infrared hyperspectral imager (900-1700 nm) to map grain size and ice layer distributions in firn cores, and the public Arctic Data Center package provides raster-like CSV outputs and stratigraphy tables useful for snow/ice imaging-spectroscopy physics and validation.
desc_zh: 该 Arctic Data Center 数据集发布了 2016-2017 年 GreenTrACS 西格陵兰冰盖 14 根 firn cores 顶部 10 米的有效粒径与冰层层序数据。相关 The Cryosphere 论文说明研究使用 900-1700 nm 近红外高光谱成像系统扫描冰芯，系统制图 firn 粒径和冰层分布。数据包采用 CC0，EML 元数据列出 43 个 CSV 数据表，包括各冰芯的粒径长表、原始栅格式粒径表、冰层位置/厚度/宽度表和核心位置表，可作为雪冰高光谱物理模型和遥感反演验证的边界支撑数据。
desc_en: This Arctic Data Center package provides effective grain-size and ice-layer stratigraphy data for the top 10 m of 14 GreenTrACS firn cores from the western Greenland Ice Sheet. The related The Cryosphere paper states that the cores were scanned with a 900-1700 nm near-infrared hyperspectral imaging system to map firn grain size and ice-layer distributions. The CC0 EML package lists 43 CSV data tables, including long-format grain-size outputs, raster-style grain-size tables, ice-layer position/thickness/width tables and core locations, making it a useful boundary validation dataset for snow and ice imaging-spectroscopy physics.
openness: public Arctic Data Center/DataONE object with public read access under CC0 1.0
source_type: laboratory near-infrared hyperspectral firn-core validation and cryosphere stratigraphy dataset
evidence: Arctic Data Center EML for DOI 10.18739/A2J38KK2T returned title Effective grain size and ice layer data from GreenTrACS firn cores, pubDate 2024, CC0 public-domain dedication, public read access in DataONE system metadata, abstract stating a NIR-HSI system at 900-1700 nm scanned 14 firn cores spanning about 1000 km, geographic coverage along the western Greenland Ice Sheet 2200 m contour, and 43 dataTable entities. Parsed EML file sizes total about 1015.6 MB across CSV grain-size, raster, ice-layer and core-location files.
scale: 14 GreenTrACS firn cores; top 10 m; 43 CSV data tables; western Greenland Ice Sheet transect
feature_count: grain-size long-format CSVs, raster-style grain-size CSVs, ice-layer stratigraphy CSVs and core-location table
file_size: about 1015.6 MB across 43 CSV data tables plus 1.1 MB EML metadata

name: UQ Kelp and Macroalgae In-Situ Hyperspectral Reflectance Dataset
provider: The University of Queensland eSpace / UQ Research Data Manager
theme_canonical: kelp and macroalgae in-situ hyperspectral reflectance library
theme_raw: Hyperspectral reflectance measurements of kelps, macroalgae, and co-occurring materials collected in Australia and New Zealand
coverage: Australia and New Zealand sampling sites; macroalgae, kelp genera and co-occurring materials measured in situ under natural lighting
license: UQ eSpace Permitted Re-use with Acknowledgement / UQ General Usage Terms
formats: RDM dataset link; spreadsheets and accompanying specimen photographs
access: open UQ Research Data Manager access endpoint; DOI and eSpace landing page documented in citation/evidence
doi_or_citation: 10.48610/c5f745e; UQ eSpace landing page https://espace.library.uq.edu.au/view/UQ:c5f745e; related article: Evaluating macroalgal hyperspectral reflectance separability in support of kelp mapping (2025)
time_span: published 2025; measurements dates stored per spectrum in dataset metadata
notes: Added from a kelp/macroalgae coastal-ecology gap search. The UQ record states the project supports Mapping Australian kelp with Earth Observation and the related work is the 2025 macroalgal spectral-separability article. Included as a field spectral library and validation/endmember dataset rather than an image cube.
desc_zh: 该 UQ eSpace 数据集发布澳大利亚和新西兰现场采集的 kelp 与大型藻类高光谱反射率。记录覆盖 15 类大型藻，其中包括 Macrocystis、Ecklonia 和 Undaria 三个 kelp 属，并附带 8 类共现材料光谱，可用于混合像元和端元分析。数据由 ASD HandHeld 2 光谱仪在自然光条件下采集，样本按行、波长按列组织，并包含地点、观测几何、照明、照片编号和日期等元数据。
desc_en: This UQ eSpace dataset provides in-situ hyperspectral reflectance measurements of kelps and macroalgae collected in Australia and New Zealand. It covers 15 macroalgal target types, including the kelp genera Macrocystis, Ecklonia and Undaria, plus spectra for eight co-occurring materials that may contribute to mixed pixels. Measurements were collected with an ASD HandHeld 2 spectrometer under natural lighting and are organized as sample rows with wavelength columns and per-spectrum metadata. It is useful for kelp mapping, macroalgal spectral separability and coastal endmember validation.
openness: Open Access in UQ eSpace; permitted re-use with acknowledgement; DataCite DOI active
source_type: field hyperspectral reflectance spectral library
evidence: UQ eSpace/OpenCLI record and DataCite DOI metadata report DOI 10.48610/c5f745e, publication year 2025, dataset type, Open Access/RDM open status, RDM access URL, and a description stating 15 macroalgal types including three kelp genera and eight co-occurring materials measured with an ASD HandHeld 2 spectrometer. The eSpace landing page blocks lychee/HEAD with 403, while the UQ RDM data access endpoint returned HTTP 200 on 2026-06-13 and is therefore used as the primary verifiable URL.
scale: 15 macroalgae target types, 3 kelp genera, 8 co-occurring materials; in-situ relative reflectance spectra with metadata and photos
feature_count: 23 target material classes; samples as rows and wavelengths as columns
file_size: RDM access endpoint verified; file package size not exposed by DataCite metadata

name: PANGAEA Moreton Bay Algal Seagrass and Substrate Spectral Reflectance Library
provider: PANGAEA / University of Queensland
theme_canonical: Moreton Bay algal seagrass and substrate hyperspectral reflectance library
theme_raw: Spectral reflectance library of algal, seagrass and substrate types in Moreton Bay, Australia
coverage: Moreton Bay, Australia; main seagrass, algal and substrate types sampled during 2000-2001 field trips
formats: PANGAEA metadata and Excel workbook download (MoretonBay_Spectra.xlsx)
access: open PANGAEA dataset with direct XLSX file link
doi_or_citation: 10.1594/PANGAEA.864310; related RSE article DOI 10.1016/j.rse.2008.12.003; related L&O Methods DOI 10.4319/lom.2011.9.396
time_span: field trips in 2000 and 2001; dataset published 2016-09-06
notes: Found by second-hop coastal seagrass/macroalgae search from current seagrass gaps and PANGAEA records. The record links to shallow-water bathymetry, hydro-optics and benthos mapping literature; included as an open spectral-library dataset supporting hyperspectral shallow-water remote sensing.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集提供澳大利亚 Moreton Bay 主要海草、藻类和底质类型的辐亮度-反射率光谱库。野外测量采于 2000-2001 年，使用 ASD VNIR 光谱仪记录 400-1050 nm 可见光到近红外范围，共 1024 个波段，约 2 nm 间隔。数据以 Excel 工作簿开放下载，可作为浅水底质、海草/藻类端元库、浴深与水柱校正以及海岸高光谱算法验证材料。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset provides a radiance-reflectance spectral library for the main seagrass, algal and substrate types in Moreton Bay, Australia. Field measurements were collected during 2000-2001 using an ASD VNIR spectrometer over 400-1050 nm with 1024 bands at roughly 2 nm intervals. The openly downloadable Excel workbook supports shallow-water benthic endmember libraries, bathymetry and water-column correction work, and coastal hyperspectral remote-sensing validation.
openness: PANGAEA open-access dataset under CC-BY-3.0
source_type: coastal benthic field hyperspectral reflectance spectral library
evidence: PANGAEA HTML/JSON-LD metadata identify this as a Dataset with DOI 10.1594/PANGAEA.864310, license https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/, Excel format, and description stating ASD VNIR 400-1050 nm measurements in 1024 bands from Moreton Bay seagrass, algal and substrate samples. HEAD check of the file handle resolved to MoretonBay_Spectra.xlsx with HTTP 200 and Content-Length 456136 bytes on 2026-06-13.
scale: 400-1050 nm ASD VNIR radiance-reflectance spectra; 1024 bands; Moreton Bay field samples
feature_count: ten random samples of each substratum class reported in metadata description

name: Zenodo Yunxiao Mangrove Flux Tower Eddy Covariance and Hyperspectral Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Zhu Xudong
theme_canonical: mangrove flux tower hyperspectral reflectance and eddy covariance dataset
theme_raw: A dataset including 2-year eddy covariance and hyperspectral data at Yunxiao mangrove flux tower
coverage: Yunxiao mangrove flux tower, southeast China; monthly and daily mangrove canopy observations from 2021-2022
formats: Zenodo downloadable XLSX workbook and TIFF figure rasters
access: open Zenodo record with DOI and direct file downloads
notes: Added from a mangrove/blue-carbon hyperspectral gap search. This is not a full airborne or satellite image cube; it is a flux-tower ecosystem dataset with hyperspectral canopy reflectance matrices, vegetation-index products and eddy-covariance/GPP/LUE variables for mangrove productivity studies.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布云霄红树林通量塔两年涡度协方差和高光谱观测数据。数据工作簿包含 2021-2022 年气象变量、PAR、VPD、降雨、GPP 与 LUE，并包含 400 nm 起的高光谱反射率矩阵、红边位置/红边比值、NDRE 与 Sentinel-2 NDRE 对照等表格。它不是完整影像立方体，而是红树林冠层光谱与生态通量耦合验证数据，适合蓝碳、生产力遥感和高光谱植被指数研究。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides two years of eddy-covariance and hyperspectral observations from the Yunxiao mangrove flux tower. The workbook includes 2021-2022 meteorological variables, PAR, VPD, rainfall, GPP and LUE, together with hyperspectral reflectance matrices starting at 400 nm, red-edge position and ratio products, NDRE and Sentinel-2 NDRE comparisons. It is not a full image cube, but it is valuable tower-scale validation data for mangrove canopy spectroscopy, blue-carbon productivity studies and hyperspectral vegetation-index research.
openness: Open Zenodo dataset under CC-BY-4.0
source_type: flux-tower field hyperspectral reflectance and ecosystem carbon-flux dataset
evidence: Zenodo API record 13829615 identifies an open CC-BY-4.0 dataset titled A dataset including 2-year eddy covariance and hyperspectral data at Yunxiao mangrove flux tower, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.13829615, with one XLSX data workbook and five TIFF files. Local inspection of the workbook found sheets for 2021-2022 PAR/air temperature/VPD/rainfall/GPP/LUE, hyperspectral reflectance matrices from about 400 nm, red-edge metrics, NDRE and Sentinel-2 NDRE comparison.
scale: two-year tower observations; workbook sheets include 731 daily rows and hyperspectral reflectance matrices with 885 columns in Figure 4/5 sheets
feature_count: 6 files; one XLSX workbook plus five TIFF rasters; hyperspectral, eddy-covariance and vegetation-index variables

name: EcoSIS Salt Marsh Vegetation Nutrients Canopy Photosynthesis Hyperspectral Dataset
provider: EcoSIS / VargasLab / University of Delaware
theme_canonical: salt marsh canopy hyperspectral reflectance nutrients and photosynthesis dataset
theme_raw: Hyperspectral data for measuring vegetation nutrients and canopy photosynthesis in a salt marsh
coverage: St. Jones Reserve, Delaware, USA; ecosystem-scale and Spartina alterniflora / S. cynosuroides salt-marsh observations from 2016-2018
license: Creative Commons Non-Commercial; JSON-LD license URI https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/2.0/
formats: EcoSIS package API and CSV export with metadata
access: open EcoSIS package with DOI and CSV export endpoint
time_span: 2016-2018 field observations; EcoSIS package created 2021-06-28, version 1.1 modified 2023-07-17
notes: Added from a salt-marsh/blue-carbon gap search. This is a proximal canopy spectroscopy and ecosystem physiology dataset rather than an airborne image cube. DataCite shows the EcoSIS dataset DOI is cited by the 2022 JGR Biogeosciences article on canopy-level nutrients and photosynthesis in a salt marsh.
desc_zh: 该 EcoSIS 数据集发布美国特拉华州 St. Jones Reserve 盐沼的冠层高光谱、植被养分和冠层光合作用数据。观测覆盖 2016-2018 年，包含生态系统尺度以及 Spartina alterniflora 和 S. cynosuroides 两个优势盐沼物种。CSV 导出经本地检查包含 7147 条光谱、475 列，其中 449 个波长列覆盖 401-849 nm，并带有 NEE、日尺度光合碳吸收、养分和盐沼类别等元数据。它适合蓝碳、盐沼生产力、高光谱植被指数和 PLSR 反演研究。
desc_en: This EcoSIS dataset provides canopy hyperspectral reflectance, vegetation nutrient and canopy photosynthesis data from the St. Jones Reserve salt marsh in Delaware, USA. Observations span 2016-2018 and cover ecosystem-scale measurements plus the dominant salt-marsh species Spartina alterniflora and S. cynosuroides. Local inspection of the CSV export found 7147 spectra, 475 columns and 449 wavelength columns from 401-849 nm, with NEE, daily photosynthetic carbon uptake, nutrient and salt-marsh category metadata. It is useful for blue-carbon, salt-marsh productivity, hyperspectral vegetation-index and PLSR retrieval studies.
openness: EcoSIS open package; Creative Commons Non-Commercial / BY-NC 2.0 URI in JSON-LD; DOI resolves through DataCite
source_type: proximal canopy hyperspectral reflectance and ecosystem physiology spectral dataset
evidence: EcoSIS package API reports package ID ef2bc7ee-095c-4a76-bb5d-b16150667e14, version 1.1, 7147 spectra, Creative Commons Non-Commercial license, reflectance measurement quantity, coastal ecosystem type, Ocean Optics JAZ spectrometer and citation DOI 10.21232/nTe4g8MW. JSON-LD identifies a Dataset with CSV DataDownload. Local CSV export inspection on 2026-06-13 found 7147 rows, 475 columns and 449 wavelength columns from 401 to 849 nm, plus NEE_sum, FA_daily_sum, nutrient and SaltMarsh_Category fields. DataCite DOI metadata links the dataset to citation DOI 10.1029/2022JG007088.
scale: 7147 spectra; 449 wavelength columns from 401-849 nm; 2016-2018 salt-marsh canopy/nutrient/photosynthesis observations
feature_count: 7147 spectra; 475 CSV columns including reflectance wavelengths and ecosystem physiology/nutrient metadata

name: EcoSIS AQGRAD Wetland Aquatic Plant Leaf Reflectance and Traits Dataset
provider: EcoSIS / CNR-IREA
theme_canonical: wetland aquatic plant leaf reflectance and traits spectral library
theme_raw: Leaf reflectance and traits of plants sampled along a water affinity gradient (AQGRAD)
coverage: Mantua lakes system, Northern Italy; temperate wetland plant communities along an aquatic-to-terrestrial water-affinity gradient, 2020-2024
license: Creative Commons Non-Commercial (Any), BY-NC 2.0 URL in CKAN metadata
formats: EcoSIS/CKAN CSV resources: leaf_spectra.csv, leaf_traits.csv and data_description.txt
access: open EcoSIS CKAN package and direct CSV downloads
notes: Added from EcoSIS wetland API search. The package supports aquatic/riparian plant spectroscopy and leaf trait modelling; it is a leaf-level spectral library, not a remote-sensing image cube.
desc_zh: AQGRAD 是 EcoSIS 上的湿地/水生植物叶片反射率与性状数据集，采样自意大利北部 Mantua 湖泊系统及相关湿地。样本沿水分亲和梯度覆盖从水生到陆生的植物群落，并包含不同物候阶段。叶片反射率被重采样到 400-2500 nm 的 1 nm 间隔，本地检查 leaf_spectra.csv 含 190 条记录和 2101 个波长列，并配套叶片性状和数据说明文件。
desc_en: AQGRAD is an EcoSIS wetland and aquatic-plant leaf reflectance and traits dataset from the Mantua lakes system in northern Italy. Samples cover plant communities along a water-affinity gradient from aquatic to terrestrial species and include different phenological stages. Reflectance spectra were resampled to 1 nm intervals over 400-2500 nm; local inspection of leaf_spectra.csv found 190 records and 2101 wavelength columns, with companion leaf-trait and data-description files.
openness: EcoSIS/CKAN public package under Creative Commons Non-Commercial; direct CSV resources verified
source_type: leaf-level wetland aquatic plant hyperspectral spectral library with traits
evidence: EcoSIS CKAN package_search/package_show reports DOI 10.21232/xYgn5GKv, CNR-IREA provider, 400-2500 nm reflectance, Spectral Evolution SR-3500, aquatic/freshwater/lake ecosystem type, and linked RSE article DOI 10.1016/j.rse.2023.113926. HEAD check on leaf_spectra.csv returned HTTP 200 and Content-Length 3564147 bytes. Local CSV inspection found 190 data rows, 2107 columns and 2101 numeric wavelength columns from 400 to 2500 nm.
scale: 190 leaf spectra; 400-2500 nm at 1 nm spacing; leaf traits for wetland/aquatic plants
feature_count: 3 resources; leaf_spectra.csv, leaf_traits.csv and data_description.txt
file_size: leaf_spectra.csv 3564147 bytes; leaf_traits.csv 18392 bytes; data_description.txt 3180 bytes

provider: EcoSIS / CNR-IREA and collaborators
theme_canonical: floating and emergent macrophyte leaf reflectance and traits spectral library
theme_raw: Leaf reflectance and tratis of floating and emergent macrophytes
coverage: Lake Hidvegi/Kis-Balaton, Hungary; Mantua lakes and Lake Varese, Italy; 2016-2018 temperate shallow lakes and surrounding wetlands
license: Creative Commons Non-Commercial (Any), BY-NC 2.0 URL in CKAN metadata
formats: EcoSIS/CKAN CSV resources: leaf_spectra.csv, leaf_traits.csv and data_description.txt
access: open EcoSIS CKAN package and direct CSV downloads
notes: EcoSIS title contains the typo tratis; catalog name keeps the source meaning but corrects the display wording to traits. This is a leaf spectral/traits dataset for floating and emergent macrophytes, not an image cube.
desc_zh: 该 EcoSIS 数据集包含 6 种温带常见漂浮和挺水大型水生植物的叶片反射率与性状数据。数据来自 2016-2018 年匈牙利 Kis-Balaton/Lake Hidvegi、意大利 Mantua 湖泊系统和 Lake Varese，覆盖不同物候阶段、季节和环境条件。CKAN 元数据说明反射率范围为 400-2500 nm，本地 leaf_spectra.csv 检查显示 325 条记录、1024 个波段列，并配套叶片性状和说明文件。
desc_en: This EcoSIS dataset provides leaf reflectance and traits for six floating and emergent macrophyte species common in temperate wetlands. Data were collected during 2016-2018 from Lake Hidvegi/Kis-Balaton in Hungary and the Mantua lakes system and Lake Varese in Italy, spanning phenological stages, seasons and environmental conditions. CKAN metadata describes 400-2500 nm reflectance, and local inspection of leaf_spectra.csv found 325 records and 1024 spectral-band columns, with companion leaf-trait and description files.
openness: EcoSIS/CKAN public package under Creative Commons Non-Commercial; direct CSV resources verified
source_type: leaf-level macrophyte hyperspectral spectral library with traits
evidence: EcoSIS CKAN metadata reports DOI 10.21232/uq4HsfSY, 400-2500 nm reflectance, Spectral Evolution SR-3500, freshwater/aquatic/lake ecosystem type and linked Plant Methods article DOI 10.1186/s13007-021-00816-4. Direct leaf_spectra.csv download was lychee-verified. Local CSV inspection found 325 data rows, 1029 columns and 1024 wavelength/band columns.
scale: 325 leaf spectra; VSWIR leaf reflectance for six floating/emergent macrophyte species
feature_count: 3 resources; leaf_spectra.csv, leaf_traits.csv and data_description.txt
file_size: leaf_spectra.csv about 3973142 bytes; companion trait/description CSV/TXT resources

theme_raw: The hyperspectral dataset for typical aquatic vegetation

desc_zh: 该 EcoSIS 数据集提供典型水生植被的原位高光谱反射率，覆盖 124 个水生植被物种，并包含原始光谱、一阶导数、连续统去除光谱、标准差、元数据和 README。CKAN 元数据说明光谱范围为 350-2500 nm，使用 Spectra Vista HR-1024i，目标包括冠层、叶片和花。本地检查 Original data.csv 显示 134 条记录、995 个波长列，波长约 346.7-2499.5 nm。
desc_en: This EcoSIS dataset provides in-situ hyperspectral reflectance for typical aquatic vegetation, covering 124 aquatic vegetation species and including original spectra, first-order derivatives, continuum-removed spectra, standard deviations, metadata and a README. CKAN metadata states a 350-2500 nm spectral range measured with a Spectra Vista HR-1024i, with canopy, leaf and flower targets. Local inspection of Original data.csv found 134 records and 995 wavelength columns spanning about 346.7-2499.5 nm.
openness: EcoSIS/CKAN public package under Open Data Commons Attribution License; direct CSV/TXT resources verified
source_type: in-situ aquatic vegetation hyperspectral spectral library
evidence: EcoSIS CKAN package_show reports title The hyperspectral dataset for typical aquatic vegetation, ODC-BY license, 350-2500 nm reflectance, 124 aquatic vegetation species, HR-1024i instrument, canopy/leaf/flower targets and six downloadable resources. Direct Original data.csv download was lychee-verified. Local inspection found 134 data rows, 1005 columns and 995 numeric wavelength columns from about 346.7 to 2499.5 nm. The associated article DOI is 10.1080/10095020.2024.2440653.

feature_count: 6 resources including Original data.csv, derivative, continuum-removal, standard-deviation, metadata and README files

provider: EcoSIS / Susan Ustin

theme_raw: Tidal Vegetation: Tom's Point, California
coverage: Tom's Point, Tomales Bay, California, USA; tidal vegetation spectra collected 2006-09-18
formats: EcoSIS/CKAN XLSX workbook
access: open EcoSIS CKAN package and direct XLSX download

desc_zh: 该 ESS-DIVE/NGEE Arctic 数据包收录 2018 年 7 月 BNL Osprey 多传感器无人机在 Alaska Seward Peninsula 的 Council、Teller、Kougarok 三个样区采集的遥感数据。数据包括沿航线采样的 VNIR 表面反射率 CSV、RGB/TIR 正射影像、DSM/DEM/CHM、点云，以及物种和植物功能型制图产品。本地抽检三个 Spectra.csv：Council 77 行、Teller 59 行、Kougarok 165 行，每个文件含 Wave_400 到 Wave_900 共 501 个 1 nm 波段列。

Local inspection of the three Spectra.csv files found 77 Council rows, 59 Teller rows and 165 Kougarok rows, each with 501 one-nanometer wavelength columns from Wave_400 through Wave_900.

desc_zh: 该 Hakai ACO 2025 记录扩展到 British Columbia、Washington 和 Alberta 的冰川/冰原航测区。公开引用元数据说明 ACO 由 Hakai Institute、University of Northern British Columbia 和 Kisik Aerial Surveying 合作，使用 Piper Navajo 平台集成机载 LiDAR、高分辨率影像和高光谱影像，服务冰川范围、质量损失和长期变化监测。数据文件需通过访问申请表获取，公开元数据提供 DOI、CC-BY-4.0 许可证和数据用途说明。
desc_en: This Hakai ACO 2025 record extends the cryosphere aerial survey series to glacier and icefield regions in British Columbia, Washington and Alberta. Public citation metadata states that the ACO is led by Hakai Institute with the University of Northern British Columbia and Kisik Aerial Surveying, using a Piper Navajo platform that combines airborne LiDAR, high-resolution imagery and hyperspectral imagery for glacier extent, mass-loss and long-term change monitoring. File access is by request form, with public DOI metadata, CC-BY-4.0 licensing and use context.
openness: Public catalogue/DOI metadata under CC BY 4.0; data request required for files
source_type: Airborne cryosphere hyperspectral survey catalogue record
evidence: Hakai CKAN package_show for ca-cioos_2e5ddbaa lists DOI 10.21966/n6ga-nc24, issued 2026-05-28, CC-BY-4.0 license and a data-access request form. The English citation abstract states that the ACO maps icefields to oceans using airborne LiDAR, high-resolution imagery and hyperspectral imagery, combining these data with field observations to detect and measure glacier mass loss and ice extent over time.
scale: regional glacier and icefield aerial survey record spanning British Columbia, Washington and Alberta
feature_count: multi-sensor ACO survey record; number of hyperspectral scenes not specified

name: East River Colorado 2023 ASD FieldSpec3 Foliar and Soil Hyperspectral Reflectance Survey
provider: Zenodo / Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory / JPL-linked East River research team
theme_canonical: Field VSWIR foliar and soil hyperspectral reflectance for metal-hotspot and watershed remote-sensing validation
theme_raw: Analytical Spectral Device Fieldspec3 foliar and soil reflectance survey, East River, CO 2023
coverage: Upper East River Basin / Gunnison County, Colorado, United States
formats: Zenodo ZIP containing raw ASD .asd spectra and corrected CSV reflectance tables with wavelength columns
access: open Zenodo landing page and direct ZIP download
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.17704197; Grant, Hechinger, Vasquez, Chadwick and West, Analytical Spectral Device Fieldspec3 foliar and soil reflectance survey, East River, CO 2023
notes: Found through DataCite imaging-spectroscopy query and RMBL Knowledge Commons author/dataset graph. Added as a ground/proximal spectroscopy companion to the existing NEON AOP/CHESS East River imaging-spectroscopy family, not as a duplicate of CHESS 2025 DOI 10.15485/2997555.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供 Colorado Gunnison County / Upper East River Basin 2023 年金属热点调查期间采集的 ASD FieldSpec3 叶片和土壤 VSWIR 高光谱反射率。Zenodo API 显示数据以 CC-BY-4.0 发布，包含 `ER23_ASD_Spectra.zip`；本地抽检 ZIP 发现 671 个原始 `.asd` 光谱文件和 26 个校正 CSV，CSV 含 SampleID、覆盖类型、经纬度、地点描述以及从 350 nm 起的波长列。该数据可用于高光谱矿物/植被胁迫、NEON AOP 影像验证和流域金属热点遥感研究。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides ASD FieldSpec3 foliar and soil VSWIR hyperspectral reflectance collected during a 2023 metal-hotspot survey in Gunnison County and the Upper East River Basin, Colorado. The Zenodo API reports CC-BY-4.0 licensing and an `ER23_ASD_Spectra.zip` archive; local ZIP inspection found 671 raw `.asd` spectra and 26 corrected CSV files with SampleID, cover type, coordinates, location description and wavelength columns starting at 350 nm. The data support hyperspectral mineral/vegetation stress studies, NEON AOP validation and watershed metal-hotspot remote sensing.
openness: open Zenodo download under CC BY 4.0
source_type: Field/proximal VSWIR hyperspectral reflectance survey
evidence: Zenodo API for record 17704197 lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.17704197, publication date 2025-11-24, CC-BY-4.0 license, keywords ASD, VSWIR, Hyperspectral reflectance, Metal hotspots and Upper East River Basin, and file ER23_ASD_Spectra.zip of 28,572,777 bytes. Local downloaded ZIP ER23_ASD_Spectra_v456.zip contains 699 files, including 671 raw .asd spectra and 26 corrected_spectra CSV files; a sample corrected CSV has SampleID, Cover_Type, Latitude, Longitude, Location_Description and wavelength columns beginning 350, 351, 352 nm.
scale: 2023 field survey in Gunnison County / Upper East River Basin metal-hotspot sites; sample CSVs include coordinates and cover types such as conifer and soil
feature_count: 671 raw ASD spectra plus 26 corrected CSV files in inspected ZIP
file_size: 28,572,777 byte Zenodo ZIP; local evidence copy research/ER23_ASD_Spectra_v456.zip

name: 4TU High-Sulphidation Epithermal System Multi-Resolution VNIR-SWIR-LWIR Remote Sensing Dataset
provider: 4TU.ResearchData / University of Twente ITC / Portela et al.
theme_canonical: Multi-resolution geological remote sensing dataset spanning laboratory, airborne and spaceborne VNIR-SWIR-LWIR hyperspectral data
theme_raw: Evaluating multi-resolution remote sensing data - A knowledge-driven approach to geological remote sensing in a high-sulphidation epithermal system
coverage: single high-sulphidation epithermal lithocap environment; study area not specified in checked metadata excerpt
license: Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0)
formats: 4TU package metadata lists PNG, TIF, PDF, XLSX, CSV, raster DAT, SHP, TeX, ASCII and TXT; University of Twente page links README PDF and external ZIP
access: 4TU/University of Twente landing page with dataset DOI and external ZIP link; local external ZIP download not verified due DNS failure
doi_or_citation: 10.4121/6e67d70e-cc55-4533-9ef9-d05dfcbde535; Portela, van der Werff, Hecker and van der Meijde, 4TU.ResearchData, 2025
notes: Added with an access caveat. DataCite and University of Twente pages verify the dataset and describe laboratory, airborne and spaceborne VNIR/SWIR/LWIR remote-sensing inputs. A linked external ZIP at areda-utsp.utwente.nl did not resolve from this environment and should be retried later.
desc_zh: 该 4TU/University of Twente 数据集服务于高硫化浅成热液系统的多分辨率地质遥感研究，覆盖实验室、机载和星载 VNIR、SWIR、LWIR 数据链。元数据说明实验室 SWIR/LWIR 高光谱测量用于定义和验证矿物光谱、纹理关系和波长位移，机载 SWIR/LWIR 高光谱数据和星载高光谱/多光谱数据用于跨尺度矿物学、蚀变和流体成矿解释。4TU/DataCite 元数据列出 `.dat`、`.tif`、`.csv`、`.xlsx`、`.shp` 等格式；外部 ZIP 链接本轮在本机 DNS 未解析成功，需后续复核。
desc_en: This 4TU/University of Twente dataset supports multi-resolution geological remote sensing of a high-sulphidation epithermal system, spanning laboratory, airborne and spaceborne VNIR, SWIR and LWIR data. The metadata states that laboratory SWIR/LWIR hyperspectral measurements define and validate mineral spectra, textural relationships and wavelength shifts, while airborne SWIR/LWIR hyperspectral data and spaceborne hyperspectral/multispectral data support cross-scale mineralogical, alteration and fluid-emplacement interpretation. 4TU/DataCite metadata lists formats including `.dat`, `.tif`, `.csv`, `.xlsx` and `.shp`; the external ZIP link did not resolve from this local environment and needs later retry.
openness: open metadata; dataset under CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0; linked external ZIP access needs retry
source_type: Multi-resolution geological hyperspectral remote-sensing research dataset
evidence: University of Twente dataset page lists DOI 10.4121/6e67d70e-cc55-4533-9ef9-d05dfcbde535, 4TU.Centre for Research Data, availability date 2025-11-26, README PDF and external ZIP link. DataCite metadata reports 4TU.ResearchData, CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0, formats including image/.tif, spreadsheet/.csv/.xlsx, raster/.dat and vector/.shp, and describes laboratory hyperspectral measurements in SWIR/LWIR plus airborne hyperspectral SWIR/LWIR and spaceborne hyperspectral/multispectral data for geological remote sensing. Local HEAD to the external areda-utsp ZIP failed DNS resolution and is documented as needing retry.
scale: single lithocap / high-sulphidation epithermal system studied across laboratory, airborne, spaceborne and regional/deposit scales
feature_count: multi-file PhD research package; exact scene/sample count not exposed in checked metadata
file_size: not exposed in DataCite metadata; README 79.8 KB reported by University of Twente page; external ZIP size not verified

name: Ag Data Commons Tomato Bacterial Leaf Spot Hyperspectral Imaging Dataset
provider: USDA Ag Data Commons / Virginia Tech / Song Li
theme_canonical: High-resolution tomato leaf hyperspectral imagery for early bacterial leaf spot disease detection
theme_raw: Data from: Hyperspectral Imaging Analysis for Early Detection of Tomato Bacterial Leaf Spot Disease
coverage: tomato leaf disease experiment; Virginia Tech / controlled plant-pathology imaging context, United States
formats: Ag Data Commons/Figshare ZIP archive tomato_leaf.zip containing hyperspectral tomato leaf data; associated article reports leaf-level and pixel-level spectra/vegetation-index analyses
access: open Ag Data Commons landing page and direct Figshare ZIP download
doi_or_citation: 10.15482/USDA.ADC/26046328.v2; Zhang, Vinatzer and Li (2024), Scientific Reports 14, 27666, DOI:10.1038/s41598-024-78650-6
notes: Boundary record: non-geospatial/proximal plant-pathology HSI rather than airborne or satellite remote sensing, but included because the catalogue already keeps a proximal agricultural HSI branch and this is a large, open, validated plant disease hyperspectral image dataset with a formal USDA DOI and associated peer-reviewed article.
desc_zh: 该 Ag Data Commons 数据集发布番茄细菌叶斑病早期检测实验的高光谱影像数据。页面说明数据针对 Xanthomonas perforans 番茄叶斑病，连续 7 天将番茄叶片高光谱影像与植株内病原菌数量验证数据关联，用于叶片级全谱、植被指数和像元级全谱机器学习分析。Figshare API 显示版本 2 DOI 为 10.15482/USDA.ADC/26046328.v2，许可为 CC-BY-4.0，文件 `tomato_leaf.zip` 约 16.53 GB。
desc_en: This Ag Data Commons dataset publishes hyperspectral imagery for early detection of tomato bacterial leaf spot disease. The landing page states that tomato leaves infected with Xanthomonas perforans were imaged and validated against in planta pathogen populations for seven consecutive days, supporting leaf-level full-spectrum, vegetation-index and pixel-level full-spectrum machine-learning analyses. The Figshare API reports version-2 DOI 10.15482/USDA.ADC/26046328.v2, CC-BY-4.0 licensing and a `tomato_leaf.zip` archive of about 16.53 GB.
openness: open Ag Data Commons/Figshare download under CC BY 4.0
source_type: Proximal plant-pathology hyperspectral imaging dataset
evidence: Ag Data Commons page lists DOI 10.15482/USDA.ADC/26046328, public access, CC BY 4.0, version 2 published 2024-11-18, temporal extent 2021-06-16 to 2023-06-15, and states hyperspectral images of tomato leaves were validated against in planta pathogen populations for seven consecutive days. Related Scientific Reports article DOI 10.1038/s41598-024-78650-6 is listed. Figshare API for article 26046328 reports DOI 10.15482/USDA.ADC/26046328.v2 and file tomato_leaf.zip of 16,531,945,854 bytes.
scale: tomato leaf bacterial spot disease experiment across seven days of pathogen progression; temporal extent 2021-06-16 to 2023-06-15
feature_count: one large tomato_leaf.zip archive; leaf-level and pixel-level hyperspectral image/spectral analysis context
file_size: 15.4 GB page download; Figshare API file size 16,531,945,854 bytes

name: NASA GES DISC CHIRP Aqua AIRS Climate Hyperspectral InfraRed Calibrated Radiances V2
provider: NASA Goddard Earth Sciences Data and Information Services Center
theme_canonical: Satellite hyperspectral infrared sounder calibrated radiances
theme_raw: CHIRP Aqua AIRS calibrated hyperspectral infrared radiances
coverage: Global observations from EOS-Aqua AIRS, sun-synchronous 13:30 orbit; CMR bounding box global
license: NASA Earthdata open data; NASA GES DISC terms
formats: NASA GES DISC CHIRP product files; cloud-hosted protected S3 and Earthdata download workflows
access: NASA GES DISC dataset landing page, Earthdata Search granules, data endpoint and OPeNDAP endpoint
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/G1DTUEAV5I18; CMR concept C2011289898-GES_DISC; citation AIRS Science Team/Larrabee Strow, 2020
time_span: AIRS-derived CHIRP subset starts 2016-09-01 and continues until AIRS end of mission; Version 2 created/updated 2020-12-20
notes: Gap-fill companion to existing CHIRP S-NPP and JPSS-1/NOAA-20 records. This is an atmospheric hyperspectral infrared sounder radiance product, not a surface reflectance imaging-spectrometer cube; kept separate because NASA assigns a distinct short name and DOI to the Aqua AIRS subset.
desc_zh: NASA GES DISC 的 CHIRP Aqua AIRS V2 产品提供由 EOS-Aqua AIRS 派生的气候高光谱红外校准辐射数据。CMR 元数据说明 CHIRP 将 AIRS 与 CrIS 系列产品放到一致的光谱响应框架中，并去除跨仪器辐射偏差；该 Aqua 子集从 2016-09-01 开始，面向温湿廓线、云、大气成分和气候遥感应用。它属于大气高光谱遥感产品，不是地表反射率影像立方体，因此在目录中作为 CHIRP 系列缺口补齐项收录。
desc_en: NASA GES DISC CHIRP Aqua AIRS V2 provides climate hyperspectral infrared calibrated radiances derived from the AIRS instrument on EOS-Aqua. CMR states that CHIRP harmonizes AIRS and CrIS spectral response functions and removes inter-instrument radiometric offsets; this Aqua subset starts on 2016-09-01 and supports atmospheric retrieval, cloud and climate applications. It is cataloged as an atmospheric hyperspectral sounder product rather than a surface-reflectance imaging cube.
openness: open_with_registration_or_repository_terms
source_type: satellite hyperspectral infrared sounder radiance product
evidence: CMR collection C2011289898-GES_DISC / SNDR13CHRP1AQCal_2 reports processing level 1, cloud_hosted true, global bounding box, time_start 2016-09-01, and DOI 10.5067/G1DTUEAV5I18. UMM JSON states direct distribution via S3 prefix s3://gesdisc-cumulus-prod-protected/CHIRP/SNDR13CHRP1AQCal.2/ plus Earthdata Search, data endpoint and OPeNDAP links.
scale: Global satellite sounder product; CHIRP V2 Aqua AIRS subset
file_size: NASA GES DISC cloud-hosted product; per-granule sizes not captured in this pass

name: NASA GHRC CoSMIR-H WHyMSIE Airborne Hyperspectral Microwave Brightness Temperature Dataset
provider: NASA Global Hydrometeorology Resource Center DAAC / NASA Marshall Space Flight Center
theme_canonical: Airborne hyperspectral microwave brightness temperature remote sensing
theme_raw: CoSMIR-H WHyMSIE calibrated geolocated brightness temperatures
coverage: Western United States and eastern Pacific flight domain; CMR bounding box 21.888 to 41.643 N, -130.627 to -88.842 E/W coordinates
license: NASA Earthdata open data; NASA GHRC DAAC data use guidance
formats: netCDF-4; calibrated and geolocated brightness temperature measurements; protected cloud S3 distribution
access: NASA Earthdata Search / GHRC DAAC landing and documentation pages; Earthdata login may be required for file download
doi_or_citation: 10.5067/WHYMSIE/COSMIRH/DATA101; CMR concept C4137308802-GHRC_DAAC; Kroodsma, Rachael. 2026. CoSMIR-H WHyMSIE
time_span: 2024-10-18 to 2024-11-13; collection published/cited by GHRC DAAC in 2026 metadata
notes: Boundary record: hyperspectral microwave remote sensing rather than optical VNIR/SWIR/LWIR imaging spectroscopy. Included because CMR/GHRC explicitly names the airborne sensor and WHyMSIE campaign as hyperspectral microwave, with calibrated geolocated brightness temperatures across 50-191 GHz.
desc_zh: NASA GHRC 的 CoSMIR-H WHyMSIE 数据集提供 NASA ER-2 高空飞机在 Westcoast & Heartland Hyperspectral Microwave Sensor Intensive Experiment 期间采集的校准、地理定位亮温观测。CMR 元数据说明 CoSMIR-H 覆盖 50-191 GHz 多频段，可用于温度廓线、水汽廓线、云性质和降水率反演，数据时间为 2024-10-18 至 2024-11-13，格式为 netCDF-4。该条目属于高光谱微波遥感边界类别，不与常见 VNIR/SWIR 反射率影像混同。
desc_en: NASA GHRC CoSMIR-H WHyMSIE contains calibrated and geolocated brightness-temperature observations from the NASA ER-2 aircraft during the Westcoast & Heartland Hyperspectral Microwave Sensor Intensive Experiment. CMR describes multi-frequency observations from 50 to 191 GHz for temperature profiles, water-vapor profiles, cloud properties and precipitation rate, distributed as netCDF-4 for 2024-10-18 through 2024-11-13. It is included as a hyperspectral microwave remote-sensing boundary case, distinct from VNIR/SWIR reflectance cubes.
openness: open_with_registration_or_repository_terms
source_type: airborne hyperspectral microwave brightness-temperature product
evidence: CMR collection C4137308802-GHRC_DAAC / cosmirwhymsie_1 states Level 1B, cloud_hosted true, NASA ER-2 platform, CoSMIR instrument, netCDF-4 archive/distribution, DOI 10.5067/WHYMSIE/COSMIRH/DATA101, and direct distribution prefix ghrcw-protected/cosmirwhymsie__1/. Summary says calibrated and geolocated TB measurements between 50 and 191 GHz from WH2yMSIE.
scale: Airborne campaign product over western US/eastern Pacific PBL regimes
file_size: GHRC DAAC netCDF-4 granules; aggregate size not captured in this pass

name: Zenodo Loess Plateau Depth-Stratified Soil Organic Carbon Hyperspectral Dataset
provider: Zenodo / Soil organic carbon hyperspectral modelling authors
theme_canonical: Soil organic carbon hyperspectral reflectance and depth-stratified modelling data
theme_raw: Loess Plateau soil samples hyperspectral SOC prediction dataset
coverage: Changzhi, Shanxi Province, Loess Plateau, China; 97 cropland sampling sites and four soil depth layers
formats: ZIP package plus manuscript/report files; hyperspectral modelling data and supporting figures/documents
access: Zenodo open dataset download
time_span: Zenodo publication 2026-05-18; field soil samples from Loess Plateau cropland study described in metadata
notes: Added as a proximal/field soil hyperspectral dataset for precision agriculture and carbon monitoring. It is not an airborne/satellite image cube, but the dataset directly supports hyperspectral remote-sensing SOC retrieval and contains depth-stratified soil-sample spectra and lab SOC context.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集服务于黄土高原农田土壤有机碳（SOC）高光谱预测研究。Zenodo API 元数据说明研究在山西长治黄土高原农田 97 个采样点采集 388 个土样，覆盖 0-20、20-40、40-60 和 60-80 cm 四个深度层，用于比较按深度分层建模与整合建模的 SOC 预测效果。它属于近地面/样品尺度高光谱土壤数据，可为精细农业、区域碳监测和卫星/无人机高光谱 SOC 反演提供训练与验证支撑。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports hyperspectral prediction of soil organic carbon in Loess Plateau cropland. Zenodo metadata describes 388 soil samples from 97 sites in Changzhi, Shanxi Province, collected across four depth layers from 0-20 to 60-80 cm to compare depth-stratified and integrated SOC modelling. It is a proximal/sample-scale hyperspectral soil dataset useful for precision agriculture, carbon monitoring and validation of UAV or satellite SOC retrieval workflows.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: proximal soil hyperspectral reflectance and SOC modelling dataset
evidence: Zenodo API for record 20271500 reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20271500, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20271499, CC-BY-4.0 license, publication_date 2026-05-18, and files including zenodo_package.zip plus manuscript/report materials. Description states 388 soil samples from four depth layers at 97 Loess Plateau cropland sites.
scale: 388 soil samples; 97 sites; four depth layers
file_size: zenodo_package.zip 34,493 bytes plus supporting files by Zenodo API

name: Zenodo NIR/SWIR Spectral Library of Plastic-Substrate Mixtures
provider: Zenodo / Zachary Holt
theme_canonical: NIR/SWIR plastic-substrate spectral reference library
theme_raw: Plastic and substrate mixture spectra for hyperspectral plastic detection
coverage: Laboratory spectral mixtures of plastics with soils, crushed cement, oak leaf powder and water substrates
formats: CSV reflectance and absorbance spectra, first/second derivative CSVs, ENVI .hdr and .sli spectral library files, README
access: Zenodo open dataset download
notes: Reference spectral-library record for plastic remote sensing. Added because it provides NIR/SWIR mixture spectra across realistic substrates and concentrations, complementing existing marine-debris and macroplastic hyperspectral records without duplicating the PANGAEA Spuikom collection.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供塑料与基底混合物的 NIR/SWIR 光谱库，可用于高光谱塑料污染、端元分解和混合像元识别研究。页面和 API 说明塑料类型包括 PE、PET、PLA、PP、PVC 和 SBR，基底包括 3 种土壤、碎水泥、橡树叶粉和水，浓度从纯基底到纯塑料多个梯度；数据以 CSV、ENVI `.hdr`/`.sli` 和 README 发布。它补充了已有海洋废弃物/宏塑料光谱库，但不是单景遥感影像。
desc_en: This Zenodo record provides a NIR/SWIR spectral library of plastic-substrate mixtures for hyperspectral plastic detection, unmixing and mixed-pixel studies. The dataset includes PE, PET, PLA, PP, PVC and SBR mixed with soils, crushed cement, oak leaf powder and water across concentrations from pure substrate to pure plastic, distributed as CSV spectra plus ENVI .hdr/.sli library files. It complements existing marine-debris and macroplastic records without duplicating the PANGAEA Spuikom collection.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: reference spectral library for hyperspectral plastic detection
evidence: Zenodo page/API for record 14233290 report DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14233290, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14233289, CC-BY-4.0 license, published 2024-11-28 and modified 2025-05-01. Files include reflectance/absorbance CSVs, first/second derivatives, README and ENVI hdr/sli, 7 files totaling about 31.3 MB on the current version page.
scale: Plastic-substrate mixtures across six plastic types, six substrates and multiple concentration levels
file_size: 7 files; current page reports 31.3 MB for this version; total all-version data volume 21.2 GB

name: German Image Spectral Library of Urban Surface Materials
provider: Zenodo / German Aerospace Center urban imaging spectroscopy authors
theme_canonical: Urban surface-material image spectral library from airborne HyMap imaging spectroscopy
theme_raw: German urban image spectra extracted from HyMap scenes over Dresden, Potsdam and Munich
coverage: Dresden, Potsdam and Munich, Germany; HyMap acquisitions on 1999-05-18, 2000-08-01, 2003-07-20, 2007-06-17 and 2007-06-25
formats: ENVI spectral library .sli/.hdr plus lookup table CSV
access: Zenodo open dataset download
time_span: HyMap source images acquired 1999-2007; Zenodo publication 2024-06-20
notes: Added as an image-derived urban spectral reference library. It complements existing urban reference compilations because the spectra are explicitly extracted from high-resolution HyMap imaging spectroscopy scenes over German cities rather than only reformatted from prior libraries.
desc_zh: German image spectral library of urban surface materials 是从德国城市高分辨率 HyMap 成像光谱数据中提取的城市地物光谱库。Zenodo 元数据说明该库包含 5102 条带标签图像光谱，覆盖 455-2449 nm，来源于 Dresden、Potsdam 和 Munich 多期 HyMap 航空影像，材料类型包括屋顶、道路、植被、土壤、水体和阴影等城市表面类别。数据以 ENVI `.sli`/`.hdr` 与查找表 CSV 发布，可用于城市高光谱分类、端元库构建和跨城市材料识别。
desc_en: The German image spectral library of urban surface materials contains 5,102 labelled image spectra extracted from high-resolution HyMap imaging spectroscopy scenes over Dresden, Potsdam and Munich. Zenodo describes a 455-2449 nm spectral range and typical urban materials such as roofs, roads, vegetation, soil, water and shadow classes. It is distributed as ENVI .sli/.hdr files plus a lookup table and is useful for urban hyperspectral classification, endmember libraries and cross-city material recognition.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: airborne imaging-spectroscopy-derived urban spectral library
evidence: Zenodo API for record 12192460 reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.12192460, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.12192459, CC-BY-4.0 license, publication_date 2024-06-20, and files lut.csv, DLR.sli and DLR.hdr. Description states 5102 labelled image spectra from HyMap data over Dresden, Potsdam and Munich, spectral range 455-2449 nm.
scale: 5,102 labelled image spectra; HyMap urban scenes over three German cities
file_size: DLR.sli 2,306,104 bytes; DLR.hdr 286,971 bytes; lut.csv 1,196 bytes by Zenodo API

name: Global Airborne Observatory Example Rapid Water Quality Maps of Hawaiian Island Coastline
provider: Zenodo / Global Airborne Observatory water-quality mapping authors

theme_raw: GAO VSWIR superpixel bio-optical inversion example maps for Hawaii coastline
coverage: Five Hawaiian Island coastline example sites from Global Airborne Observatory VSWIR Spectrometer data
formats: ZIP archive of water-quality maps and auxiliary information produced from GAO VSWIR spectrometer data
access: Zenodo open dataset download

time_span: Zenodo publication 2024-11-19; source GAO example sites described in related 2024 Remote Sensing paper
notes: Added as derived airborne imaging-spectroscopy map products rather than raw radiance/reflectance cubes. It is valuable because it links Global Airborne Observatory VSWIR data to reproducible water-quality parameter maps for coastal Hawaii.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供 Global Airborne Observatory（GAO）VSWIR 成像光谱数据反演得到的夏威夷海岸水质示例图。记录说明数据伴随 2024 年 Remote Sensing 论文“Rapid Water Quality Mapping from Imaging Spectroscopy with a Superpixel Approach to Bio-Optical Inversion”，包括 5 个示例站点的水质图和辅助信息。它不是原始高光谱立方体，而是由机载 VSWIR 成像光谱经过超像素与生物光学反演得到的开放地图产品，可用于水质遥感算法复现和示例验证。
desc_en: This Zenodo record provides example water-quality maps produced from Global Airborne Observatory VSWIR imaging spectroscopy data along Hawaiian Island coastlines. It accompanies the 2024 Remote Sensing paper on rapid water-quality mapping with a superpixel bio-optical inversion approach and includes maps plus auxiliary information for five example sites. The record is cataloged as derived airborne imaging-spectroscopy map products rather than raw hyperspectral cubes.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: derived airborne VSWIR imaging-spectroscopy water-quality map products
evidence: Zenodo API for record 14187955 reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14187955, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14187954, CC-BY-4.0 license, publication_date 2024-11-19, and one file Zenodo.zip of 1,747,831,703 bytes. Description states the maps were produced from GAO VSWIR Spectrometer data for five Hawaiian coastline example sites and are tied to Remote Sensing DOI 10.3390/rs16234344.
scale: Five coastal Hawaii example sites; derived GAO VSWIR water-quality map package

name: IDTReeS 2020 Competition NEON RGB Hyperspectral LiDAR Tree Crown Dataset
provider: IDTReeS / NEON / Zenodo
theme_canonical: NEON airborne hyperspectral RGB LiDAR individual tree crown benchmark
theme_raw: IDTReeS 2020 competition data for delineation and species classification
coverage: NEON forest sites OSBS, MLBS and TALL in the United States; competition train/test split for cross-site tree crown tasks
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0); underlying NEON/Battelle data terms referenced
formats: ZIP package with geospatial/tabular training data and packaged NEON remote-sensing/field products
access: Zenodo open dataset download plus IDTReeS competition documentation
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.3700197; concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3700196; related IDTReeS competition paper DOI 10.7717/peerj.12655
time_span: Zenodo publication 2020-03-07; NEON products downloaded/packaged for IDTReeS 2020 competition
notes: Added as a benchmark dataset distinct from NeonTreeEvaluation. IDTReeS 2020 focuses on individual tree crown delineation and species classification across OSBS, MLBS and TALL using NEON RGB, LiDAR and hyperspectral remote-sensing data plus field data.
desc_zh: IDTReeS 2020 Competition Data 是面向单木树冠分割和树种分类的开放遥感 benchmark。IDTReeS 页面说明该竞赛使用 NEON 三个森林站点的 0.1-1 m RGB、LiDAR 和高光谱遥感数据，训练/测试任务关注跨站点树冠边界和物种识别；Zenodo 记录提供 CC-BY-4.0 的训练数据包。该条目与已有 NeonTreeEvaluation 不同，后者是更广域的树冠检测基准，而 IDTReeS 是竞赛结构化数据包。
desc_en: IDTReeS 2020 Competition Data is an open benchmark for individual tree crown delineation and species classification. The IDTReeS documentation describes 0.1-1 m RGB, LiDAR and hyperspectral NEON airborne data across the OSBS, MLBS and TALL forest sites, with cross-site training and test tasks. Zenodo provides the CC-BY-4.0 training package, making this a distinct competition-style benchmark from the broader NeonTreeEvaluation record already in the atlas.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: airborne RGB hyperspectral LiDAR tree crown benchmark
evidence: Zenodo API for record 3700197 reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3700197, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3700196, CC-BY-4.0 license, publication_date 2020-03-07, and IDTREES_competition_train.zip of 44,200,496 bytes. IDTReeS pages state the competition uses NEON RGB, lidar and hyperspectral data from three forest sites for delineation and species-classification tasks.
scale: Competition train package; OSBS/MLBS/TALL forest sites; RGB, LiDAR, hyperspectral and field data

name: GREENPEG Spectral Library of European Pegmatites Minerals and Host Rocks
provider: Zenodo / GREENPEG project / Universidade do Porto
theme_canonical: Geological VNIR-SWIR reflectance spectral library for pegmatite minerals and host rocks
theme_raw: European pegmatites, pegmatite minerals and pegmatite host-rock reflectance spectra
coverage: Pegmatite locations in Austria, Ireland, Norway, Portugal and Spain; laboratory spectral measurements at Universidade do Porto
formats: Microsoft Access database, geodatabase, geopackage, resampled spectra ZIP, PDF metadata/report, sample photographs and extracted absorption features
access: Zenodo open dataset download
doi_or_citation: 10.5281/zenodo.7938407; concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.6518318; related GREENPEG database article
notes: Reference spectral-library record for geological/mineral remote sensing. It is not an airborne/satellite scene, but the dataset explicitly supports hyperspectral mineral identification and GIS interoperability for pegmatite exploration.
desc_zh: GREENPEG 谱库发布欧洲伟晶岩、伟晶岩矿物及围岩的反射光谱，可用于地质高光谱解译、矿物识别和锂/稀有金属伟晶岩勘查。Zenodo 元数据说明样品来自奥地利、爱尔兰、挪威、葡萄牙和西班牙的 LCT/NYF 型伟晶岩与围岩，包含原始与连续统去除光谱、样品照片、自动提取的主吸收特征，以及 Access、Geodatabase、Geopackage 和重采样光谱文件。它作为地质遥感参考谱库收录，不是单景遥感影像。
desc_en: The GREENPEG database provides reflectance spectra of European pegmatites, pegmatite minerals and host rocks for hyperspectral mineral identification and geological remote-sensing interpretation. Zenodo metadata describes LCT- and NYF-type pegmatites from Austria, Ireland, Norway, Portugal and Spain, with raw and continuum-removed spectra, sample photographs, automatically extracted absorption features and GIS-interoperable database packages. It is cataloged as a geological reference spectral library rather than a single airborne or satellite scene.
openness: open_repository_license
source_type: geological reference spectral library for hyperspectral mineral mapping
evidence: Zenodo API for concept record 6518318 resolves current version 7938407 and reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7938407, concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.6518318, CC-BY-4.0 license, publication_date 2022-05-06, and seven downloadable files including 0-Database_files.zip (1,412,367,055 bytes), Microsoft Access database, Geodatabase, Geopackage and 4-Resampled_spectra.zip. Description states reflectance spectra for European pegmatites/minerals/host rocks, raw and continuum-removed spectra, sample photographs and absorption features.
scale: European pegmatite sample spectral database across five countries
file_size: 7 files; largest 0-Database_files.zip 1.41 GB, Geopackage 900.9 MB, Access database 841.3 MB by Zenodo API
